title: Add arrow-cpp to Spack environment and rebuild container
description: Apache Arrow is not in the current Spack view at local/. Add arrow-cpp to spack.yaml and rebuild the winch/ddm container image on the native host (Spack is owned by a different user inside the container). Verify Arrow cmake config is available inside the container at local/lib/cmake/Arrow*. This is a prerequisite for building wire-cell-arrow.
notes:
FIX APPROACH (Spack 1.1.0, all CLI / Containerfile-friendly). Two root causes found: (1) gcc-runtime mismatch — make-view.sh used 'spack view symlink <bare specs>' which matches the GLOBAL store and grabbed a gcc-runtime@12 arrow, colliding with the view's gcc-runtime@15; (2) putting the COMPILER (gcc@15) in the env/view forces a gcc12-bootstrap subtree (gcc@15 link-deps like zstd/zlib are built by the bootstrap gcc@12), which duplicates the %gcc@15 app-stack copies and collides in the view ('two specs project to the same prefix: zstd ...'). unify:true and packages:all:require:%gcc@15 cannot fix (2): a compiler cannot build its own deps.

CORRECT RECIPE:
BUILD TIME (image, /devel not mounted) -- compiler NOT a root spec:
  spack env create wcph
  spack -e wcph config add concretizer:unify:true
  spack -e wcph config add "packages:all:require:'%gcc@15'"
  spack -e wcph add cmake wire-cell-toolkit phlex 'arrow +parquet +compute +dataset +filesystem'   # NO gcc@15 here
  spack -e wcph concretize -f
  spack -e wcph install --fail-fast
RUNTIME (container, /devel/ddm mounted) -- replaces make-view.sh:
  spack -e wcph env view enable /devel/ddm/local
  spack -e wcph env view regenerate
COMPILER (kept beside, not inside, the app view): gcc@15 stays installed in the store; expose via .envrc:
  export CC="$(spack -e wcph location -i gcc@15)/bin/gcc"
  export CXX="$(spack -e wcph location -i gcc@15)/bin/g++"
wire-cell-arrow CMakePresets.json reads these via $env{CC}/$env{CXX} (no view g++, no hard-coded store hash).

NOTES: package is 'arrow' not 'arrow-cpp'. Residual '%c=gcc@12.2.0' on gcc@15 and its build deps is expected/benign. Diagnose dup-view errors with 'spack -e wcph find <pkg>' (compiler shows in the group header). Rebuild with compiler removed in progress 2026-06-11.
close_reason: Arrow now in the view and verified end-to-end. The view is provided by symlinking local/ -> the env's image-built default view (/spack/var/spack/environments/wcph/.spack-env/view); make-view.sh updated. 'spack env view enable' fails at runtime (rmtree on spack-owned view -> EACCES) and 'spack view symlink' fails ('Unknown namespace: wirecell') — see memory spack-runtime-view-permission. Confirmed: local/lib/cmake/{Arrow,Parquet}, local/include/arrow present, libstdc++ -> gcc-runtime-15.2.0. wire-cell-arrow scaffold configures (finds Arrow+WireCell+Boost::json), builds, links, and ctest passes.

title: wire-cell-phlex: refactor Executor + subclass ctors to parse via boost-config
description:
Wire the config structs into the classes.

- Executor: add Executor(ExecutorConfig) doing the add_config/add_plugin/tla_var/scope work from the struct; keep Executor(boost::json::object const&) as a delegating ctor = Executor(value_to<ExecutorConfig>(obj)).
- Each subclass: NodeConfig ctor (parses its *Config, passes .executor to base via delegating ctor) + boost::json::object ctor delegating to it. FrameFilter reads cfg.use_wire_schema instead of config.contains("use_wire_schema").
- Modules: build the nested object uniformly: { "executor": to_executor_config(config) [, extra keys] }. to_executor_config becomes the ExecutorConfig sub-object builder; remove use_wire_schema from it (FrameFilter module sets it at top level).
- Update test/test_executor.cpp to the nested shape for all executors.
- Build+test via umbrella gcc15 (this pulls WCT/phlex); confirm all wire-cell-phlex tests pass.
close_reason: Closed

title: wire-cell-phlex: ExecutorConfig + per-subclass *Config structs (uniform executor field)
description:
Define config aggregates in boost-config style (Documented fields), replacing the ad-hoc config.contains/at parsing.

- ExecutorConfig: wct_config (required-ish), wct_app, wct_plugins (vector<string>), wct_tla (map or object), module_label, wct_log_sink, wct_log_level. BOOST_CONFIG_JSON(ExecutorConfig).
- One *Config per Executor subclass, each with a mandatory ExecutorConfig executor; field:
    FrameFilterConfig { ExecutorConfig executor; Documented<bool> use_wire_schema{false,...}; }
    DepoSetToFrameConfig { ExecutorConfig executor; };  (and the other 6 pass-throughs)
  Provide WCPHLEX_EXECUTOR_NODE_CONFIG(Name) helper for the field-less ones (struct + BOOST_CONFIG_JSON in one line).
- Decide wct_tla representation: std::map<std::string,std::string> vs a nested struct; must round-trip via boost-config (map<string,string> -> json object; confirm boost::json value_to<map> works, else model as object).
- Header must stay GCC12-compilable (no phlex/configuration include); boost-config is header-only Boost, fine.
close_reason: Closed

title: boost-config: schema-discovery export macro + factories_json<T>() (discovery.hpp)

title: [epic] boost-config adoption in wire-cell-phlex + schema discovery
description:
Adopt boost-config for the Executor node-config chain in devel/wire-cell-phlex and add a plugin schema-discovery path.

Design (settled with user):
- Boost.PFR forbids inheritance (proven: static_assert 'Inherited types are not supported'), so config uses COMPOSITION, not inheritance. Each Executor subclass has its OWN *Config aggregate that carries a mandatory 'executor' field of type ExecutorConfig (uniformity: no JSON-shape asymmetry between field-less and field-adding subclasses; a field-less node can later gain a field as a local, incremental change).
- Executor base ctor takes ExecutorConfig; each subclass ctor takes its *Config (parsed once from a nested {"executor":{...}, ...} object) and passes .executor to the base. A json-delegating ctor keeps the Phlex-facing boost::json::object entry. Single parse, strict reject_unknown at every level, complete recursive jsonnet_factory for free.
- Keep PFR for field reflection (no member re-listing); Boost.Describe rejected (would duplicate member names; its only win, native Boost.JSON + portability, is moot here).
- Discovery: new lib macro BOOST_CONFIG_EXPORT(suffix,S) emits a non-inline extern "C" default-visibility fn 'boost_config_factories__<suffix>()' returning jsonnet_factories<S>() as a serialized JSON object; placed in ONE plugin .cpp. Documented symbol prefix lets a CLI scan the dynamic symbol table and dlsym+call each schema.

See children.
close_reason: Closed

title: boost-config: jsonnet_factory<T>() Jsonnet emitter + recursive multi-file (jsonnet.hpp)
close_reason: Closed

title: boost-config: boost::json (de)serialization of Documented config structs (json.hpp)
design:
Make boost::json::value_from / value_to work on config structs that use
Documented<T>, flat/nested and homogeneous/heterogeneous, recursing over nested
structs and std::vector<T>.

Mechanism (PFR + tag_invoke):
 - Iterate fields by index: I in make_index_sequence<boost::pfr::tuple_size_v<S>>.
   key   = boost::pfr::get_name<I,S>()   (Boost 1.90 core_name; C++20/gcc15 OK)
   field = boost::pfr::get<I>(s)
   Unwrap Documented via as_value(field) (from ddm-6zj.2). Serialize the
   underlying value: recurse if it is a config struct; for std::vector<U> emit a
   JSON array (each U scalar or struct); else scalar via boost::json.
 - value_to reverse: value_to<field_value_type_t<F>> per field, re-wrap into the
   field (assign to .underlying()).

ADL / injection (IMPORTANT — evaluate & decide): a generic tag_invoke template
living in namespace boost_config is NOT found by ADL for a value_to<S>() call
when S is in the author's namespace (associated namespaces are boost::json and
S's namespace, not boost_config). Do NOT inject templates into boost::json.
Recommended: provide the generic implementation as boost_config free functions,
plus a one-line opt-in macro the author writes at namespace scope next to their
struct:
     BOOST_CONFIG_JSON(MyConfig)
   expanding to thin tag_invoke(value_from_tag/value_to_tag<MyConfig>, ...) free
   functions in the CURRENT namespace that delegate to the boost_config generic
   impl. This is what makes boost::json::value_from/value_to<MyConfig>() work as
   the requirement states, without annotating fields. (Confirm whether a hidden-
   friend form is cleaner; document the chosen form.)

Mismatch policy (satisfies the required tests):
 - parse<S>(json::value const&, parse_options{bool reject_unknown=true;
   bool require_all=false;}) is the configurable entry point:
     * start from S{} (defaults from member initializers)
     * overlay each present key
     * reject_unknown=true  -> throw on a JSON key with no matching field
     * require_all=true     -> throw when a field has no key in the JSON
       (models a "required / no default" field)
 - value_to<S> delegates to parse with {reject_unknown=true, require_all=false}.

Tests (test_json.cpp):
 - round-trip value_from -> value_to for: flat all-Documented; flat mixed
   Documented + plain (heterogeneous, e.g. MyConfig{Documented<int> x; float y;});
   nested (MyStruct/MyParams); vector-of-scalar and vector-of-struct fields.
 - EXTRA attribute in JSON -> value_to throws (reject_unknown).
 - MISSING attribute in JSON for a required field -> parse(require_all=true)
   throws; and that with require_all=false the struct default is kept.
close_reason: Closed

title: boost-config: Documented<T> wrapper + traits (Documented.hpp)

title: boost-config: scaffold the CMake package (header-only INTERFACE lib + tests + install/export)
description:
Create devel/boost-config/ mirroring the layout/style of devel/phlex-arrow-common:

Layout:
 - boost-config/CMakeLists.txt  (cmake_minimum_required 3.25; project(boost_config VERSION 0.1.0 CXX); C++20, no extensions; -Wall -Wpedantic -Werror)
 - boost-config/boost_config/   (public headers: Documented.hpp, type_name.hpp, json.hpp, jsonnet.hpp, boost_config.hpp umbrella)
 - boost-config/test/CMakeLists.txt + test_*.cpp
 - boost-config/cmake/boost_configConfig.cmake.in
 - boost-config/CMakePresets.json
 - boost-config/README.md, boost-config/.gitignore
 - init a git repo (per-package repo like siblings)

Target: header-only INTERFACE library boost_config::boost_config (all functionality is template/inline). Rationale: Documented<T>, PFR reflection, tag_invoke, and jsonnet emission are all templates; a compiled lib would carry no non-template symbols worth exporting. (Boost.Core demangle used by jsonnet is header-only.)

CMake essentials:
 - find_package(Boost REQUIRED)  # PFR, JSON, Core all header-only, part of Boost
 - add_library(boost_config INTERFACE)
 - target_link_libraries(boost_config INTERFACE Boost::boost Boost::json)  # confirm json target name in this Boost (Boost::json)
 - target_include_directories(boost_config INTERFACE $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}> $<INSTALL_INTERFACE:include>)
 - target_compile_features(boost_config INTERFACE cxx_std_20)
 - enable_testing(); add_subdirectory(test)
 - install(TARGETS ... EXPORT), install(DIRECTORY boost_config/ ... PATTERN *.hpp), install(EXPORT ... NAMESPACE boost_config::), configure_package_config_file + write_basic_package_version_file (SameMajorVersion)

Config.cmake.in: @PACKAGE_INIT@ + find_dependency(Boost) + include Targets.cmake.

Tests: plain style used across devel/ — a check(bool, msg) helper, main() returns nonzero on failure, add_test per exe. No gtest.

devel/ superbuild auto-discovers the package (scans for project()/find_package()); Boost is external, so no edit to devel/CMakeLists.txt needed. Acceptance: cmake -S devel -B builds/devel builds boost-config and its (initially trivial) test passes.
close_reason: Closed

title: edep.segments n_electrons ~ 0: SecondaryDeposit equals EnergyDeposit in default physics
description:
The edep.observables 'segments' recombination split is implemented exactly per edep-sim's own model (EDepSimHitSegment.hh): N_q=E/19.5eV, N_ph=N_q*SecondaryDeposit/EnergyDeposit, N_e=N_q-N_ph. In the default edep-sim/Geant4 physics config, however, TG4HitSegment.SecondaryDeposit (fed by G4Step::GetNonIonizingEnergyDeposit(), summed in EDepSimHitSegment.cc:223) comes out EQUAL to EnergyDeposit for nearly every segment. That drives N_ph=N_q and N_e~0 for a MIP muon in LAr, i.e. the ionization-electron count -- the primary input to downstream TPC drift sim -- is ~zero. Verified via the run-smoke-hdf.sh HDF5 output: secondary_deposit==energy_deposit for ~all rows; n_electrons=0 except a stray row.

## Context
Not a defect in the observables node or the Q5 pipeline (both verified end-to-end in ddm-6rn). This is an edep-sim physics-configuration matter: NonIonizingEnergyDeposit must reflect the scintillation (recombination) fraction, which normally requires enabling edep-sim's ionization/recombination handling (its physics constructor / LArQL-style model) rather than relying on the bare QGSP_BERT default. Investigate what edep-sim configuration correctly populates SecondaryDeposit as the optical-photon fraction, and confirm the example.gdml LArTracker SD path exercises it.
acceptance_criteria: n_electrons is physically sensible (dominant over n_photons for a MIP in LAr) for the smoke workflow, driven by correct edep-sim SecondaryDeposit population; documented how the recombination fraction is configured.

title: Simphony GPU runtime: pin optix-dev to 8.1.0 to match host NVIDIA driver 555 (fix optixInit LIBRARY_NOT_FOUND)
description:
The edep-simphony-plugin GPU optical path aborts at optixInit() with OPTIX_ERROR_LIBRARY_NOT_FOUND (CSGOptiX/Ctx.cc), so no GPU photons are produced. Discovered while running tests/edep-simphony-cli in gpu mode on wcgpu0 (see closed ddm-p5j.6).

DIAGNOSIS (OptiX SDK vs driver version mismatch):
- The gcc15 env concretizes optix-dev@9.1.0; simphony's libCSGOptiX.so exports g_optixFunctionTable_118 (OptiX 9.1.0 / ABI 118).
- simphony/docs/getting-started.md OptiX<->driver matrix: OptiX 9.1.0 requires NVIDIA driver >= 590 (Dec 2025); OptiX 8.1.0 requires >= 555 (Oct 2024).
- Host driver is 555.42.06 (per /proc/driver/nvidia/version). So 9.1.0 is too new; 8.1.0 is the matching OptiX for this host.

ROOT-CAUSE LOCATION:
- simphony/package.py (extern/repos/edep-simphony-spack/.../packages/simphony/package.py:58) has an UNCONSTRAINED depends_on('optix-dev'), so Spack picks the latest (9.1.0). (Note: this is the simphony recipe, not edepsim's.)

PROPOSED FIX:
- Pin optix-dev@8.1.0 to match driver 555. optix-dev@8.1.0 is available in Spack.
- Preferred: constrain at the ENVIRONMENT level (host-specific), e.g. add 'simphony ^optix-dev@8.1.0' (or an optix-dev@8.1.0 spec) to extern/envs/gcc15/spack.yaml, since the right OptiX version depends on the host driver. (A recipe-level bound would wrongly pin all hosts.)
- Rebuild simphony + edep-simphony-plugin against optix-dev@8.1.0.

VERIFY AFTER:
- nm -D $(spack -e extern/envs/gcc15 location -i simphony)/lib/libCSGOptiX.so | grep g_optixFunctionTable  -> expect the 8.1-matching ABI, not _118.
- Confirm the driver OptiX host library libnvoptix.so.1 is present/loadable (only libnvidia-rtcore.so.555.42.06 was found on the host; if libnvoptix.so.1 is genuinely absent this is a SEPARATE driver-userspace gap that the version pin will not fix).
- Initialise the Opticks RNG cache (~/.opticks/rngcache/RNG), also required by the GPU path.
- Validation: EDEP_SIMPHONY_MODE=gpu tests/edep-simphony-cli/run.sh gcc15  -> non-empty GPUPhotonHits.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Brett Viren
comments.text:
RESOLVED — GPU optical path now works end to end.

Two fixes:
1. optix-dev pinned to 8.1.0 (matches driver 555.42.06) and simphony + edep-simphony-plugin rebuilt. This cleared the ABI-118/driver-590 mismatch; libCSGOptiX.so now targets the OptiX 8.1 ABI.
2. libnvoptix.so.1 IS present (Debian pkg) at /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/nvidia/current/libnvoptix.so.1, but that dir is NOT in the ldconfig cache or ld.so.conf.d, so optixInit()'s dlopen failed with OPTIX_ERROR_LIBRARY_NOT_FOUND. tests/edep-simphony-cli/run.sh now locates it (nvoptix_dir helper) and prepends the dir to LD_LIBRARY_PATH for the GPU run.

Also: the pre-generated Opticks RNG cache (~/.opticks/rngcache/RNG) turned out NOT to be required by this build — the run succeeds without it (the earlier RNG error was under the old optix-dev 9.1.0 build). Removed that gate from the test.

VERIFIED on wcgpu0: EDEP_SIMPHONY_MODE=gpu tests/edep-simphony-cli/run.sh gcc15 -> PASS, non-empty GPUPhotonHits (13931 for 1 event, 26498 for 2). Plugin log shows genstep collection + GPU transport (nphoton=19257, nhit=13931). auto mode now selects GPU; full 'umbrella gcc15 test' suite green.

RESIDUAL (optional, not blocking the test): libnvoptix's dir is only added to LD_LIBRARY_PATH inside the test script. For general GPU runs (or the future phlex-node GPU path, ddm-p5j.7) it would be cleaner to put /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/nvidia/current on the loader path system-wide (an /etc/ld.so.conf.d entry) or in the env's runtime environment.

title: Implement Q5 observables output: edep.segments + edep.photons Arrow TableGroup
description: Replace the placeholder edep_sim_phlex::TrackingResult with the decided Q5 OBSERVABLES layer (ddm-4nd.5 decision). The Tracking node emits one phlex_arrow::TableGroup product (type 'edep.observables') with members: 'segments' (ionization electrons N_e + scintillation photons N_ph per TG4HitSegment, via recombination N_q=E/19.5eV, N_ph=N_q*Sec/E, N_e=N_q-N_ph, NO re-fluctuation; plus start/stop 4-pos, track_length, primary_id, contributors list, sd name) and 'photons' (per TG4PhotonHit: start/stop 4-pos, energy, process, primary_id, sd name). Arrow schema metadata follows the ecosystem convention (arrow.schema/arrow.schema.version keys; CLHEP mm/MeV/ns units declared). MC-truth (HepMC) and associations layers are deferred to follow-on issues.
notes:
Observables layer IMPLEMENTED + smoke-tested. Tracking node now returns phlex_arrow::TableGroup (type edep.observables) with members segments (edep.segments: N_e/N_ph via recombination, start/stop 4-pos, track_length, primary_id, contributors list<int32>) + photons (edep.photons). New files edep_sim_phlex/Observables.{hpp,cpp}; Data.hpp now holds product-type/member-name constants (kObservablesType/kSegmentsMember/kPhotonsMember); TrackingResult removed. Deps added: Arrow + phlex_arrow_common. ValidateFull() guards both tables. Smoke test green: segments=1580/1505 photons=0, phlex exit 0. Deferred layers -> new follow-on issues. Units mm/MeV/ns in schema metadata.
REFACTOR (per edep-sim author discussion): split into two Phlex nodes. Tracking now outputs the native edep-sim TG4Event by value (product suffix 'tg4event') -- no Arrow dependency in the tracking node. A NEW separate transform node 'edep_observables' (modules/observables.cpp, lib esp_observables) consumes TG4Event const& and produces the edep.observables TableGroup via to_observables(). Chain: gun -> edep_sim_tracking(GenEvent->TG4Event) -> edep_observables(TG4Event->TableGroup) -> tracking_observer. VERIFIED: TG4Event works as a Phlex product (moved by value; copied out of persistency->summary()); build clean, smoke test green (segments=1580/1505), phlex exit 0. Observer default input_from now 'edep_observables'. to_observables()/Observables.{hpp,cpp} unchanged.
=== VERIFIED END-TO-END + CLOSED (2026-07-09, Claude) ===
Checked the full capability: TG4Event -> observables -> Arrow -> HDF5. ALL THREE work.

(1) CONVERT TG4Event: the Tracking node emits the native TG4Event (suffix tg4event); the separate edep_observables node (modules/observables.cpp, lib esp_observables) consumes it. Confirmed present + built.

(2) EXTRACT OBSERVABLES -> ARROW: to_observables() (Observables.cpp) builds the edep.observables TableGroup with members segments (n_electrons, n_photons, energy_deposit, secondary_deposit, track_length, primary_id, start/stop 4-pos, contributors list<int32>, sd) and photons, each with arrow.schema/arrow.schema.version + edep.units.{length,energy,time}=mm/MeV/ns metadata. Formula is exactly edep-sim's documented model.

(3) SAVE TO HDF5: no new code needed -- the generic narrow-waist phlex_arrow_hdf_output module (phlex-arrow-hdf) writes ANY Arrow TableGroup product to HDF5. It picks up edep.observables automatically. PROVEN end-to-end: produced edep-observables.h5 (565 KB, phlex exit 0) holding both events with full segments (1505/1580 rows) + photons tables and the Arrow schema metadata, structured under /event/<n>/edep_observables:.../observables/{segments,photons}/<column>.

GAPS FOUND + FIXED while verifying:
 - Stale GDML path in test/smoke-gun-tracking.jsonnet (reference/edep-sim -> devel/edep-sim); the old path aborted with G4 InvalidRead. Fixed.
 - No workflow/test wired observables -> HDF5. Added test/smoke-gun-tracking-hdf.jsonnet + test/run-smoke-hdf.sh (asserts the .h5 exists AND contains observables/segments/n_electrons). README gained a Testing section. Both smoke tests green.
 - run-smoke-hdf.sh check bug: grep -q under set -o pipefail SIGPIPE-killed h5ls -> false negative; fixed by capturing h5ls output first.

SEPARATE PHYSICS CONCERN (not part of this issue's deliverable, filed as follow-on ddm-496): in the default physics config SecondaryDeposit == EnergyDeposit for ~all segments, so n_electrons ~ 0. The observables node applies edep-sim's formula faithfully; correcting the recombination split is an edep-sim physics-configuration matter.
close_reason: Q5 observables layer implemented (edep.observables TableGroup: segments+photons) AND the full TG4Event -> observables -> Arrow -> HDF5 chain verified end-to-end (new run-smoke-hdf.sh produces a valid .h5 with both tables). HDF5 persistence needed no new code -- the generic phlex_arrow_hdf_output module handles the TableGroup. --force: blocker ddm-4nd.11 stays open for its own remaining scope (GDML-as-resource, macro validation, hooks) but its tracking capability is verified working. Physics-config concern (n_electrons~0) split out to ddm-496.

title: GPU test job: exercise the plugin standalone (edep-sim + plugin) and assert photon output
description:
A test that runs the plugin end-to-end at the edep-sim level (before Phlex integration) to prove the Spack-installed plugin works on a real GPU.

Job: run 'edep-sim -p QGSP_BERT -g <test.gdml> -o out.root -e <N> macro/...mac' with EXTRAPHYSICS pointing at the installed libedep-simphony-plugin.so and the OPTICKS_* env from setup_run_environment.

Assertions:
- process exits 0, GPU launch (G4CXOpticks::simulate) actually runs (not a CPU-only fallback);
- out.root contains EDepSimEvents AND a non-empty GPUPhotonHits tree (branches EventId/TrackId/Process/Wavelength/HitPos);
- optionally exercise EDEP_SIMPHONY_DUAL / DEBUGHEAVY to populate CPU/all-photon trees.

GPU requirements (from README): NVIDIA GPU with OptiX 8.1.0-compatible driver (e.g. 555.42.06); simphony's CSGOptiX7.ptx present; enough VRAM for the slot budget. This is NOT runnable on a GPU-less CI node -- mark the test GPU-required (skip/xfail without a GPU) and document the target host (e.g. wcgpu1). Capture the exact driver/OptiX/CUDA versions used.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Brett Viren
comments.text:
BLOCKER assessment (wcgpu0.phy.bnl.gov, 2026-07-07):

Build/install verified working: edep-simphony-plugin-master installed, macros installed to <prefix>/share/edep-simphony-plugin/macro, CSGOptiX7.ptx present in view.

Two blockers prevent running the GPU test now:

1) GPU not reachable on wcgpu0: no /dev/nvidia* device nodes; system /usr/bin/nvidia-smi fails to communicate with the driver, though the kernel module IS loaded (NRVM 555.42.06 per /proc/driver/nvidia/version). Needs host-admin action (device nodes / GPU reset) or a different host.

2) OptiX ABI mismatch: env concretized optix-dev@9.1.0; simphony 0.7.0's libCSGOptiX.so exports g_optixFunctionTable_118 (OptiX ABI 118, OPTIX_VERSION 90100). Driver 555.42.06 supports OptiX 8.1.0/ABI 93 only (epic notes even ABI 105 is incompatible). optixInit() will fail to bind the ABI-118 table on this driver. Root cause: simphony recipe depends_on('optix-dev') with no version bound -> drifted to 9.1.0. Fix options: pin optix-dev@8.1.0 (ABI 93) in the env + rebuild simphony/plugin, OR upgrade the host driver to one supporting OptiX 9.1 (R565+/570+). This likely warrants its own issue and overlaps ddm-p5j.4/.5 scope.

comments.author: Brett Viren
comments.text:
Delivered the GPU-gated test harness under tests/gpu/ (per decision to write it now; runnable once the env blockers above are resolved):
- run_plugin_gpu_test.sh: skips cleanly (exit 77) when no usable GPU; else activates the gcc15 Spack env (spack env activate -d), runs 'edep-sim -p <phys> -g <gdml> -o <out> -e <N> plugin_gpu_test.mac', then asserts. Parameterized via PLUGIN_TEST_* env vars; geometry PLUGIN_TEST_GDML is required (ddm-p5j.5).
- plugin_gpu_test.mac: self-contained macro (inlines the action loading; no relative /control/execute).
- assert_gpu_output.py: PyROOT check for EDepSimEvents present + non-empty GPUPhotonHits with branches EventId/TrackId/Process/Wavelength/HitPos. Non-empty GPUPhotonHits is the proxy for 'GPU launch actually ran'.
- README.md: requirements, target host, the driver<->OptiX-ABI check, geometry-fixture note, and the current blockers.

Verified on wcgpu0 (GPU-independent parts only): bash/python syntax; GPU-skip path returns 77; assertion logic validated against 4 synthetic ROOT files (good->pass; empty/missing-tree/missing-branches->fail) using the env's PyROOT (ROOT 6.38.04). The end-to-end GPU run itself remains UNVERIFIED pending (1) a reachable GPU and (2) OptiX-ABI reconciliation (optix-dev 9.1.0/ABI118 vs driver 555.42.06).

Also fixed the package.py: setup_run_environment now also exports PLUGIN_LIB (the shipped macros load actions via $(PLUGIN_LIB); previously only EXTRAPHYSICS was set).

comments.author: Brett Viren
comments.text:
Dispensed via tests/edep-simphony-cli/ — the edep-sim CLI counterpart of the phlex tests/edep-simphony test (shared geometry + 1 MeV e- primary). Runs the plugin standalone through the edep-sim command line with optical tracking backend selected by EDEP_SIMPHONY_MODE=auto|cpu|gpu:
- cpu: Geant4 CPU optical (plugin NOT loaded), asserts non-empty TG4Event.PhotonDetectors. VERIFIED PASS on wcgpu0: 32181 photon-detector hits / 2 events.
- gpu: Simphony plugin -> GPUPhotonHits tree, asserts non-empty. Harness in place; GPU-gated + OptiX-runtime-gated.
- auto (default): gpu if usable else cpu.

Superseded/removed the earlier tests/gpu/ (its assert + gate logic folded into the CLI test). Integrated into 'umbrella <env> test'; full suite green (edep-simphony + edep-simphony-cli).

GPU RUNTIME BLOCKER discovered (recorded for whoever completes GPU deployment): the GPU path aborts at optixInit() with OPTIX_ERROR_LIBRARY_NOT_FOUND because the driver's OptiX host library libnvoptix.so.1 is absent in this bubblewrap (only libnvidia-rtcore.so.555.42.06 present), and the Opticks RNG cache (~/.opticks/rngcache/RNG) is not initialised. Also simphony is built against OptiX 9.1 / ABI 118 (g_optixFunctionTable_118) vs driver 555.42.06 — ABI compatibility still to confirm once libnvoptix is present. The gpu mode skips (77) on these until the OptiX runtime is deployed.

title: Wire edep-simphony-plugin into the gcc15 Spack env + reconcile cxxstd=23
description:
Add 'edep-simphony-plugin' to the apps group in extern/envs/gcc15/spack.yaml (which already lists edepsim@4.3.0, simphony, root, etc. and the edep_simphony repo). Then concretize + install.

Reconcile the C++ standard: the plugin CMakeLists currently sets C++17, but the env is cxxstd=23 (geant4/root/edepsim/simphony all built cxxstd=23). Make the plugin build cxxstd=23 so it links against the env's libraries without ABI mismatch -- add a cxxstd variant to the package (as simphony/edepsim have) and set it to 23 in the spec.

Watch for: nvcc host-gcc gating (env uses gcc@15; nvcc caps host gcc -- reuse simphony's _cuda_host_cxx trick), and the optix-dev / cuda_arch concretization. Consider whether this belongs in the gcc15 env or the gccxx env (see memory spack-env-gccxx). Verify: 'spack -e extern/envs/gcc15 concretize -f' clean, then install, then the plugin .so appears in the view.
close_reason: Closed

title: Create edep-simphony-plugin Spack package.py
description:
Add a package.py for edep-simphony-plugin to the existing edep_simphony Spack repo (extern/repos/edep-simphony-spack/edep_simphony/spack_repo/edep_simphony/packages/edep-simphony-plugin/). Model it on the sibling simphony/edepsim recipes.

Scope:
- CMakePackage (+ CudaPackage if a cuda_arch variant is wanted). git+url sources; version 'main' from the reference repo's upstream, plus a tagged version if one exists.
- depends_on: edepsim (the plugin-capable variant/patch from ddm-p5j.3), simphony, geant4, root+tree, cuda, optix-dev, plog, glm. Propagate cxxstd to match the env (see ddm-p5j.4).
- cmake_args: feed the paths the adapted CMakeLists (ddm-p5j.1) expects; likely reuse simphony's nvcc host-gcc gating helper (_cuda_host_cxx / NVCC_APPEND_FLAGS) since the plugin also compiles against CUDA.
- install: the .so plus macro/ run macros; simphony's CSGOptiX7.ptx must be locatable at runtime.
- setup_run_environment: export the runtime knobs the plugin needs -- EXTRAPHYSICS=EXTERN:<lib>:CreatePhysicsConstructor, OPTICKS_INTEGRATION_MODE, CSGOptiX__ptxpath (-> installed .ptx), and reasonable defaults for OPTICKS_MAX_SLOT / EDEPSIM_DOKEBIRKS_VISE. EDEP_SIMPHONY_* mode vars stay user-set.
- Add the package to the edep_simphony repo's index if needed.

Verify with 'spack -e extern/envs/gcc15 spec edep-simphony-plugin' before install.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Brett Viren
comments.text:
Created edep_simphony repo recipe: extern/repos/edep-simphony-spack/edep_simphony/spack_repo/edep_simphony/packages/edep_simphony_plugin/package.py (dir underscores -> package name 'edep-simphony-plugin', class EdepSimphonyPlugin, per the wire_cell_toolkit convention).

Design:
- Plain CMakePackage (NOT CudaPackage): the plugin has no .cu sources (project LANGUAGES CXX), so nvcc is never invoked and simphony's host-gcc gating helper is NOT needed. It only links cudart transitively via simphony.
- version('master', branch='master') from https://github.com/brettviren/edep-simphony-plugin.git (no tags yet; default branch is master, not main).
- cxxstd variant (17/20/23, default 17) propagated to root/geant4/edepsim/simphony so all ABI-sharing deps unify.
- Direct deps mirror the adapted CMakeLists find_package set: root, geant4, edepsim, simphony, glm, cuda. glm is explicit because simphony 0.6.0's config references glm::glm without find_dependency(glm) (to be dropped once simphony ships the fix).
- cmake_args: only -DCMAKE_CXX_STANDARD=<cxxstd> (CMakeLists finds all else via CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH).
- @run_after('install') install_macros: copies macro/ to <prefix>/share/edep-simphony-plugin/macro (CMakeLists installs only the .so).
- setup_run_environment: EXTRAPHYSICS=EXTERN:<lib>:CreatePhysicsConstructor, OPTICKS_INTEGRATION_MODE=1, CSGOptiX__ptxpath -> simphony prefix lib/CSGOptiX7.ptx, OPTICKS_MAX_SLOT=M1, EDEPSIM_DOKEBIRKS_VISE=1. EDEP_SIMPHONY_* mode vars left user-set.

Verified: 'spack -e extern/envs/gcc15 spec edep-simphony-plugin' concretizes cleanly (default cxxstd=17); 'spec edep-simphony-plugin cxxstd=23' unifies plugin+edepsim+geant4+root+simphony all at cxxstd=23 (the env value). 'spack audit packages edep-simphony-plugin' -> DIRECTIVES/ATTRIBUTES/PROPERTIES all pass.

NOT installed here -- installation + env wiring is ddm-p5j.4. edepsim hitless-event patch is ddm-p5j.3 (recipe depends on plain edepsim for now).

comments.author: Brett Viren
comments.text: FOLLOW-UP (glm): depends_on('glm') removed from package.py — glm is not directly used by the plugin; it was only needed because simphony's exported config references glm::glm without find_dependency(glm). Expect simphony 0.8.0 to add find_package/find_dependency(glm); once on that version, remove the find_package(glm) line from the plugin CMakeLists too. Until then the plugin builds because glm is a transitive link dep of simphony (on CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH) and the CMakeLists resolves glm::glm itself.

title: Adapt edep-simphony-plugin CMakeLists to build against Spack-provided deps
description:
The plugin's CMakeLists.txt (reference/edep-simphony-plugin/CMakeLists.txt) is machine-specific and cannot be built by Spack as-is.

Current hard requirements to remove/replace:
- OPTIC_GPU_ROOT (FATAL_ERROR if unset) -> derive simphony/edep-sim from CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH instead.
- HACK_LOCAL (FATAL_ERROR if unset) -> plog + OptiX headers should come from the Spack plog and optix-dev packages.
- SPACK_VIEW / EDEPSIM_INST / EICOPT_INST hardcoded install prefixes -> use find_package / CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH.
- /usr/local/cuda/include and 'cuda' link -> use Spack cuda (CUDAToolkit / find_package).
- simphony include dirs are enumerated by hand (include/simphony/{sysrap,CSG,qudarap,CSGOptiX,gdxml,u4,g4cx}); confirm simphony's Spack install layout and prefer a simphony CMake config/target if it exports one, else keep explicit dirs relative to the simphony prefix.

Deliverable: a CMakeLists that configures purely from CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH (as Spack sets it) with no machine-specific FATAL_ERRORs, still linking G4CX/U4/CSGOptiX/QUDARap/CSG/SysRap in the correct order. Decide whether to patch the CMakeLists upstream in the plugin repo or carry the patch in the Spack package.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Brett Viren
comments.text:
Rewrote devel/edep-simphony-plugin/CMakeLists.txt (patched in the plugin repo checkout):

- Removed all machine-specific env vars / FATAL_ERRORs: OPTIC_GPU_ROOT, HACK_LOCAL, SPACK_VIEW, EDEPSIM_INST, EICOPT_INST, /usr/local/cuda.
- All deps now resolved via find_package + CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH: ROOT, Geant4, EDepSim, glm, simphony.
- simphony ships a full CMake config with imported targets (simphony::{G4CX,U4,CSGOptiX,QUDARap,CSG,SysRap}) that carry include dirs + transitive plog/glm/OptiX/CUDA(cudart). So the hand-enumerated include/simphony/* dirs, target_link_directories, plog/optix/cuda paths, and the explicit 'cuda' link are all gone. Plugin uses no CUDA driver API nor glm directly, so cudart-via-simphony suffices.
- C++ standard: -DCMAKE_CXX_STANDARD=NN honored; if unset, inherits ROOT's standard (INTERFACE_COMPILE_FEATURES on ROOT::Core, fallback to ROOT_CXX_STANDARD var). NOTE: find_package(EDepSim) overwrites CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD to ROOT's value, so the requested value is captured before that call and re-applied after.
- Build product name unchanged: libedep-simphony-plugin.so.

Verified in the gcc15 env view: configures + fully compiles + links (C++23 default). Override paths tested: no -D -> C++23; -D=17 -> warning + C++17; -D=23 -> C++23.

GOTCHA for ddm-p5j.2: simphony's exported simphonyConfig.cmake references glm::glm in SysRap's link interface but omits find_dependency(glm) -> any consumer fails at generate unless it find_package(glm) first. Worked around in the plugin, but the simphony package.py should add glm to its Config (find_dependency).

title: [epic] Run edep-simphony-plugin (GPU optical) end-to-end in Phlex
description:
Goal: run the edep-simphony-plugin inside edep-sim, inside edep-sim-phlex, inside Phlex.

The edep-simphony-plugin (libedep-simphony-plugin.so) couples edep-sim (Geant4 CPU energy-deposition sim) with simphony (GPU optical-photon transport via NVIDIA OptiX). edep-sim tracks charged particles on CPU; Cerenkov/Scintillation gensteps are handed to simphony, which ray-traces all optical photons on GPU (OptiX). Both CPU ionisation and GPU photon hits land in one ROOT file (EDepSimEvents + up to 5 GPU/CPU photon trees). The plugin is loaded into edep-sim via its EXTERN user-action mechanism (EXTRAPHYSICS, /edep/actions/load*).

## What this epic delivers
1. An edep-simphony-plugin Spack package.py.
2. Its installation wired into the gcc15 dev Spack environment.
3. A GPU test job that exercises the plugin end-to-end and asserts the GPU photon output.
4. Integration so the plugin runs within the edep-sim Phlex node (edep-sim-phlex), and thus inside Phlex.

## Reference material
- reference/edep-simphony-plugin/ : git clone of the plugin (source, CMakeLists.txt, macro/, README.md, PLAN_a2_thin_scint.md, setup_env.example.sh).
- extern/repos/edep-simphony-spack/ : existing Spack repo (edep_simphony namespace) already providing edepsim and simphony packages -- natural home for the new plugin package.
- Related epic ddm-4nd (edep-sim as a Phlex data-flow node) provides the Phlex node the plugin must plug into.

## Key constraints gathered from the reference
- Runtime REQUIRES a GPU. simphony built with OptiX 8.1.0 headers (ABI 93), compatible with driver 555.42.06; OptiX 9.0.0 (ABI 105) is NOT. GPU/driver/OptiX versions are pinned and matter for the test job.
- Plugin depends on: edep-sim, simphony, Geant4, ROOT (Core/Tree/RIO), CUDA runtime, OptiX headers, plog, glm.
- edep-sim needs a ONE-LINE source change (EDepSimUserEventAction.cc): the external-action loop moved before the 'if (!HCofEvent) return' early exit, so the GPU plugin runs even on events with no ionisation hits.
- Plugin CMakeLists.txt is machine-specific (OPTIC_GPU_ROOT, HACK_LOCAL, /usr/local/cuda, hardcoded install prefixes) -- must be adapted to consume Spack-provided deps.
- Plugin currently targets C++17; dev env is cxxstd=23. Must reconcile so it links the cxxstd=23 geant4/root/edepsim/simphony.
- Runtime knobs are env vars (EXTRAPHYSICS, OPTICKS_INTEGRATION_MODE, CSGOptiX__ptxpath -> installed CSGOptiX7.ptx, OPTICKS_MAX_SLOT, EDEP_SIMPHONY_* modes).

title: edep-sim FUNCTION node — the single edep-sim node (GenEvent in -> tracking out)
description:
Implement the node body for the FUNCTION-node pattern (Phlex transform):
input = HepMC3 GenEvent (Q3, ddm-4nd.3); output = Q5 products (ddm-4nd.5);
config = Q4 (ddm-4nd.4); kinematics injection = approach 4-A (Q7, ddm-4nd.9).

This is the FIRST/reference implementation and concretizes the node-body sketch in
ddm-4nd.9 (non-CLI parts of app/edepSim.cc main() + config marshaling). It will
establish the shared "backend" that the generator/mixing variants reuse (see the
backend-factoring decision).

Scope:
- Stand up run manager (physics-list, Q4), geometry from GDML (Q1), Phlex-bridging
  persistency manager (Q5; no ROOT/TGeo).
- Approach 4-A generator wiring; per-call feed GenEvent -> /run/beamOn (node owns
  beamOn). Support 1:1 and optional N-GenEvents-per-beamOn batching.
- Marshal captured TG4Event -> Q5 Arrow/HepMC products (+ associations).
- Correct lifecycle/ordering + clean teardown (ClearGenerators, ownership per Q7).

Acceptance: runs from in-memory GenEvent(s) with no ROOT output; emits the Q5
products; deterministic event->output mapping.

FRONTEND: receives GenEvents from upstream. BACKEND: shared (see backend decision).
notes:
=== SCOPE CLARIFICATION (2026-07-06, Brett) ===
This is now THE single edep-sim node. Kinematics ALWAYS arrive as a HepMC3
GenEvent (Q3); the SOURCE of that GenEvent -- particle-gun node (ddm-4nd.12),
mixing node (ddm-4nd.13), or any file/generator reader -- is a separate UPSTREAM
non-edep-sim node. The edep-sim node never does its own kinematics I/O; approach
4-A simply feeds whatever GenEvent it receives.
=== IMPLEMENTATION: first cut landed (2026-07-06) ===
New package devel/edep-sim-phlex/ (project edep_sim_phlex, namespace
edep_sim_phlex, C++23), auto-discovered by the devel/ superbuild.

Files:
- edep_sim_phlex/IGenEventSink.hpp  : abstract sink; feed_genevent(
    std::shared_ptr<const HepMC3::GenEvent>)  [zero-copy, lifetime-safe across
    feed->beamOn; chosen over by-value GenEvent].
- edep_sim_phlex/GenEventKine.{hpp,cpp} : G4VPrimaryGenerator + IGenEventSink.
    DIRECT G4VPrimaryGenerator (not wrapped in EDepSim::PrimaryGenerator) since
    the GenEvent carries its own vertex pos/time. Delegates conversion.
- edep_sim_phlex/Convert.{hpp,cpp} : append_primaries(GenEvent, G4Event*) free
    fn (delegated conversion). Final-state (status==1) only; unit conversion via
    HepMC3::Units to CLHEP mm/MeV/ns. TODO: validate time (c*t) convention.
- edep_sim_phlex/Tracking.{hpp,cpp} : the FUNCTION node (main()-minus-CLI).
    Approach 4-A: CreateRunManager(physics_list); base EDepSim::PersistencyManager
    (NO ROOT/TGeo) for the TG4Event summary; const_cast GetUserPrimaryGeneratorAction
    -> AddGenerator(kine). operator()(Kinematics) feeds + /run/beamOn 1 + reads
    GetEventSummary(). Ownership: node owns kine_ (unique_ptr), never
    ClearGenerators; member order gives safe teardown (persistency,runmanager,kine).
- edep_sim_phlex/Data.hpp : Kinematics{shared_ptr<const GenEvent>} input; and a
    PLACEHOLDER TrackingResult (counts) output.
- modules/tracking.cpp : PHLEX_REGISTER_ALGORITHMS -> m.transform("edep_sim_tracking",
    ..., concurrency::serial).input_family(suffix "genevent").output "tracking".
- CMakeLists.txt, cmake/edep_sim_phlexConfig.cmake.in, README.md, .clang-format.

Verified against the gcc15 view: EDepSim::edepsim/_io, phlex::core/module,
Geant4, phlex::configuration API, and the needed edep-sim internal headers all
resolve. NOT yet buildable: HepMC3 must be added to the Spack env view (Brett).

TODO before this closes: HepMC3 in view + first build/smoke test; real Q5 output
marshaling (ddm-4nd.5); GDML-as-resource (ddm-4nd.1/.2); macro validation +
lifecycle (ddm-4nd.4); hooks (ddm-4nd.6); validate the HepMC3 time convention.
=== BUILDS GREEN (2026-07-06) ===
After adding hepmc3 to the gcc15 view, the package configures, compiles and links:
  libedep_sim_phlex.so + libesp_tracking.so (the phlex module), no missing ldd deps.

Two fixes were needed:
1. phlex PACKAGING BUG (upstream): phlexConfig.cmake has an EMPTY find_dependency
   block, yet phlexTargets.cmake sets INTERFACE_LINK_LIBRARIES on spdlog::spdlog,
   TBB::tbb, fmt::fmt. So find_package(phlex) errors ("target not found") unless
   the consumer creates those imported targets FIRST. Fix: find_package(Boost
   json / TBB / spdlog / fmt) BEFORE find_package(phlex), in both CMakeLists.txt
   and the installed Config.cmake.in. (wire-cell-phlex only dodged this via its
   own find_package(TBB) + spdlog-via-WireCell.) Worth raising upstream; cf.
   ddm-c3s.19 (other phlex packaging gap).
2. HepMC3 API: there is no Units::conversion_factor(); use Units::convert(v,from,to)
   (in-place *=) on a unit value to derive the scale factor. Fixed in Convert.cpp.

Still TODO before close: first runtime smoke test (a tiny workflow feeding a
GenEvent), then Q5 output marshaling, GDML-as-resource, macro validation, hooks.
=== REFACTOR: consume HepMC3::GenEvent by value; drop Kinematics wrapper (2026-07-06) ===
Following the phlex product-model finding (see ddm-4nd.12): the node input is now
HepMC3::GenEvent (not a wrapper). Changes:
- Removed edep_sim_phlex::Kinematics; Data.hpp keeps only TrackingResult (a plain
  by-value aggregate output).
- Tracking::operator()(HepMC3::GenEvent const&); module input suffix "genevent".
- IGenEventSink::feed_genevent(HepMC3::GenEvent const&) is now NON-OWNING (stores
  a const*), because phlex delivers the product as a const& alive for the whole
  transform call (incl. the synchronous beamOn). A shared_ptr feed would have
  forced a deep copy of the HepMC3 graph -- avoided.
Rebuilds green. GenEventGun(HepMC3::GenEvent) -> Tracking(HepMC3::GenEvent const&)
now connect with the SAME product type and no shared wrapper / no cross-package dep.
=== SMOKE TEST PASSES END-TO-END (2026-07-06) ===
Workflow test/smoke-gun-tracking.jsonnet: hmp_gen_event_gun (1 GeV mu+ via
HepMC3::GenEvent) -> esp_tracking (edep-sim/Geant4, example.gdml) ->
esp_tracking_observer. Helper: test/run-smoke.sh (judges success by output, not
exit code). Result (exit 0, no crash):
  [edep-smoke] run=0 event=0 primaries=1 trajectories=274 segments=1599 photons=0
  [edep-smoke] run=1 event=0 primaries=1 trajectories=220 segments=1500 photons=0
Non-zero segments = real Geant4 energy deposition in the sensitive LArTracker.
(photons=0: example geom's LArTracker is a segment SD, not a photon SD. Trajectory
count varies per event: G4 secondaries are stochastic. event=0 both: each
beamOn 1 is a fresh G4 run.)

THREE fixes required (all relevant to the shared backend / other node variants):
1. DEFERRED INIT (thread affinity): G4RunManager is thread-affine. Building it in
   the node CTOR (plugin-registration = main thread) but running beamOn in
   operator() (a phlex WORKER thread) => empty events. Fix: defer all G4 setup to
   the first operator() via ensure_initialized(), so build+beamOn share one
   thread. Confirmed via thread-id prints: init + both events on the same worker
   thread (phlex pins a serial node to one thread). Mirrors wire-cell's Executor.
2. edep-sim BUG: EDepSim::PersistencyManager::GetEventSummary()/GetPrimaries()/
   GetTrajectories()/GetSegmentDetectors() are DECLARED in the header but NOT
   defined in libedepsim (undefined-symbol at runtime; nm shows only Store).
   Worked around with edep_sim_phlex::SummaryPersistency subclass exposing the
   protected fEventSummary. (Candidate to raise upstream, like phlex#656.)
3. TEARDOWN CRASH: ~G4RunManager() segfaults at process exit (thread-affine dtor +
   G4/ROOT static-destruction order). Fix: intentionally LEAK the G4 run manager /
   persistency / generator in ~Tracking (one node per process => lifetime == process;
   OS reclaims). run-smoke.sh also judges success by output for robustness.

New files: modules/tracking_observer.cpp (+ esp_tracking_observer target),
test/smoke-gun-tracking.jsonnet, test/run-smoke.sh. Package still builds green.
=== CORRECTNESS RISK: thread affinity across the job lifecycle (2026-07-06) ===
Per Brett: phlex runs nodes in a TBB flow_graph; a node's operator() may run on a
DIFFERENT pool thread on each call over the job. The deferred-init fix only
guarantees build+beamOn share a thread WITHIN the first call. G4's sequential
RunManager relies on G4ThreadLocal state -- most critically the navigator/world
via G4TransportationManager (thread_local in an MT-capable G4 build, which is the
norm). Geometry/physics init populates the navigator on the INIT thread; a later
beamOn on a DIFFERENT thread hits that thread's uninitialized thread-local G4
state -> empty events (or crash). The 2-event smoke test passed only because TBB
happened to schedule both operator() calls on the init thread -- luck, not a
guarantee. With more events / different load it WILL break.

FIX (needed; belongs in the shared backend, ddm-4nd.10): pin ALL G4RunManager
operations to ONE dedicated node-owned thread. The node spawns a single "G4
worker" thread that does init and every beamOn; operator() marshals each event to
it (enqueue GenEvent, block for TrackingResult) via mutex+condvar. This decouples
G4's thread affinity from TBB scheduling -- the standard pattern for embedding a
thread-affine engine in a task framework. (Also lets us join/leak that one thread
cleanly at teardown.)
=== G4TaskRunManager/useTBB evaluated and REJECTED for the affinity fix (2026-07-06) ===
Verified in the view: G4 is built G4MULTITHREADED; G4TransportationManager::
fTransportationManager is G4ThreadLocal (affinity problem confirmed);
G4TaskRunManager(G4bool useTBB=G4GetEnv("G4USE_TBB",false)) exists with a
master/worker model (GetMasterThreadId(), master<->worker barriers).

Why it does NOT solve our thread-affinity issue:
- MASTER affinity remains: BeamOn is master-side, driven from the thread owning
  the master run manager. Our operator() runs on arbitrary Phlex TBB threads =>
  still must marshal BeamOn to one fixed (master) thread = the dedicated-thread
  solution. useTBB changes nothing here.
- useTBB only routes G4's WORKER pool (PTL) through TBB (and only if the G4 build
  defined GEANT4_USE_TBB); it parallelizes events ACROSS a BeamOn(N), not within
  one event. Our model is 1 event per operator() with PHLEX owning the loop, so no
  benefit.
- Nesting G4's TBB task submission inside a Phlex flow_graph task, with the master
  blocking on workers, invites nested-arena contention + reentrancy (TBB stealing
  Phlex tasks onto the blocked thread). Fragile, no upside here.

Dedicated-G4-thread marshaling stands as the fix. The "+1 OS thread" is a blocking
hand-off (caller TBB thread parks while the G4 thread runs beamOn), so no real
oversubscription. useTBB + BeamOn(N) on the dedicated master could be a far-future
batched-events optimization only, and conflicts with Phlex-as-driver.
=== THREAD PINNING DONE; but an UNAVOIDABLE Geant4 process-exit crash (2026-07-06) ===
Implemented the dedicated-G4-thread marshaling: a heap G4Worker owns one thread
that does init + every beamOn + teardown; operator() hands each event to it and
blocks for the result (single-slot mutex/cv handshake). CORRECTNESS ACHIEVED: all
events track correctly regardless of which TBB thread calls operator(). Verified
via thread-id prints earlier: G4 work stays on the one worker thread.

REMAINING ISSUE (does NOT affect results): phlex exits with SIGSEGV. gdb shows the
crash is on the MAIN thread during __run_exit_handlers ->
G4PhysicalVolumeStore::~G4PhysicalVolumeStore() -> Clean() -> ~G4PVPlacement() ->
G4VPhysicalVolume::GetRotation(). It fires AFTER all events are tracked and phlex
prints its final summary (Seen layers / Max RSS). G4PhysicalVolumeStore is a PLAIN
GLOBAL singleton (fgInstance), torn down by its own static destructor at process
exit on the main thread.

Tried ALL plugin-side teardown variants with a dedicated thread -- delete run
manager on the owning thread, leak it, join the thread, detach it -- ALL crash
identically. The ONLY clean-exit configuration was NO dedicated thread + geometry
built on a TBB worker + leaked run manager (relied on phlex keeping the serial
node on one thread, which was empirically true in tests but per Brett not
guaranteed). => Building the Geant4 geometry on a dedicated NON-main thread and
then having G4's global geometry store torn down by main-thread static destructors
at exit is not reconcilable from the plugin side. This is a Geant4
process-teardown limitation, orthogonal to physics correctness.

run-smoke.sh judges success by OUTPUT (non-zero segments), so it reports PASS
despite the exit-time SIGSEGV. Committed state: dedicated thread + leak G4 objects.

OPTIONS for Brett (see next steps): (a) accept the post-results exit crash + note
it (correctness-first, current state); (b) drop the dedicated thread and rely on
phlex serial-node thread affinity + add a runtime same-thread assertion (clean
exit, loud failure if phlex ever migrates, instead of silent empty events);
(c) heavier: isolate edep-sim in its own process. Candidate to raise with the
edep-sim / phlex authors (off-main-thread run-manager teardown).
=== TESTED: main-thread G4 store instantiation does NOT fix the exit crash (2026-07-06) ===
Brett's idea: call G4PhysicalVolumeStore/LogicalVolumeStore/SolidStore
::GetInstance() in the Tracking ctor (main thread) so the store singletons are
created + registered for static destruction on the main thread, then build
geometry on the worker. IMPLEMENTED + TESTED: gdb shows the IDENTICAL crash
(G4VPhysicalVolume::GetRotation -> ~G4PVPlacement -> G4PhysicalVolumeStore::Clean
-> ~G4PhysicalVolumeStore -> __run_exit_handlers). Reverted.

=> The store singleton's creation thread is NOT the lever. The crash is about the
VOLUMES (G4PVPlacement + their rotation data), which are built on the worker
thread, being torn down by the main-thread static destructor at exit. Empirically,
EVERY config where geometry is built on a raw std::thread we create crashes at
exit (delete-RM/leak-RM x join/detach x pre-touch-stores). The ONLY clean-exit
config is geometry built on a phlex/TBB-managed thread (no dedicated thread). The
differentiator appears to be TBB's COORDINATED thread-pool shutdown ordering,
which a raw std::thread does not participate in.

Most promising untried real fix: run the G4 work on a TBB-managed thread that is
also pinned -- e.g. a tbb::task_arena with a single worker slot, arena.execute()
from operator(). That thread would participate in TBB shutdown (like the clean
case) while staying stable. Fiddly + re-introduces nested-TBB-arena concerns; not
yet attempted. Awaiting Brett's direction before investing.
=== TESTED: parse-GDML-on-main + adopt-on-worker -- BLOCKED by G4GDMLParser thread affinity (2026-07-06) ===
Implemented Brett's refined idea: in the Tracking ctor (main thread) do
G4GDMLParser::Read(gdml); pass the parser to the worker's run manager via
UserDetectorConstruction::SetGDMLParser, so the worker's Construct() adopts the
world without re-parsing.

RESULT: crash MOVED from process-exit to geometry-construction time. gdb:
G4GDMLParser::GetWorldVolume() (called by edep-sim Construct() on the WORKER)
BUILDS the G4PVPlacements (G4GDMLReadStructure::GetWorldVolume -> G4PVPlacement
ctor -> SetRotation -> SIGSEGV). So the actual volume placements are created in
GetWorldVolume(), NOT in Read(); and G4GDMLParser is THREAD-AFFINE -- Read() and
GetWorldVolume() must run on the same thread. Splitting them (Read on main,
GetWorldVolume on worker) corrupts the parser -> crash. GetWorldVolume is not a
cached accessor, so we can't cheaply force the build onto main either. Reverted.

CONCLUSION: putting the geometry store on the main thread while tracking on the
worker is exactly the G4 MT master/worker model. Realizing it needs EITHER full
G4 MT (master builds on main, workers clone) -- but MT drives BeamOn from the
master/main thread, which phlex occupies (deadlock) -- OR a hand-rolled
equivalent: build ALL geometry on main (Read + GetWorldVolume on main), a custom
G4VUserDetectorConstruction that adopts the pre-built world and does thread-local
SD attachment on the worker. That is a substantial, fragile redesign (SD/field
thread-local behavior). Not a quick fix.

Current committed state: dedicated-G4-thread (correct physics, verified by the
smoke test output) + exit-time SIGSEGV in G4's global G4PhysicalVolumeStore static
destructor. run-smoke.sh judges by output and reports PASS. Escalate the
off-main-thread teardown to the edep-sim/Geant4 authors.
=== DEFINITIVE: main-thread geometry blocked by G4LogicalVolume split-class TLS (2026-07-06) ===
Extended Brett's idea: in the ctor (main) call BOTH Read() AND GetWorldVolume().
Result via gdb: GetWorldVolume() DID cache (G4GDMLReadStructure::setuptoPV) -- the
world was reused, NOT rebuilt on the worker. But Construct() then crashed at the
NEXT step, EDepSimUserDetectorConstruction.cc:230 -> RootGeometryManager::Update
-> CreateMaterials -> G4LogicalVolume::GetMaterial() (segfault, on the worker).

ROOT CAUSE (definitive): G4LogicalVolume (and G4VPhysicalVolume) use SPLIT-CLASS
thread-local storage (G4LVData). A logical volume built on the MAIN thread has its
per-thread data set up for main only; ANY access from the worker
(GetMaterial(), navigation, ...) reads that worker's uninitialized G4LVData
subinstance -> crash. Allocating per-worker split-class data against shared master
geometry is exactly what G4 MT worker-initialization does. There is no by-hand
shortcut: you cannot build G4 geometry on one thread and use it from another
without G4's MT worker-init.

=> Confirms from the geometry side: "build geometry on main, worker reads
read-only" REQUIRES full G4 MT (master builds; workers run worker-init), which
drives BeamOn from the master/main thread -- incompatible with phlex owning the
main thread. Reverted to the dedicated-thread version (correct physics; exit-time
SIGSEGV in G4's global geometry-store static dtor; smoke test passes by output).
The off-main-thread teardown / MT-integration question is one for the edep-sim /
Geant4 / phlex authors.
=== RESOLVED: exit crash fixed -- clean exit + correct physics (2026-07-06) ===
Brett's "geometry fully on one thread" idea, completed. The crashing singleton is
GEANT4's global G4PhysicalVolumeStore (not edep-sim's), torn down by a MAIN-thread
static destructor at process exit. Geometry was already built+used on the worker;
the missing piece was DESTRUCTION. Fix: at worker stop (still on the worker
thread), empty the global geometry stores where the volumes were built --
  G4GeometryManager::GetInstance()->OpenGeometry();
  G4PhysicalVolumeStore::Clean();  G4LogicalVolumeStore::Clean();  G4SolidStore::Clean();
-- so the main-thread process-exit static destructors find them empty. The run
manager itself is still leaked (~G4RunManager is separately teardown-crash-prone).

RESULT: phlex exits 0 (no SIGSEGV) AND both events track correctly
(primaries=1, trajectories~200-260, segments~1500-1580). Full chain works:
GenEventGun -> edep_sim_tracking -> observer, dedicated-G4-thread pinning +
clean teardown. Smoke test passes and exits cleanly.

So the thread-pinning saga is fully closed: correctness (thread-affine G4 on one
dedicated worker thread) AND clean process exit (geometry destroyed on that same
thread). No upstream change was needed for this; the earlier off-main-thread
teardown escalation is no longer required.

title: Q7: Wire up Phlex node <-> edep-sim external kinematics generator instance
description:
NEW research question (being discussed with the edep-sim author).

## Question

The generator instance is constructed BY edep-sim from a macro line:
  /generator/kinematics/external/loadGenerator <library> <symbol> <option>
(also a 3-line variant; <option> is passed to the generator constructor). We need
a method for the Phlex node to pass in-memory HepMC3 GenEvent objects to that
instance, which edep-sim owns and does not hand back.

## Approaches so far

1. edep-sim provides a method for the Phlex node to RETRIEVE the generator
   instance. (May require changes to edep-sim if no such accessor exists.)
2. A singleton REGISTRY; the generator self-registers its `this` pointer under a
   key given via <option>; node looks it up and calls a setter (borrow-the-instance).
3. A singleton registry holds CHANNEL objects (e.g. std::deque<GenEvent>); the
   node constructs+registers a channel and passes its key via <option>; node
   pushes, generator pulls (mediating channel). [recommended in prior discussion]

## Comments / constraints (from discussion)

- SINGLETON-FREE variant: encode a pointer as a string in <option>; the generator
  parses it to size_t and reinterpret_casts to the target type. In approach 3 this
  lets the registry be omitted entirely and the channel pointer be passed directly.
- SHARED TYPE CONTRACT: approaches 2/3 need a small shared library (the channel/
  feed type, and any borrow interface) that BOTH the node and the plugin .so link
  via DT_NEEDED. dlopen detail: edep-sim loads the plugin with dlopen(RTLD_LAZY)
  i.e. RTLD_LOCAL (EDepSimGetExternalActionConstructor.cc:38); that only hides the
  plugin's OWN symbols — it still resolves its DT_NEEDED deps, so a shared feed lib
  maps ONCE and both sides see one object.
- LIFECYCLE: the node must not exercise the conduit before it is fully
  established. Generator is constructed at generator-setup (GetGenerator) time,
  before the first /run/beamOn; the node owns beamOn (Q4). Push event(s) then
  BeamOn; generator pulls in GeneratePrimaryVertex(G4Event*, suggestedPosition).
- CONCURRENCY: G4RunManager is a PROCESS singleton -> exactly one edep-sim instance
  per process; the node must run serial (Phlex concurrency::serial). Serial gives
  deterministic event->output mapping. G4-MT would create per-worker generator
  instances and scramble ordering -> avoid; parallelize across processes/nodes.
- SYMMETRY with Q6: this data-IN kinematics feed and the Q6 data-OUT hook/truth
  sink are the SAME node<->plugin rendezvous pattern in opposite directions;
  design them as one convention.
- PACKAGING: each approach has different repercussions for how deps (phlex,
  geant4, hepmc3, edep-sim, arrow, ROOT) factor across packages -> see the new
  package/dependency-factoring issue.

## Deliverable

Ranked approaches + a recorded decision, coordinated with the edep-sim author
(esp. whether approach 1 warrants an upstream edep-sim accessor).
notes:
=== APPROACH 4: node CONSTRUCTS the generator and injects it via AddGenerator ===
(code-verified against reference/edep-sim, 2026-07-06)

## The relevant edep-sim API (all already exist -- no upstream changes needed)

- EDepSim::UserPrimaryGeneratorAction::AddGenerator(G4VPrimaryGenerator*) is
  PUBLIC and just push_backs a raw ptr (UserPrimaryGeneratorAction.hh:31-33).
- GeneratePrimaries() auto-creates a DEFAULT generator ONLY when the list is
  empty (UserPrimaryGeneratorAction.cc:29-31). => if the node pre-adds one, the
  default/macro path is suppressed. Generators PERSIST across events (not cleared
  per event).
- ClearGenerators() deletes every held generator (cc:76-84). The action DESTRUCTOR
  does NOT call it (cc:22-24). Messenger triggers it on `/generator/clear`
  (Messenger.cc:174); `/generator/add` does CreateGenerator()+AddGenerator()
  (:177-178).
- CreateGenerator() returns an EDepSim::PrimaryGenerator -- a COMPOSITE
  G4VPrimaryGenerator wrapping (kinematics, count, position, time)
  (PrimaryGenerator.hh:22-25). Its GeneratePrimaryVertex(evt) pulls a suggested
  vertex from the POSITION generator and passes it to
  VKinematicsGenerator::GeneratePrimaryVertex(evt, vertex) (PrimaryGenerator.cc:45,50).
- CRUCIAL ownership fact: EDepSim::PrimaryGenerator's destructor is EMPTY
  (PrimaryGenerator.cc:31) -- it does NOT delete its sub-generators.

## Consequence: a bare VKinematicsGenerator cannot be added directly

Our HepMC->G4 converter is a VKinematicsGenerator (signature GeneratePrimaryVertex
(evt, suggestedPosition)), NOT a G4VPrimaryGenerator. It must be WRAPPED in an
EDepSim::PrimaryGenerator (kine=ours, + count/position/time) before AddGenerator.

## Variant 4-A (recommended): node builds the whole object graph

Node constructs: our HepMC VKinematicsGenerator + trivial count/position/time
sub-generators + the EDepSim::PrimaryGenerator wrapper; calls
action->AddGenerator(wrapper). Node KEEPS the pointer to the HepMC kinematics
generator and feeds each GenEvent by a DIRECT method call before beamOn.
  + No dlopen, no extern-C ABI, no <option> string, no registry/singleton,
    no pointer-in-string trick. The data-in path is a plain method call.
  + The generator need not be a runtime plugin .so at all -- it's a normal class
    the node links (from a kinematics lib depending on edep-sim+G4+HepMC3).
  + Strong type safety; trivially unit-testable; no upstream edep-sim change.
  - Node must assemble the PrimaryGenerator (incl. a position generator). For
    HepMC the vertex is IN the GenEvent, so use a position generator whose
    ForcePosition()==false (PrimaryGenerator.cc:75) so the GenEvent positions are
    respected; the kinematics gen can offset by the suggested vertex if desired.
  - Bypasses edep-sim's macro/factory generator config for position/time/count
    (slight tension with Q4 "macros as sole config" -- but Q4 already reserves
    node-owned setup; the node simply does NOT emit /generator/... macros).

## Variant 4-B: use the messenger/factory to build it

Node sets kinematics factory=external via macro + /generator/add. But then the
instance is created inside the factory and the node lacks the pointer -> collapses
back to approaches 1-3's retrieval problem. Not worth it.

## Ownership resolution (4-A) -- the key ask

- Sub-generators (incl. our HepMC kinematics gen): NODE-OWNED. PrimaryGenerator's
  empty dtor means ClearGenerators() does NOT delete them. So the node's feed
  pointer stays valid until the NODE deletes it. Clean.
- The PrimaryGenerator WRAPPER: owned by the action -- ClearGenerators() deletes
  it. So after AddGenerator the node must NOT delete the wrapper (avoid double
  free). For clean teardown: node calls action->ClearGenerators() ONCE (deletes
  wrappers) THEN deletes its sub-generators; or just let process exit.
- Contract to document: AddGenerator transfers wrapper ownership to the action;
  sub-generators remain node-owned; never feed after ClearGenerators.

## Accessing the action (correction to the Q7 sketch)

AddGenerator is public on the ACTION, not the messenger -- the node needs the
UserPrimaryGeneratorAction*, not the UserPrimaryGeneratorMessenger. Two ways:
  (a) node creates the action itself (it replicates CreateRunManager's
      SetUserAction step; see node-body sketch) -> holds the pointer directly,
      no cast. CLEANEST.
  (b) node calls edep-sim CreateRunManager(), then
      const_cast<EDepSim::UserPrimaryGeneratorAction*>(
        static_cast<const EDepSim::UserPrimaryGeneratorAction*>(
          runManager->GetUserPrimaryGeneratorAction()))
      (G4RunManager::GetUserPrimaryGeneratorAction() returns a CONST ptr).

## Ranking (updated)

4-A > 3 (channel/feed) > 2 (self-register) > 1 (edep-sim accessor) > 4-B.
Rationale: 4-A removes the entire rendezvous problem -- the node holds the pointer
by construction. Approaches 1-3 only earn their keep if we deliberately want the
generator to remain a dlopen'd plugin DECOUPLED from the node (looser link-time
deps). That is a packaging trade-off, see ddm-4nd.10:
  - 4-A: node links edep-sim+G4+HepMC3 (heavier node deps), fewest moving parts.
  - 1-3: node only needs the feed contract lib; generator is a separate .so
    (looser coupling, more ceremony).
No approach needs an upstream edep-sim change (unlike approach 1, which might).
=== PHLEX NODE BODY SKETCH (maps edepSim.cc main() -> node; where Q7 slots in) ===

Per Brett: node implements the non-CLI parts of app/edepSim.cc main(), replacing
getopt with Phlex-config marshaling.

ONE-TIME (node construction / first configure):
  1. EDepSim::LogManager::Configure(); set log/debug levels from config.
  2. Build run manager. Either call EDepSim::CreateRunManager(physicsList) [Q4
     physics-list category], OR replicate its body so the node HOLDS the
     UserPrimaryGeneratorAction* (approach 4-A, access path (a)).
     (CreateRunManager sets: UserDetectorConstruction, PhysicsList(physName),
     UserPrimaryGeneratorAction, Run/Event/Stacking/Tracking/Stepping actions.)
  3. Install our Phlex-bridging persistency manager INSTEAD of
     RootPersistencyManager (Q5; => no ROOT output, and by Q1 no TGeo built).
  4. UI = G4UImanager::GetUIpointer().
  5. Geometry: apply `/edep/gdml/read <gdml>` from config/resource (Q1) -- NOT a
     CLI path. (No `/edep/db/open`; Q5.)
  6. `/edep/control edepsim-defaults 1.0`.
  7. Apply validated user macro TEXT (physics-tunings etc.) from config (Q4).
  8. [APPROACH 4-A] Construct HepMC VKinematicsGenerator (+count/position/time),
     wrap in EDepSim::PrimaryGenerator, action->AddGenerator(wrapper); KEEP the
     kinematics-gen pointer for feeding. Do NOT emit any /generator/... macro.
  9. `/edep/validateGeometry` (config), optional seed, `/edep/update`.
     [Ordering: geometry + generator + user-actions (Q6) all BEFORE /edep/update.]

PER PHLEX CALL (one input GenEvent -> one event; node owns beamOn, Q4):
  a. kinematicsGen->setEvent(genEvent);   // direct method call (approach 4-A)
  b. UI->ApplyCommand("/run/beamOn 1");   // node-generated (Q4)
  c. Persistency manager has captured TG4Event -> node emits Q5 Arrow/HepMC
     products (observables/associations/truth). (Batching: N events per beamOn is
     possible if we feed a queue.)

TEARDOWN: persistencyManager->Close(); action->ClearGenerators() (deletes
wrappers) then delete node-owned sub-generators; delete runManager.

Open ordering questions to confirm in planning: exact G4 state (PreInit vs Idle)
required for AddGenerator and for the segment-tolerance / hook-load commands;
whether multiple GenEvents per beamOn (batching) is wanted vs strict 1:1.
=== DECISION (2026-07-06): Approach 4-A ===
The Phlex node constructs the EDepSim::PrimaryGenerator (wrapping our HepMC3
VKinematicsGenerator + trivial count/position/time sub-generators), calls
action->AddGenerator(wrapper), and keeps the kinematics-generator pointer to feed
each GenEvent by a direct method call. No dlopen / extern-C / <option> / registry.
Ownership: sub-generators are NODE-owned (PrimaryGenerator has an empty dtor);
the wrapper is action-owned (deleted by ClearGenerators()).
PreInit vs Idle: NOT a blocker -- the node constructs Geant4 in the context of
having received all user configuration, so it can reach either state as needed;
we will settle the exact state ourselves.
Batching: promoted to first-class scope -- see the new node-variant issues; the
node may feed 1 GenEvent : 1 beamOn, or N per beamOn.
Packaging consequence recorded in ddm-4nd.10 (generator is a linked class; the
node link-depends on edep-sim + G4 + HepMC3).
close_reason: Closed

title: Q5: What data representation(s) for edep-sim tracking output
description:
## Question

What should "the" edep-sim node output as Phlex data product(s)? Output is
variable: ionization energy deposits (→ TPC current waveforms downstream) and/or
scintillation photon hits (→ photo-detector PE), possibly both in one run, and
varying by detector type.

## Context

- Native summary is TG4Event (ROOT TObjects), extracted via
  EDepSim::PersistencyManager::GetEventSummary():
  - Primaries: vector<TG4PrimaryVertex> (vertex 4-pos, generator/reaction/xsec/
    weight/prob, particles[PDG,4-mom,trackid]).
  - Trajectories: vector<TG4Trajectory> (trackid, parentid, PDG, initial 4-mom,
    points[4-pos, 3-mom, process, subprocess]) — truth.
  - SegmentDetectors: map<sdname, vector<TG4HitSegment>> (energy deposit,
    secondary/scintillation deposit, track length, start/stop 4-pos, contributors)
    — the ionization/energy-deposit product.
  - PhotonDetectors: map<sdname, vector<TG4PhotonHit>> (start/stop 4-pos, energy,
    process, primaryid) — the optical product.
- Our declared transient lingua franca is Apache Arrow; existing devel packages
  wrap Arrow RecordBatches as Phlex products with facade accessors (see
  phlex-arrow-common, wire-cell-arrow).
- Downstream consumers differ (WCT sim expects depos; PDS expects photon hits).

## Sub-questions / candidate directions (to research)

1. One product (whole TG4Event-equivalent) vs several products (deposits,
   photon-hits, trajectories, primaries) so downstream nodes subscribe to only
   what they need. Multiple named products is likely.
2. Representation per product: pass TG4* objects directly (ROOT dep everywhere)
   vs Arrow RecordBatch (lingua franca, jagged per-event lists) vs WCT IDepo-like
   facades. Map TG4HitSegment → deposit schema; TG4PhotonHit → photon-hit schema.
3. How does detector-type variability select which products are active? (config
   in Q4, hooks in Q6.)
4. Relationship to existing DUNE data-model / wire-cell-arrow depo schema; reuse
   vs new schema. Truth-association keys (contributors/trackid) preserved in Arrow.

## Deliverable

Ranked options with pros/cons; a recorded decision. Couples to Q3 (shared
particle/vertex types), Q4 (which outputs active), Q6 (hook-provided products).
notes:
=== RESEARCH FINDINGS — options, pros/cons, ranking (partial; primaries part defers to Q3) ===

## Key facts

- Arrow lingua franca in this ecosystem = `phlex_arrow::TableGroup { std::string
  type; std::map<std::string, std::shared_ptr<arrow::Table>> members; }`
  (phlex-arrow-common/TableGroup.hpp). Domain naming already in use:
  `wc.frame` (members traces/frame_tags/trace_tags/cmm), `wc.deposet`,
  `wc.tensorset`. Convert nodes do to_table_group / *_from_table_group, and the
  read side wraps Arrow tables in domain structs (facade role).
- WCT already has an Arrow-backed depo path: `wc.deposet` (see wire-cell-arrow +
  wire-cell-phlex-arrow), and the WCT sim consumes DepoSet
  (deposet_to_frame.cpp). This is the natural downstream sink for edep-sim
  ionization output.
- edep-sim native summary TG4Event fields (all read without Geant4 via
  edepsim_io, ROOT-only): Primaries[TG4PrimaryVertex], Trajectories[TG4Trajectory
  + points], SegmentDetectors: map<sdname,[TG4HitSegment]> (ionization/energy
  deposits), PhotonDetectors: map<sdname,[TG4PhotonHit]> (optical).
- Products are C++20 aggregates; jagged per-event data (variable #particles/hits)
  maps onto Arrow list<struct> columns.

## Sub-decision 1: one product vs several

Recommend SEVERAL named products (one TableGroup type each, or members within):
  - `edep.segments` (ionization energy deposits; from SegmentDetectors)
  - `edep.photons`  (optical photon hits; from PhotonDetectors)
  - `edep.trajectories` (truth particle paths)
  - `edep.primaries` (primary vertices/particles; representation tied to Q3)
so downstream nodes subscribe only to what they need (WCT sim wants segments/
depos; PDS wants photons). Detector-type variability (Q4 config, Q6 hooks)
selects which are active/emitted. A single monolithic TG4Event product is
rejected: forces ROOT/all-consumers coupling and over-delivery.

## Sub-decision 2: representation per product

OPT-A: Pass TG4* ROOT objects directly as Phlex products (thin wrapper struct).
  + Zero conversion; loss-free; trivial to produce from PersistencyManager.
  - Injects a ROOT TObject dependency into every downstream consumer; violates
    the Arrow narrow-waist norm; no zero-copy/columnar benefits; poor for HDF5 io.

OPT-B (recommended): Convert to Arrow TableGroup products with explicit schemas:
  - TG4HitSegment -> `edep.segments`: columns pdg-primaryid, energy_deposit,
    secondary(scint)_deposit, track_length, start{x,y,z,t}, stop{x,y,z,t},
    contributors: list<int32> (truth keys).
  - TG4PhotonHit -> `edep.photons`: start/stop 4-pos, energy, process, primaryid.
  - TG4Trajectory -> `edep.trajectories`: trackid, parentid, pdg, initial 4-mom,
    points: list<struct{4-pos,3-mom,process,subprocess}>.
  + Fits the lingua-franca / narrow-waist; HDF5 io for free; columnar; downstream
    consumers already speak TableGroup.
  + Truth-association keys (trackid/parentid/contributors) preserved as columns.
  - Need to author + version schemas; jagged nesting via list<struct>; per-event
    small-batch overhead (mitigate by batching events).

OPT-C: Emit WCT-native facades directly (e.g. build IDepo/DepoSet from segments).
  + Plugs straight into the existing WCT sim node (deposet_to_frame) with no new
    schema; reuses wire-cell-arrow depo schema (wc.deposet).
  - Couples edep-sim output to WCT interfaces; lossy vs full TG4HitSegment
    (a WCT IDepo is a point-charge, not a start/stop segment with secondary
    deposit + contributors); photons/trajectories have no WCT depo analogue.

## Ranking

B > C > A, with an important nuance: adopt B as the CANONICAL, loss-free Arrow
representation, and provide a SEPARATE downstream convert node (segments ->
wc.deposet) so the WCT sim can consume it (recovering C's plug-in benefit without
making the canonical edep output lossy). Reuse existing wc.deposet schema for
that bridge; do NOT overload it as the primary edep-sim output.

## Open items / dependencies

- `edep.primaries` schema: align with the Q3 kinematics-input choice (if input is
  HepMC3/NuHepMC, mirror that structure on output for symmetry; TG4PrimaryVertex
  already carries generator/reaction/xsec/weight/probability + particles).
- Reconcile against any existing DUNE data-model depo/truth schema before
  finalizing column names/units (units: edep-sim uses CLHEP mm/MeV/ns; declare
  units in schema metadata).
- Batching policy (events-per-RecordBatch) to avoid tiny-batch overhead.
- Couples to Q4 (which products active) and Q6 (hook-provided products share
  association keys).
=== ADDENDUM (post-Q3 research): edep.primaries schema alignment ===

Per Q3, the canonical kinematics INPUT object is HepMC3 GenEvent + NuHepMC
conventions. For input/output SYMMETRY, define `edep.primaries` to mirror that
structure rather than inventing a parallel one:
  - particle table (pdg, 4-momentum, mass, status[NuHepMC reserved codes],
    trackid link to trajectories) + vertex table (4-position, generator/reaction,
    NuHepMC xsec/weight/process/flux attributes) + lineage as integer index
    columns (the Arrow graph-as-indices pattern from Q3).
  - TG4PrimaryVertex already carries generator/reaction/CrossSection/
    DiffCrossSection/Weight/Probability + particles -> maps cleanly to NuHepMC.
Result: the same particle/vertex Arrow schema serves both the kinematics input
(via HepMC3<->Arrow convert nodes, Q3 OPT-D) and the edep-sim primaries output.
=== SEGMENT SEMANTICS + CONSUMER REQUIREMENTS (code-verified + domain input) ===

## TG4HitSegment is an AGGREGATE, not one G4 step (src/EDepSimHitSegment.hh)

Built by AddStep() accumulating "a part of a step" across MANY G4 steps, bounded
by three tolerances (ctor defaults): maxSagitta=1mm, maxSeparation=1mm,
maxLength=5mm. Header comment: "normally, a scintillator element will only have a
single hit for a through going track (& delta-rays)."
  - maxSagitta: straightens a curved multi-step path into one Start->Stop LINE
    (lossy by design; fPath keeps the constituent step endpoints).
  - maxLength: CHOPS a long track into <=5mm pieces (one step can span several
    segments).
  - maxSeparation: FOLDS delta-ray secondaries into the parent's segment ->
    fContributors is a LIST of track ids. Comment: "When this is zero, then only
    one track id can be in a particular segment."
  - Stores NO momentum. Only Start/Stop (4-pos), EnergyDeposit, SecondaryDeposit
    (scint split), TrackLength (incl. delta-rays), Contributors[], and a heuristic
    PrimaryId ("most interesting" ancestor: pi0->gamma, mu-decay->e remapping).
  => step<->segment is MANY-TO-MANY; a segment is a calorimetric summary keyed to
     trajectories, not an element of one.

## IMPORTANT RISK: single-track-id assumption is violated by default

Existing downstream code elsewhere assumes a segment/depo has ONE uniquely
associated track id. Under DEFAULT edep-sim config (maxSeparation=1mm) that is
FALSE: delta-rays are folded, so a segment has multiple Contributors.
MITIGATION KNOB: set the SegmentSD maxSeparation=0 => "only one track id per
segment" (delta-rays become their own segments). Multi-STEP merging of the SAME
track still happens (fine for single-id; not one-step). PrimaryId remains a
separate heuristic ancestor pointer. So the single-id assumption is RECOVERABLE
by config, at the cost of more segments. ACTION: audit downstream consumers that
assume single-id; decide default (fold vs no-fold) per run. (Config surface -> Q4.)

## Two consumer classes to design the output around

C1. Ionization drift + detector-response sims. Two consumption modes:
    (a) point-like depos (samples ALONG a segment), or
    (b) the segment directly as a LINE source.
    BOTH need the number of IONIZATION ELECTRONS (N_e), NOT ionization ENERGY.
C2. Association truth (depo/segment id <-> track id[s]) for sim validation / ML
    training. Large; needed only on some runs.

## Energy -> ionization electrons is the G4 app's job (recombination)

edep-sim already applies a recombination/quenching model
(/edep/phys/ionizationModel: 0=NEST slow/general, 1=DokeBirks fast/LAr; default 1)
which fills SecondaryDeposit (the scintillation portion). Converting to quanta is
then the documented formula (TG4HitSegment.h):
    N_q  = EnergyDeposit / (19.5 eV)
    N_ph = N_q * SecondaryDeposit / EnergyDeposit
    N_e  = N_q - N_ph
NOTE: SecondaryDeposit ALREADY includes the binomial fluctuation -> do NOT
re-fluctuate N_e / N_ph. So the node (the G4 app) should EMIT N_e (ionization
