electrons) and N_ph (scintillation photons) per segment as the primary
observable, not raw energy (energy/secondary can be carried too, but N_e is what
C1 needs). This makes the recombination-model choice part of the output contract.

## Q5 output shape (refined)

- CANONICAL observable = per-segment LINE source carrying N_e (+ N_ph, + Start/
  Stop 4-pos, TrackLength). Serves C1(b) directly.
- POINT depos = a SAMPLING of the line segment (distribute N_e along Start->Stop).
  Serve C1(a) via a convert/sampler node (this is the segments->wc.deposet bridge;
  WCT IDepo is a point charge, so it consumes the sampled form). Sampling density
  is a node parameter.
- => provide segment (line) as canonical Arrow product + a sampler node to point
  depos; do not make the point form the only/native output (it is derived).

## Accepted asymmetry (per Brett)

Three tiers with DIFFERENT models, deliberately asymmetric:
  - OBSERVABLES (always): Arrow tables (N_e line-segments / point-depos, photon
    hits). The only thing most runs need.
  - MC TRUTH (optional): HepMC (primaries + trajectory genealogy; Q3/earlier
    analysis). Idiomatic there.
  - ASSOCIATIONS (optional, large): Arrow table(s) mapping observable id
    (segment/depo/photon-hit) -> contributing track id(s) [+ PrimaryId]. Keyed to
    the HepMC/trajectory truth by track id. Emitted only when truth is requested.
This HepMC-for-truth / Arrow-for-observables(+associations) split is ACCEPTED as
the design direction. Associations are a first-class (optional) Arrow product,
NOT squeezed into HepMC.

## Cross-refs

- Config knobs (maxSeparation fold/no-fold; ionizationModel NEST/DokeBirks) -> Q4
  as structured, output-affecting parameters.
- Associations as optional truth products <-> Q6 (hooks/truth) share the trackid/
  contributor association keys.
=== DECISION (2026-07-02, from edep-sim-phlex.org) ===
Three-layer output:
- MC-truth graph: primaries + trajectory genealogy -> HepMC (idiomatic, lossless;
  symmetric with Q3 input). Trajectory step-point path is the one truth item
  HepMC can't type -> Arrow side-table if needed.
- Observables: segments + photons -> Arrow tables (not HepMC).
- Associations: cross-layer links via existing keys (TrackId/ParentId truth;
  Contributors/PrimaryId observables->truth) -> Arrow tables.
Also: attempt to DISABLE edep-sim's existing ROOT output types (same mechanism as
Q1's "no TGeo": custom Phlex-bridging persistency manager, no RootPersistencyManager).

Refinements already recorded in earlier notes and still in force (planning detail):
- Observable is IONIZATION ELECTRONS (N_e) + scintillation photons (N_ph), NOT
  raw energy; recombination (NEST/DokeBirks) is the G4 app's job; N_q=E/19.5eV,
  N_ph=N_q*Sec/E, N_e=N_q-N_ph, no re-fluctuation.
- Canonical = line-segment carrying N_e; point-depos are a sampling (sampler/
  convert node; feeds WCT wc.deposet).
- Segments AGGREGATE across steps AND (by default) across tracks (delta-rays) ->
  single-track-id assumption is violated by default (see the new audit issue).
close_reason: Closed

title: Q3: Transient object model for primary-particle kinematics input
description:
## Question

What form should the primary-particle kinematics INPUT to the edep-sim node take
as an in-memory object (possibly produced by an upstream node), non-lossy w.r.t.
what Geant4 requires?

## Context

- edep-sim core (via edep main) reads several file formats: GENIE gRooTracker,
  HEPEVT text, GPS macro-driven. As a Phlex node we want an OBJECT, not a file.
- Injection hook: external kinematics generator plugin exposing C symbol
  `EDepSim::VKinematicsGenerator* CreateKinematicsGenerator(const char*)` whose
  `GeneratePrimaryVertex(G4Event*, suggestedPos)` adds G4PrimaryVertex /
  G4PrimaryParticle. So the node can pull kinematics from an in-memory object
  instead of a file — we choose that object's type.
- Geant4 needs per particle: PDG code, 4-momentum, vertex 4-position, status;
  and ideally mother/daughter structure + per-vertex interaction metadata
  (generator name, reaction, cross-section, weight, probability) — cf.
  TG4PrimaryVertex which already carries these.

## Sub-questions / candidate directions (to research)

1. HepMC3 GenEvent as the transient model (widely used, graph of vertices/
   particles, attributes, shared_ptr ownership). Non-lossy? G4 conversion path?
2. NuHepMC (neutrino HepMC3 conventions) — DUNE/GENIE adoption; adds neutrino
   interaction metadata. Strong candidate given DUNE context.
3. Invent our own minimal struct/schema.
4. Apache Arrow (our declared transient lingua franca): can kinematics be an
   Arrow table/struct? Friction with graph (mother/daughter) relations. Synergy
   vs HepMC. Possibly HepMC-as-object + Arrow-at-rest.

## Deliverable

Ranked options with pros/cons; a recorded decision. Interacts with Q5 (shared
particle/vertex representation) and the Arrow lingua-franca norm.
notes:
=== RESEARCH FINDINGS — options, pros/cons, ranking (web-cited + code-verified) ===

## Grounding facts

- HepMC3 GenEvent: shared_ptr-owned graph. GenParticle{PDG, FourVector momentum,
  mass, status, weak_ptr to production/end vertex}; GenVertex{FourVector position,
  in/out particle lists}; GenRunInfo{tools, weight names, attributes}. Typed
  Attribute system scoped by id (0=event, >0 per-particle, <0 per-vertex) carries
  arbitrary metadata (xsec, weight, process). Non-lossy for everything Geant4
  needs. v3.3.1 (2025-03). Widely adopted (Sherpa/Herwig/WHIZARD/Rivet4). [HIGH]
- NuHepMC: neutrino CONVENTIONS on HepMC3 (not a new format). SciPost Codebases
  57 (2025), spec v1.0.0. Adds mandatory central-value "CV" weight, cross-section
  metadata (GenCrossSection tot_xs/proc_xs), flux/beam metadata, exposure (POT),
  reserved status codes (particle 1=final,4=beam,20=target,21=struck-nucleon;
  vertex 1=primary,2=FSI). Implemented in ACHILLES/NEUT6+/GiBUU; converters for
  NuWro/NEUT5; MARLEY in progress. GENIE support only in UNMERGED PR #333. DUNE
  adoption UNCONFIRMED. [HIGH; DUNE-negative UNVERIFIED]
- edep-sim has NO HepMC reader (grep-confirmed: zero hepmc refs in tree/history).
  Geant4's bundled HepMC example is HepMC2-only; no official HepMC3 example found.
  So a small HepMC3 -> G4PrimaryVertex/G4PrimaryParticle converter must be WRITTEN
  by us, hosted in an external-generator .so (CreateKinematicsGenerator ABI). [HIGH]
- Current DUNE ND path: GENIE (gRooTracker) -> edep-sim -> HDF5 -> larnd-sim.
  rooTracker = flat StdHep parallel arrays (index-based graph). edep-sim tracks
  only StdHepStatus==1 (final-state) as G4 primaries; status 0 kept as
  informational, rest skipped. [HIGH]
- Arrow (our lingua franca): excellent for jagged per-event particle tables as
  List<Struct<pdg,px,py,pz,e,...>>. POOR fit for the mother/daughter GRAPH:
  Arrow/Parquet nesting is a strict one-to-many TREE with no cross-row reference
  type; a many-to-many particle<->vertex graph must be encoded as explicit integer
  index/edge columns (like rooTracker / HepMC3's own serialization), and
  cross-RecordBatch references need a global row-numbering scheme. [HIGH]

## Options

OPT-A (core recommendation): HepMC3 GenEvent + NuHepMC conventions as the
canonical transient kinematics OBJECT delivered into the edep-sim node. Feed G4
via a custom external-generator .so wrapping a HepMC3->G4PrimaryVertex converter
we write.
  + Non-lossy for PDG/4-momentum/vertex-4-position/status + mother-daughter graph;
    carries neutrino xsec/weight/process/flux/POT via NuHepMC attributes (exactly
    the metadata TG4PrimaryVertex already records) => input/output symmetry (Q5).
  + Emerging community standard; ergonomic graph object; shared_ptr ownership.
  - Not in DUNE's edep-sim path today (GENIE=rooTracker); GENIE->NuHepMC still an
    unmerged PR; must write+maintain the HepMC3->G4 converter and (interim) a
    rooTracker/HEPEVT -> HepMC3 shim (or a rooTracker external generator).

OPT-C: Apache Arrow table as the kinematics product (List<Struct> particles +
integer lineage-index columns).
  + Aligns with the Arrow narrow-waist / HDF5 io; jagged tables are natural.
  - Fights the graph structure (mother/daughter -> index columns), effectively
    reinventing rooTracker; loses HepMC/NuHepMC ergonomics + validated neutrino
    conventions; cross-batch reference fragility.

OPT-B: Invent our own minimal kinematics struct/schema.
  + Full control; minimal.
  - Reinvents HepMC3/NuHepMC; no ecosystem, no converters, no validated neutrino
    metadata conventions. Avoid.

OPT-D (recommended packaging of A): HepMC3/NuHepMC is the in-memory transient
OBJECT consumed by the sim node; provide HepMC3<->Arrow CONVERT nodes for at-rest
/ inter-node transport where the Arrow lingua franca matters, using an explicit
particle-table + lineage-index-column schema (accepting the documented graph-as-
indices friction; mirrors HepMC3's own serialization).
  + Best of both: rich graph object where G4 needs it; Arrow narrow-waist for
    storage/interchange; single validated metadata vocabulary.
  - Two representations to keep in sync via the convert nodes.

## Ranking

A (with D's Arrow bridge) > C > B.

Recommend: adopt HepMC3 GenEvent + NuHepMC conventions as the canonical transient
kinematics object; write the external-generator .so (HepMC3->G4) ourselves;
provide HepMC3<->Arrow convert nodes for at-rest/interchange. Do NOT invent our
own model. Do NOT make Arrow the primary graph carrier.

## Follow-ups / dependencies

- Must WRITE the HepMC3->G4PrimaryVertex converter (nothing reusable exists).
- Interim GENIE ingestion: rooTracker->HepMC3 shim OR a rooTracker external
  generator, until GENIE mainline emits NuHepMC.
- Pin HepMC3 version; decide NuHepMC conventions version to target.
- Feeds Q5 edep.primaries schema (mirror HepMC3/NuHepMC structure for symmetry).
- Consider a package: xerosere HepMC3/NuHepMC transient type + G4 converter + the
  Arrow convert nodes.

Sources: HepMC3 arXiv:1912.08005 + gitlab.cern.ch/hepmc/HepMC3 headers; NuHepMC
arXiv:2310.13211 / SciPost Codebases 57 (2025) + github.com/NuHepMC/Spec;
Geant4 examples/extended/eventgenerator/HepMC; edep-sim src/kinem/*RooTracker*,
*HEPEVT*, examples/ExternalKinematics; Arrow columnar format spec; Awkward Array
to/from_arrow docs.
=== DECISION (2026-07-02, from edep-sim-phlex.org) ===
- Adopt HepMC3 GenEvent with NuHepMC conventions.
- A GenEvent is the type INPUT to the node.
- Converters from other formats (e.g. Apache Arrow) are FUTURE scope.

Consequence for ddm-4nd.7 (kinematics package): the HepMC3<->Arrow convert-node
portion drops to future scope; the near-term deliverables are the GenEvent type
adoption + the HepMC3->G4 external-generator .so. (The generator instance <-> node
wiring is now its own question, Q7 / ddm-4nd.9.)
close_reason: Closed

title: Q2: Phlex-level concerns with the geometry resource type
description:
## Question

Are there Phlex-level issues with the chosen geometry representation when it is
exposed as a Phlex "resource"? The same const geometry object would be delivered
to the node's function on every call.

## Context

- Phlex "resource" = const, shared, long-lived object handed to a node each call
  (contrast with per-event data products and with one-time config).
- Phlex is concurrent: the same resource object may be read by many node
  invocations across threads simultaneously → const-correctness + thread-safe
  read access are required.
- Candidate geometry objects (from Q1): a G4 geometry, a TGeoManager, a DD4hep
  Detector, or a custom facade. Some of these have process-global singleton
  state (TGeoManager::gGeoManager; Geant4's geometry is inherently global to the
  run manager) which is in tension with "an object delivered as a resource".
- Geant4's own geometry is tied to its (thread-local) navigators and run manager,
  not naturally a passable const object.

## Sub-questions / candidate directions (to research)

1. Can a TGeoManager / DD4hep Detector satisfy Phlex's resource const + thread
   contract, given ROOT/G4 global state? What is actually const-safe to read
   concurrently?
2. Does the geometry resource need to be an opaque handle (identity/loader spec)
   rather than the live geometry, with each consumer building its own view?
3. How does the resource lifetime/identity interact with Geant4's requirement to
   build geometry inside its run manager (the node may need to construct G4 geom
   from a resource that describes rather than embodies the geometry)?
4. Confirm the state of the Phlex resources API (ddm-999) and its const/thread
   guarantees.

## Deliverable

Ranked options with pros/cons; a recorded decision. Tightly coupled to Q1.

Cross-ref: ddm-999, ddm-3j8.1.13/.14.
notes:
=== RESEARCH FINDINGS — options, pros/cons, ranking (Phlex-internal) ===

## Key Phlex facts (reference/phlex v0.3.0 + devel packages)

- Phlex 0.3.0 has NO first-class "resource" type yet. ddm-999 tracks the awaited
  resources API. TODAY the de-facto resource pattern is: a PROVIDER node emits a
  JOB-LAYER product once, and transforms downstream take it as an extra const
  input. Precedent: wire_schema_source.cpp emits `WireSchema { WireCell::
  WireSchema::Store store; }` at the job layer; frame_filter.cpp consumes
  `(WireSchema const&, Frame const&)`. So "geometry resource" == job-layer const
  product, delivered on every call to the consuming transform.
- Phlex is concurrent; a job-layer product is shared across many invocations and
  threads => it must be genuinely const and safe for concurrent READS.
- Products are C++20 aggregates (often thin pointer wrappers). A geometry
  resource would be e.g. `struct Geometry { std::shared_ptr<const Impl> ptr; }`.

## Object-specific thread/const hazards

- ROOT TGeoManager: relies on the process-global `gGeoManager` singleton and
  keeps MUTABLE navigation state. Topology/material queries on a fully-built,
  never-mutated manager are fine to share read-only, BUT navigation (point/track
  location) is NOT thread-safe unless per-thread navigators are used
  (TGeoManager::AddNavigator / thread-local GetCurrentNavigator). Sharing one
  TGeoManager as a const resource is OK for static queries; concurrent navigation
  needs per-thread navigators.
- Geant4 geometry: owned by the run manager, bound to thread-local navigators
  (MT). It is NOT a passable const object — it is built inside a
  G4VUserDetectorConstruction. Therefore G4 geometry CANNOT itself be the shared
  resource; the edep-sim node must BUILD its own G4 geometry internally. The
  resource's role for the sim node is to IDENTIFY/PROVIDE the source (GDML/spec),
  not to hand over a live G4 object.

## Options

OPT-A: Resource carries the live in-memory geometry object (TGeoManager or DD4hep
Detector), const + shared; consumers do read-only queries; navigation via
per-thread navigators. edep-sim still builds its OWN G4 geometry from the same
source.
  + Non-sim (reco) nodes get a ready shared geometry, zero re-parse.
  + Matches the WireSchema job-layer-product precedent directly.
  - gGeoManager global-singleton tension; only one TGeoManager per process.
  - Navigation not concurrency-safe without per-thread navigators (footgun).
  - Does nothing for G4 (sim must rebuild anyway) => object is only half-useful.

OPT-B: Resource is an opaque geometry SPEC/handle (identity + loader params:
GDML bytes/path + world-volume name + units), NOT a live geometry. Each consumer
builds the representation it needs (G4 node builds G4 geom; reco builds TGeo).
  + Sidesteps shared-mutable-global and singleton issues entirely.
  + Symmetric: every consumer owns its representation, clean lifetimes.
  + Trivially const/thread-safe (immutable bytes + identity).
  - Duplication / re-parse cost; two+ in-memory copies (the very thing Q1 asks
    to avoid) — but see Q1: two copies may be unavoidable for G4 anyway.
  - No shared reco geometry object; each reco node re-parses.

OPT-C (recommended): Hybrid resource = authoritative immutable SPEC (as in B)
that ALSO lazily materializes and caches a single const TGeo (or DD4hep) VIEW for
non-sim consumers. edep-sim uses the spec to drive its internal G4 build
(/edep/gdml/read or DDG4); reco nodes share the cached const view (per-thread
navigators for navigation).
  + Immutable identity/bytes => trivially safe to share; view built once, cached.
  + G4 sim gets what it actually needs (a spec to build from); reco gets a shared
    object => avoids N re-parses without pretending G4 can share a live object.
  + Cleanly accommodates the DD4hep single-source path from Q1 (spec = compact
    description; view = DD4hep Detector/TGeo; G4 = DDG4).
  - Slightly more machinery; must decide who owns/caches the view (the resource
    provider node) and enforce read-only navigation discipline.

## Ranking

C > B > A.  Rationale: Geant4 fundamentally cannot consume a shared live
geometry object for tracking, so the resource's essential job is to be an
immutable, shareable SPEC/identity; adding a cached const TGeo view (C) recovers
A's benefit for reco without A's singleton/navigation hazards.

## Cross-cutting notes / dependencies

- Tightly coupled to Q1 (what the spec/view actually is: raw GDML vs DD4hep).
- BLOCKED-ish on ddm-999: the real Phlex resources API may impose its own
  const/thread contract; until it lands, use the provider→job-layer-product
  pattern (WireSchema precedent). Confirm ddm-999 status before planning.
- Whatever is chosen, document the navigation-thread-safety contract explicitly
  (per-thread TGeo navigators) as part of the resource's API.
=== DECISION (2026-07-02, from edep-sim-phlex.org) ===
- Given Q1 (edep-sim builds its own G4 geom; no TGeo), this question is now
  relevant ONLY to TGeo consumers (not to the G4 sim path).
- We DELAY implementing a TGeo Phlex "resource".

Consequence: the Phlex resources-API confirmation (ddm-4nd.8) is NO LONGER
gating the edep-sim node; it is deferred with the TGeo resource. Revisit both when
a TGeo (or DD4hep, ddm-dxp) reco-geometry resource is picked up.
close_reason: Closed

title: Q1: How to provide detector geometry (G4 + TGeo from one source)?
description:
## Question

How should detector geometry be provided to the edep-sim node and shared with
dissimilar downstream Phlex nodes that need different representations?

## Context

- GDML XML is the de-facto at-rest standard.
- Geant4 must build its own geometry (G4VPhysicalVolume tree).
- Non-Geant4 (reconstruction) code typically depends on ROOT TGeo (TGeoManager).
- Reading the same GDML into both G4 and TGeo yields two in-memory copies of the
  same information. Is there a better option?
- edep-sim reads GDML via `/edep/gdml/read` (G4GDMLParser) and also builds a
  TGeoManager for its ROOT output (EDepSimRootGeometryManager). So edep-sim
  itself already holds both representations.
- Geometry is delivered to Phlex nodes as a const, shared "resource" (see Q2).

## Sub-questions / candidate directions (to research)

1. Two-copies from one GDML (baseline): G4GDMLParser for G4 + TGDMLParse/TGeo for
   the rest. Simple, lossy? memory cost.
2. DD4hep: single source of truth (compact XML/C++), TGeo as the native in-memory
   model, DDG4 producing the Geant4 geometry. Does it give both without a second
   parse? Adoption/fit for DUNE LArTPC.
3. VGM (Virtual Geometry Model) to convert G4<->TGeo in memory.
4. What DUNE/LArSoft do today, and any DD4hep-for-DUNE direction.

## Deliverable

Ranked options with pros/cons; a recorded decision. Depends conceptually on Q2.

Cross-ref: ddm-999 (Phlex resources API), ddm-3j8.1.13/.14 (resource precedent).
notes:
=== RESEARCH FINDINGS — options, pros/cons, ranking (web-cited + code-verified) ===

## Grounding facts

- CURRENT PRACTICE (all "GDML single at-rest source -> two in-memory copies"):
  * LArSoft: GDML -> ROOT TGeoManager (geo::GeometryCore) as its in-memory model,
    PLUS a separate G4GDMLParser::Read() to feed Geant4. [HIGH]
  * edep-sim: reads GDML into Geant4 (G4GDMLParser in DetectorConstruction), then
    EDepSim::RootGeometryManager TRANSLATES the built G4 tree into a TGeoManager
    (gGeoManager) for its ROOT output. So it's G4 first, then a G4->TGeo
    translation (VGM-style), verified in src/EDepSimRootGeometryManager.cc. [HIGH]
  * DUNE ND geometry is generated by dunendggd (built on gegede/GGD — our own
    project) emitting GDML. We control GDML generation. [HIGH]
- DD4hep: the ONLY mainstream true single-source-of-truth in memory — native ROOT
  TGeo model, DDG4 converts to Geant4 on demand, DDRec lets non-G4 reco use the
  TGeo model directly (ACTS does this). Adopted by CMS/LHCb/EIC-ePIC/FCC via
  Key4hep. Actively maintained (v01-37, 2026-04). BUT: NO evidence of DUNE
  adoption; would require re-expressing DUNE geometry in DD4hep compact/C++
  (migration away from gegede/GDML). [HIGH; DUNE-negative UNVERIFIED]
- VGM: in-memory G4<->TGeo conversion, no file round-trip. Caveats: cannot convert
  Geant4 parameterised volumes; on unsupported "exotic" solids it SILENTLY
  replaces them with a small box; single maintainer (v5-4, 2025-12). [HIGH]
- GDML round-trip is lossy at the edges and VERSION-SENSITIVE: ROOT approximates
  <ellipsoid>->BBox, <elcone>->Cone; <paramvol> is Geant4-only and ROOT silently
  drops it; NIST material names resolve in G4 but NOT in ROOT; reflections/names/
  default units (cm/deg vs mm/rad) diverge. Note: parametrised volumes are a G4
  C++ construct, not GDML, so GGD/GDML-sourced DUNE geometry likely avoids that
  particular gap — but ellipsoid/elcone/materials/units still apply. [HIGH]

## Options

OPT-A (baseline): GDML stays the single at-rest source; each consumer builds its
own in-memory copy independently (G4 via G4GDMLParser; reco via ROOT TGDMLParse).
Two copies.
  + Exactly what DUNE/LArSoft/edep-sim do now; lowest risk; we already produce the
    GDML (dunendggd).
  + Two copies for G4 are unavoidable anyway (G4 geometry is built inside the run
    manager; see Q2).
  - Duplicated parse; the well-known GDML edge-lossiness/version sensitivity
    applies to the independent TGeo parse.

OPT-D (recommended NOW; = A aligned to the Q2 resource design): GDML remains the
at-rest single source and is delivered as the geometry RESOURCE SPEC (Q2 OPT-C).
The edep-sim node builds its own G4 geometry from the GDML internally; downstream
reco nodes share ONE const TGeo view built once from the same GDML.
  + Same low risk as A but fits the Phlex resource model; reco nodes share one
    TGeo view instead of N re-parses.
  + Clean separation of "source spec" (shared, immutable) from per-consumer
    representation.
  - Still two representations (G4 built in-sim + shared TGeo); accepts GDML edge
    lossiness. Mitigate with a geometry-consistency validation test (compare G4 vs
    TGeo volume/material counts) and pinned ROOT/G4 versions.

OPT-C (strategic, separate track): Adopt DD4hep as the single source of truth
(native TGeo + DDG4->G4 + DDRec for reco).
  + Genuinely one in-memory model; eliminates the two-copy problem; strong
    long-term ecosystem (Key4hep) and non-G4 reco story (DDRec/ACTS).
  - Large adoption cost: re-express DUNE geometry in DD4hep, migrate off
    gegede/GDML; no DUNE precedent; scope far beyond the edep-sim node. Should be
    its OWN decision/spike, not a blocker here.

OPT-B (fallback optimization): Build G4 from GDML and derive TGeo by in-memory
G4->TGeo translation (edep-sim's RootGeometryManager, or VGM) — one parse + one
translation, no second GDML parse.
  + Avoids the independent second GDML parse; edep-sim ALREADY produces a
    TGeoManager this way, so a sim run yields the TGeo essentially for free.
  - Ties the reco geometry to having run the sim / to G4 being present; VGM's
    silent-box lossiness + parametrised-volume gap; couples reco to sim lifecycle.

## Ranking

D (recommended now) ~ A > C (strategic, evaluate separately) > B (only if the
independent TGeo parse proves problematic).

Rationale: GDML is already DUNE's single at-rest source and we generate it; two
in-memory copies are unavoidable for G4 regardless; D gets the Phlex-resource
benefits (shared TGeo view) at baseline risk. DD4hep is the "right" long-term
answer to the two-copy problem but is a separate, large migration decision that
must not gate the edep-sim node.

## Follow-ups / dependencies

- Coupled to Q2 (resource = GDML spec + cached const TGeo view).
- Recommend a separate DECISION/SPIKE issue to evaluate DD4hep for DUNE (out of
  scope for the edep-sim node; strategic).
- Add a geometry-consistency validation test; pin ROOT+G4 versions (GDML export
  bug status is version-dependent).

Sources: DD4hep manual + AIDASoft/DD4hep; VGM (vmc-project) + EPJ Web Conf 245
02014 (2020); ROOT TGDMLParse/TGDMLWrite docs; GDML User's Guide §3.5.3;
LArSoft geometry docs; edep-sim README + src/EDepSimRootGeometryManager.cc.
=== DECISION (2026-07-02, from edep-sim-phlex.org) ===
- edep-sim generates ONLY its own G4 geometry, directly from a given GDML file.
- edep-sim is made to NOT generate any ROOT TGeo.

Reconciliation with the research options: this selects a simpler path than the
ranked OPT-D (GDML spec + shared const TGeo view) — the shared-TGeo-view for reco
is dropped for now (see Q2: TGeo resource deferred).

Technical connection to Q5: edep-sim builds its TGeoManager ONLY to write the
ROOT output (EDepSimRootGeometryManager / RootPersistencyManager.cc:103-109 build
+ write gGeoManager). So "no ROOT TGeo" is achieved by the SAME mechanism as Q5's
"disable ROOT output": use our custom Phlex-bridging persistency manager and never
instantiate RootPersistencyManager / open a ROOT file => no TGeo is ever built.

The DD4hep single-source evaluation (ddm-dxp) remains orthogonal and open as the
long-term option; this decision does not close it.
=== IMPLEMENTATION FINDING (2026-07-06): base persistency does NOT suppress TGeo ===
The Q1 decision assumed "disable ROOT output => no TGeo built". FALSE in practice:
during `/edep/update`, edep-sim's geometry path builds a TGeoManager
(EDepSim::RootGeometryManager -> "Update ROOT Geometry / TGeoManager created")
REGARDLESS of which persistency manager is installed. Observed in the smoke test
(ddm-4nd.11) even with the base (no-ROOT-output) SummaryPersistency: gGeoManager
is still created, and it emits benign "X not found in /" warnings for volume names
in the edepsim-defaults macro (cryo/drift) not present in example.gdml.
=> Achieving Q1's "edep-sim generates NO ROOT TGeo" needs a distinct mechanism
(suppress the ROOT-geometry update step / a build flag), not just avoiding ROOT
output. Not blocking (TGeo build is harmless for now); tracked for the geometry work.
=== TGeo trigger pinned down (2026-07-06): NOT the persistency manager ===
The TGeoManager is built by EDepSim::UserDetectorConstruction (src/
EDepSimUserDetectorConstruction.cc:230: RootGeometryManager::Get()->Update(
fPhysicalWorld,...)) during geometry construction (/edep/update -> /run/initialize
-> Construct). It is INDEPENDENT of the persistency manager. => Supplying our own
G4VPersistencyManager (replacing EDepSim::PersistencyManager) would NOT suppress
the TGeo build. To achieve Q1's "no ROOT TGeo" we must bypass that call: either
(a) use our own G4VUserDetectorConstruction that reads GDML + assigns SDs without
calling RootGeometryManager, or (b) patch edep-sim to make the Update call
optional (upstream). Caveat: SD assignment (SensDet aux tags -> segment/photon
SDs) lives in the SAME UserDetectorConstruction, so option (a) must replicate it.
Not blocking (TGeo build is harmless at runtime); revisit with the geometry work.
close_reason: Closed

title: edep-sim as a Phlex data-flow node
description:
Establish the packages and data-model norms needed to run the edep-sim
Geant4-based energy-deposition simulation as a Phlex data-flow node.

## Vision

A central "edep-sim node" wraps the edep-sim/Geant4 core so it participates in a
Phlex dataflow graph like any other node.

Configuration:
1. One-time Geant4 "macro" (file or text) → edep-sim core (physics processes, etc.).
2. One-time Phlex-level node config (points at the G4 macro, meta-parameters
   describing the nature of the output).

Per-execution inputs:
1. Primary particle kinematics for Geant4 to track (object, not necessarily a file).
2. Detector geometry, delivered as a Phlex "resource" (const, shared, long-lived).

Per-execution outputs:
1. Products of tracking (energy deposits / ionization, optical photons / PE, ...).
2. Output produced by "user hooks" (truth info associated with observables).

"User hooks" = functions in plugins handed to edep-sim core so it can call them
at points in the Geant4 tracking lifecycle (G4 user actions + edep-sim's own
extra hooks) to collect additional information.

## Phase plan

RESEARCH → PLANNING → IMPLEMENTATION. We are in RESEARCH.

This epic groups six "decision" (ADR) issues capturing the open research
questions Q1–Q6. Each will accumulate options with pros/cons and a ranking, then
a recorded decision. Planning begins once all six are settled.

## edep-sim architecture facts gathered (reference/edep-sim)

- Config is entirely Geant4-macro-driven. app/edepSim.cc `main()` merely maps CLI
  options to `UI->ApplyCommand(...)` macro strings (e.g. `/edep/gdml/read`,
  `/edep/db/open`, `/edep/update`, `/edep/control edepsim-defaults 1.0`).
- Output data model (io/, ROOT TObject classes, no G4 link needed to read):
  TG4Event { RunId, SubrunId, EventId, Primaries[TG4PrimaryVertex],
  Trajectories[TG4Trajectory], SegmentDetectors: map<name,[TG4HitSegment]>,
  PhotonDetectors: map<name,[TG4PhotonHit]> }.
- Output extraction hook: EDepSim::PersistencyManager (a G4VPersistencyManager
  singleton). `Store(const G4Event*)` calls UpdateSummaries() → fills TG4Event
  fEventSummary; `GetEventSummary()` returns `const TG4Event&`. A custom subclass
  replacing RootPersistencyManager is the natural bridge to a Phlex product.
- Kinematics injection hook: external generator plugin, C symbol
  `EDepSim::VKinematicsGenerator* CreateKinematicsGenerator(const char*)`;
  `GeneratePrimaryVertex(G4Event*, pos)` adds G4PrimaryVertex/G4PrimaryParticle.
  Selected via `/generator/kinematics/external/loadGenerator` + `.../set external`.
- User-hook injection: `/edep/actions/loadUser{RunAction,EventAction,TrackAction,
  StepAction,UpdateGeometry}` load C-symbol factories returning G4User*Action
  (or the UserUpdateGeometryAction) pointers, before `/edep/update`.
- edepsim_io library depends only on ROOT (readable without Geant4).

## Related existing issues

- ddm-999 Await Phlex resources API (geometry/channel-map delivery depends on it).
- ddm-3j8.1.13 Channel map as parse-once queryable shared object (resource pattern precedent).
- ddm-3j8.1.14 Deliver channel map via Phlex job config (resource node precedent).
notes:
=== RESEARCH-PHASE CONSENSUS SNAPSHOT (2026-07-02, from edep-sim-phlex.org) ===
DECIDED (closed): Q1 (G4-only geom from GDML, no ROOT/TGeo), Q2 (defer TGeo
resource), Q3 (HepMC3 GenEvent + NuHepMC as node input; Arrow converters future),
Q4 (G4 macros as the config control-language, categorized by lifecycle, node
reserves+validates), Q5 (3-layer output: HepMC truth / Arrow observables(N_e) /
Arrow associations; disable edep-sim ROOT output).
OPEN: Q6 (user hooks) — last open original question; Q7/ddm-4nd.9 (node<->generator
wiring) — new.
FOLLOW-ONS: ddm-4nd.10 (package/dependency factoring), ddm-4nd.7 (kinematics pkg,
Arrow bridge now future), ddm-4nd.8 (resources API, deferred with TGeo), ddm-dxp
(DD4hep, orthogonal), ddm-q3y (audit single-track-id assumption in existing code).
Planning phase begins once Q6 and Q7 settle.
=== SCOPE ADDITION (2026-07-06): three node variants, shared backend ===
Q7 decided: approach 4-A (ddm-4nd.9 closed). The "edep-sim Phlex node" is now
THREE variants differing only in FRONTEND (kinematics handling), sharing a BACKEND:
- FUNCTION (ddm-4nd.11): GenEvents in -> tracking out (transform; approach 4-A).
- GENERATOR (ddm-4nd.12): source; kinematics from G4-internal gun/GPS via macro.
- MIXING (ddm-4nd.13): source; multi-stream file reading + sliding time-window
  mixing/batching (research G4+edep-sim support first).
- BACKEND factoring decision (ddm-4nd.14): if/how to share the common backend;
  blocks .12/.13; informed by .11.
=== ARCHITECTURE SIMPLIFICATION (2026-07-06, Brett) ===
Collapse the three edep-sim node variants to ONE. Mixing and particle-gun/generator
concerns move to standalone, NON-edep-sim UPSTREAM nodes emitting HepMC3 GenEvents:
- ddm-4nd.11 FUNCTION node = the single edep-sim node (GenEvent in -> tracking out).
- ddm-4nd.12 = standalone particle-gun kinematics node (non-edep-sim).
- ddm-4nd.13 = standalone HepMC3 mixing node (non-edep-sim, upstream); mixing done
  in HepMC3-land, so Geant4 mixing support is largely moot.
- ddm-4nd.14 (multi-variant backend factoring) CLOSED as moot.
Isolates non-official-Phlex file I/O inside the mixing node. Moving to implementation.

title: phlex packaging: install phlex/driver.hpp (blocks custom drivers)
description:
phlex v0.3.0 does NOT install phlex/driver.hpp. Its own phlex/CMakeLists.txt line 37 installs only 'concurrency.hpp module.hpp source.hpp' (+ detail/plugin_macros.hpp, configuration.hpp) — driver.hpp is omitted. Yet PHLEX_REGISTER_DRIVER is listed as an intended-public API in the v0.3.0 release notes, and ALL of driver.hpp's dependencies ARE installed (configuration.hpp, core/source.hpp, detail/plugin_macros.hpp, metaprogramming/type_deduction.hpp, model/fixed_hierarchy.hpp, utilities/resumable_driver.hpp). So an out-of-tree custom driver plugin cannot #include "phlex/driver.hpp".

Confirmed: grep of extern/envs/gcc15/view/include finds PHLEX_REGISTER_DRIVER/driver_proxy in NO installed header; not present anywhere under the phlex-0.3.0 install prefix.

FIX OPTIONS:
 1. Patch the phlex Spack recipe / phlex CMakeLists to add driver.hpp (line 37 install FILES) and reinstall phlex (header-only change, but Spack rebuilds phlex -> slow). Proper.
 2. Vendor a verbatim copy of the v0.3.0 phlex/driver.hpp into our tree as a documented stopgap (all deps installed, so it compiles now); remove when upstream ships. Fast.
 3. Raise upstream to add driver.hpp to the install list; defer ddm-c3s.16.

Blocks ddm-c3s.16.
notes: STOPGAP IN PLACE (2026-07-01): a verbatim v0.3.0 phlex/driver.hpp is vendored at devel/phlex-arrow-hdf/vendor/phlex/driver.hpp and used only when the installed header is absent (CMake find_file guard). ddm-c3s.16 is unblocked/done via this. Remaining scope here: get phlex to install driver.hpp upstream (raise issue / patch the phlex Spack recipe), then delete the vendored copy -- the guard makes that transition automatic.

title: phlex-arrow-hdf: adopt phlex::source for the read path (retire PHLEX_REGISTER_PROVIDERS)
description:
Adopt Phlex 0.3.0's OFFICIAL source model for HDF5 Arrow input, replacing the 0.2.0-style PHLEX_REGISTER_PROVIDERS + m.provide(lambda).output_product(...) in phlex-arrow-hdf/SourceModule.cpp. Verified against reference/phlex @ v0.3 (core/source.hpp, driver.hpp, form/form_source.cpp, test/provider_test.cpp).

MODEL (facts):
- phlex::source is a SANCTIONED public alias: 'namespace phlex { using source = detail::source; }' (core/source.hpp). Two pure virtuals:
  * provider_bundles create_providers(phlex::product_selector const&): implicit-provider FACTORY. The framework (make_computational_edges::find_matching_implicit_providers) calls it on every registered source with each downstream input product_selector and appends the returned bundles, wiring providers into the graph automatically. Return one provider_bundle per product this source can supply that matches the selector.
  * phlex::index_generator indices(): coroutine yielding data_cell_index_ptr for the cells the source knows (co_yield).
- provider_bundle{ std::function<product_ptr(data_cell_index const&)> provider_function; concurrency max_concurrency; product_specification spec; std::string layer; std::string stage; } (phlex::detail).
- Registered via PHLEX_REGISTER_SOURCE(s,config){ ...; s.add_source<ArrowHdfSource>(name, args...); }; config under the job 'sources' section.

IMPLEMENTATION (phlex-arrow-hdf):
- New ArrowHdfSource : public phlex::source. Ctor opens the HDF5 file ONCE and runs arrow_hdf Hdf5File::scan() -> phlex_arrow::CellHierarchy::from_addresses (c3s.15) -> cells + products present. OWNS this shared open-file+scan (this is the natural owner that resolves c3s.10).
  * create_providers(selector): for each (creator, product, type) held that matches selector.match(spec, layer, stage), push provider_bundle whose provider_function reads the TableGroup for a given cell (reuse ArrowHdfInput::read()) wrapped via phlex::detail::product_for; spec = product_specification{algorithm_name(output_creator), identifier{product}, type_id of TableGroup}.
  * indices(): co_yield CellHierarchy cells as data_cell_index_ptr.
  * const accessors hierarchy()/cells() so the file-driven DRIVER (c3s.16), which receives the SAME source instance, drives exactly the discovered cells.
- Retire SourceModule.cpp PHLEX_REGISTER_PROVIDERS path.

VERBOTEN-FOOTPRINT EFFECT: adopts official extension points (phlex::source, PHLEX_REGISTER_SOURCE, index_generator = public) and RETIRES the informal provider builder (m.provide(...).output_product(...) = phlex::detail provider_api + the experimental::identifier{} wrapping of layer/suffix). Residual verboten types (provider_bundle, product_specification, product_ptr, algorithm_name, identifier) are the UNAVOIDABLE source ABI -- even Phlex's own FORM source and unit tests spell phlex::detail::provider_bundle -- so consolidate them into ArrowHdfSource + the existing PhlexTypes.hpp aliases: the read path then touches verboten only at this one sanctioned boundary.

ACCEPTANCE: source registered via PHLEX_REGISTER_SOURCE + add_source supplies TableGroup products for the cells a driver yields; the existing single-cell read-back test passes through the new source path.
close_reason: Closed

title: [epic] Fix umbrella devel/ code for Phlex 0.3.0 (compile + tests)
description:
Phlex 0.3.0 (v0.3.0, PR #679 'Rationalize namespaces' + #616/#602/#620/#624/#637/#658) changes symbols inside the verboten namespaces experimental/internal/detail that our devel/ packages reach into, breaking the build. Goal: get devel/ compiling and tests passing against 0.3.0 before starting the newly-unblocked issues.

KEY FINDING for review: 0.3.0 did NOT promote our core Phlex types to the sanctioned top-level 'phlex' namespace. 'Rationalize namespaces' merely SHUFFLED them between experimental and detail:
  - still phlex::experimental: product_store, algorithm_name, identifier
  - moved to phlex::detail: product_specification, type_id/make_type_id, app run()/version()
  - product_query -> phlex::product_selector IS now top-level (sanctioned).
So we CANNOT fully remove verboten-namespace dependencies without losing features. Per guidance, we adapt to the new locations and keep them localized. Proposed global solution: keep+extend the existing devel/phlex-arrow-common/.../PhlexTypes.hpp aliasing pattern to also cover the detail-namespace types, confining the verboten surface to one header per package. Relates to ddm-4or.3 (which assumed 0.3.0 might stabilize the model API -- it does not yet).

Ground truth from standalone umbrella builds against the updated gcc15 view. Child tasks enumerate each concrete break.
close_reason: Closed

title: dune-config: SP input swap simulation <-> raw DUNE-DAQ files

Wire the daq-hdf provider through the registries but mark it gated: the actual phlex DAQ source (decode + provide + channel-map resource) is blocked on Phlex 0.3.0 source/driver and resources APIs. Coordinate with the DAQ-input epic.

Confirms the live/gated split concretely.
19/19 ctests PASS.
LIVE RUN DEFERRED to ddm-4pz.12: a full PDHD sim-sigproc run needs wire-cell-data PDHD files that are ABSENT from the xerosere view -- MISSING: np04hd-garfield-6paths-mcmc-bestfit.json.bz2 (APA0 field), protodunehd-field-response-filters.json.bz2 (APA0), protodunehd-noise-spectra-14mVfC-v1.json.bz2 (noise, needed by AddNoise even for APA1-3); dune-garfield-1d565.json.bz2 is in extern/spack wire-cell-data + gcc15 view but not the xerosere view; protodunehd-wires-larsoft-v1.json.bz2 IS in the xerosere view. title: dune-config: phlex workflow builders parameterized by {detname,variant,in_kind,out_kind}
description: Port cfg/dune/phlex/job/*.jsonnet to workflow builders that take detname+variant and select the WCT job-config plus input/output kinds. Mirror the WCT I/O-ends registry at the phlex layer (phlex/sources.jsonnet, sinks.jsonnet keyed by kind). The phlex objects reference (do not duplicate) the WCT job-config file they feed. Preserve the params-file selection mechanism documented in docs/dune-config.md.
notes:
DONE. phlex workflow builder + source/sink registries (phlex-layer mirror of the WCT ends).
- cfg/dune/phlex/sources.jsonnet: keyed by input kind {deposet-file, frame-file, daq-hdf(gated stub)}; each has product:: + make(filename) -> phlex source module spec. wct_config names point at GENERIC wire-cell-phlex file-IO graphs (resolved at runtime), not dune configs.
- cfg/dune/phlex/sinks.jsonnet: keyed by output kind {frame-file, deposet-file}; product:: + make(filename, input_from).
- cfg/dune/phlex/workflow.jsonnet: function(detname, wct_job, in_kind, out_kind, in_file, out_file, variant="ideal", anode_index=0, nevents, wct_log_*). Builds {driver=generate_layers, sources:{source}, modules:{wct, sink}}. Executor cpp picked from (src.product -> snk.product): deposet->frame=wcp_deposet_to_frame, frame->frame=wcp_frame_filter (asserts a clear error for unsupported flows). The WCT body is only REFERENCED by filename (dune/wct/job/<wct_job>.jsonnet); detname/variant/anode_index passed as wct_tla strings. sink.input_from = executor instance ("wct"), per phlex creator=instance-name routing.
- Removed stale phlex/job/ placeholder; added cfg/dune/phlex/README.md (builder usage + SP-input-swap mapping table + phlex/phlexed TLA note).
KEY: variant is now plumbed end-to-end (job TLA -> detector overlay). The 4 job-level input kinds map to phlex sources here; "sim" SP-job = deposet-file source + sim-sigproc body.
Self-contained: workflow.jsonnet is pure data (no wirecell import), so test/phlex-test.jsonnet runs with -J cfg only. Verified structure for sim->SP (deposet_source+deposet_to_frame+sim-sigproc), frame->SP (frame_source+frame_filter+sigproc), variant=real reaches TLA, daq-hdf assembles (gated module), anode/nevents/log knobs, gated marker, deposet sink registry. ctest dune_config_phlex. 18/18 ctests PASS.
Sets up ddm-4pz.10 (the swap: sim live, daq-hdf gated) and ddm-4pz.12 (integration test).

title: dune-config: swappable I/O ends registries (sim, frame-file, deposet-file, daq-hdf stub)
description: Introduce input/output "ends" as port-bearing pnodes selected from registries (wct/inputs.jsonnet, wct/outputs.jsonnet) keyed by kind: sim (drift+depotransform producing frames), frame-file, deposet-file, and a daq-hdf stub. A job becomes pipeline([inputs[in_kind], body, outputs[out_kind]]). This is the seam that makes SP-input a parameter rather than a new job file.
notes:
DONE. Swappable I/O ends + bodies registries.
- cfg/dune/wct/lib/ends.jsonnet: inputs (deposet-file, frame-file, daq-hdf STUB, sim composite), outputs (frame-file, deposet-file), bodies (sim, sigproc, splat). Each returns {pnodes:[ordered stage pnodes], extra:[non-wired services]}. daq-hdf is a WCT-level frame-boundary stub; decode+drive live in the future phlex DAQ source (gated, ddm-3j8.1.6/ddm-4pz.10).

KEY INSIGHT (documented in ends.jsonnet): at the WCT graph there are only TWO input boundary types (deposet, frame); the 4 job-level input KINDS collapse onto them -- file/sim/daq is a PHLEX-source concern (ddm-4pz.9). "sim" is a composite end (deposet boundary + drift/sim/digitize) producing frames.
DESIGN NOTE: ends expose a flat pnode LIST (not one composite pnode) so the job is a single flat pipeline -> edges emerge in source->sink order, byte-identical to the originals (a nested composite reordered the Pgrapher edge list - set-equal but order-different; flat list avoids that).
PARITY PRESERVED THROUGH REFACTOR: all 10 goldens PASS; wire-cell-phlex cross-check still exact (sim/sigproc/splat, pdhd/0+pdvd/0+pdvd/4).
SWAP DEMONSTRATED: test/ends-test.jsonnet asserts SP job assembled with in_kind=sim == the sim-sigproc job, in_kind=frame-file == the sigproc job, in_kind=daq-hdf == frame-file (stub) -- the seam reproduces the dedicated builders exactly. ctest dune_config_ends. 17/17 ctests PASS.

title: dune-config: WCT sim/sigproc/splat subgraphs as pnodes; sim-sigproc via pipeline
description: Port cfg/dune/wct/job/{sim,sigproc,sim-sigproc,splat}.jsonnet to pnode subgraph builders (per single anode) consuming a resolved detector description. Express sim-sigproc as pg.pipeline([sim, sigproc]) sharing services, eliminating the hand-duplicated combined file. Keep the elec-type dispatch (ColdElecResponse/JsonElecResponse) and optional FilterResponse/filter_response handling.
notes:
DONE. Ported sim/sigproc/sim-sigproc/splat to pnode subgraphs.
- cfg/dune/wct/job/parts.jsonnet: single inode factory parts(detector,anode_index,service_prefix,variant) -> all WCT components (dft,rng,wires,fr,elec[poly],anode,pirs,drifter,setdrifter,transform,reframer,noise_model,addnoise,digitizer,splat,sigproc[+filter_response],sp_filters) + timing. Unifies on the NEWER sim.jsonnet semantics (DepoTransform carries rng; PIR overall_short_padding = 2*response_plane/drift_speed) -- the old hand-written sim-sigproc.jsonnet had drifted to older values (no rng, 0.1ms padding); that divergence is now structurally impossible.
- cfg/dune/wct/job/{sim,sigproc,sim-sigproc,splat}.jsonnet: thin builders. Wrap parts inodes via lib/graph.jsonnet source()/filter()/sink(), compose with pg.pipeline, finalize with application(graph, extra=services). sim-sigproc = pg.pipeline([src, setdrifter, transform, reframer, addnoise, digitizer, sigproc, snk]) -- the 322-line combined file is gone. Job fns keep the original TLA signature + a new variant="ideal".
PARITY (order-independent, by type:name + deep data, the .11 method): new sim/sigproc/splat are CONTENT-IDENTICAL to the wire-cell-phlex originals for pdhd/0, pdvd/0, and pdvd/4 (JsonElecResponse top-drift path).
sim-sigproc composition verified: edge chain = DepoSetBoundarySource->DepoSetDrifter->DepoTransform->Reframer->AddNoise->Digitizer->OmnibusSigProc->FrameBoundarySink; set == sim + {OmnibusSigProc, 3 FilterResponse, 16 SP filters}; shared services (DFT/AnodePlane/FieldResponse/...) appear exactly ONCE (pg.uses dedup) and are byte-identical to the standalone sim.

Carries forward the splat-window-tracks-overrides divergence noted on .4/.5 (byte-identical under default params).

Implement the variant mechanism: a detector resolves as base + variant-overlay (Jsonnet +) selected by a params.variant field; default variant = ideal/base. title: dune-config: pnode/pgraph composition conventions + helpers

Define project conventions: WCT subgraph builders return pnodes (intern with declared iports/oports) rather than bare component lists + hand-written Pgrapher edges. Provide thin helpers/conventions for: building a single-anode pnode, sharing service components (DFT/Random/wires/FieldResponse/Elec) across composed stages, and capping a composed graph with a Pgrapher app node. Document port conventions. This replaces the bespoke edge lists in cfg/dune/wct/job/*.
