title: boost-config: README + Phlex-node usage guide
description:
Write boost-config/README.md documenting:
 - purpose (config schema-as-struct for Phlex nodes) and the header set
 - Documented<T>: the two forms (cfg.x = 5 and cfg.x.value = 5), nested structs
 - how a Phlex node uses it: take the boost::json::value the node receives and
   do  auto cfg = boost::json::value_to<MyConfig>(jv);  (with the BOOST_CONFIG_JSON
   opt-in macro next to the struct), plus the parse<T>(jv, options) strict entry
 - jsonnet_factory<T>()/jsonnet_factories<T>() and the lower-case file/import
   naming convention; how the emitted .libjsonnet integrates with the suite (goes
   with the go-jsonnet libjsonnet virtual — see project memory jsonnet-engine-virtual)
 - build: part of the devel/ superbuild (auto-discovered), or standalone via
   CMakePresets against the Spack view
Include the two worked examples from the epic. Keep the style/tone of sibling
READMEs (phlex-arrow-common, dune-config).
close_reason: Closed

title: boost-config: type-name utilities + is_config_struct concept (type_name.hpp)
design:
Supporting layer used by both JSON and (especially) Jsonnet.

is_config_struct<S> concept — a reflectable schema struct:
   std::is_aggregate_v<S> && !is_documented_v<S>
   && !is_std_vector_v<S> && !std::is_array_v<S>
   && (boost::pfr is able to reflect it)
Use it to constrain the generic serializer/factory so they only bind to
config structs, never scalars/vectors.

type_name<T>() -> std::string  (used for the `(int)` / `(MyParams)` annotations
in Jsonnet @param comments):
 - scalars via explicit specializations: bool->"bool", int->"int",
   double->"double", float->"float", std::string->"string",
   std::vector<U> -> "array of " + type_name<U>() (or just "array"); pick one,
   document it.
 - struct types (is_config_struct): boost::core::demangle(typeid(T).name())
   then strip leading namespace qualifiers -> "MyParams". This is a RUNTIME
   value (jsonnet_factory is a runtime function), so demangle is acceptable.
 - customization point: allow authors to override by specializing
   config_type_name<T> (falls back to demangle). Needed when demangled name is
   ugly or namespace-stripping is wrong.

jsonnet_module_name<T>() -> std::string : lower-cased type_name (e.g.
"MyParams" -> "myparams"), used for the file name and import identifier stem.
The imported local is bound to the ORIGINAL type_name (local MyParams = import
"myparams.libjsonnet"), so keep both.

Tests: type_name of scalars/vector/struct; module-name lowercasing; concept
accepts MyConfig/MyParams/MyStruct and rejects int/float/std::vector/Documented.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add tests for hepmc-phlex (GenEventGun): no package-local coverage
description:
The hepmc-phlex package (devel/hepmc-phlex/, GenEventGun / hmp_gen_event_gun provider) has NO tests of its own. Its only current coverage is indirect: it is the source node in the edep-sim-phlex smoke tests (run-smoke.sh, run-smoke-hdf.sh), which pull in the full Geant4/edep-sim stack just to exercise a pure phlex+HepMC3 node. A regression in GenEventGun (config parsing/validation, momentum-vs-energy XOR, unit handling, primary construction, by-value GenEvent product) would only surface via that heavy downstream chain, or not at all if the chain is skipped.

Add lightweight package-local tests that exercise GenEventGun directly, WITHOUT Geant4/edep-sim:
- Unit tests of GenEventGun over the phlex configuration: valid configs (energy+direction form and direct momentum form), and fail-fast rejection of invalid/inconsistent configs (missing pdg, both/neither kinematics forms, bad units, etc.).
- Assertions on the emitted HepMC3::GenEvent: number of primaries, status==1, 4-momentum (magnitude/direction), vertex position+time, and unit conversion (MeV/GeV, mm/cm).
- Optionally a minimal phlex workflow: hmp_gen_event_gun -> a tiny HepMC3 observer (would need a small observer module in this package) that checks the product round-trips through phlex.

## Context
Follow the emerging xerosere test conventions rather than adding another ad-hoc run.sh: author under the BATS framework (ddm-4fe) and use host-agnostic, repo-relative paths + XEROSERE_SPACK_ENV instead of hardcoded absolute paths (ddm-iuc) -- the existing edep-sim-phlex run-smoke*.sh scripts bake in /home/bviren/... and builds/envs/gcc15/, which these tests must avoid. Not a blocker for any deliverable; hepmc-phlex is functionally verified via the downstream smoke tests. This is about self-contained, fast, framework-consistent regression coverage.
acceptance_criteria: hepmc-phlex has package-local tests exercising GenEventGun directly (no Geant4), covering valid + invalid configs and the emitted GenEvent contents; tests use the shared BATS + host-agnostic setup (ddm-4fe/ddm-iuc) rather than a bespoke run.sh with absolute paths.

title: Remove host/user-specific paths from xerosere tests (.envrc + XEROSERE_SPACK_ENV + variables doc)
description:
The tests must not bake in host- or user-specific absolute paths. Make them reference only xerosere-relative locations and documented, user-set variables.

Scope:
- Add a tests-area .envrc (direnv) that populates the environment tests rely on: LD_LIBRARY_PATH (incl. the driver OptiX dir, e.g. /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/nvidia/current for libnvoptix.so.1), and any other required vars.
- Mint an env var (e.g. XEROSERE_SPACK_ENV) to name the active Spack environment so tests can locate the xerosere-produced per-env areas -- extern/envs/<env>/view, builds/envs/<env>, installs/envs/<env> -- without hardcoding. Tests derive these from XEROSERE_SPACK_ENV (default gcc15) + the repo root.
- Provide and maintain a document (e.g. tests/README or docs/) listing every variable a user may need to set and why (LD_LIBRARY_PATH additions, XEROSERE_SPACK_ENV, GPU/OptiX prerequisites, EDEP_SIMPHONY_* knobs).
- Audit current tests for stray absolute/user paths (e.g. $HOME, /home/bviren, hardcoded driver dirs) and route them through the .envrc / variables instead.

Enables the BATS migration chore to share a clean, host-agnostic setup.

title: Migrate xerosere-level tests from ad-hoc run.sh to BATS (bats-core)
description:
Replace the hand-rolled tests/<name>/run.sh convention with bats-core (BATS) Bash unit tests.

Scope:
- Adopt bats-core for tests/ (tests/edep-simphony, tests/edep-simphony-cli, and future tests).
- Preserve the current semantics: pass / fail / skip (bats has 'skip'), and the per-test output area builds/envs/<env>/xerosere/<name>/.
- Reconcile with the 'umbrella <env> test' runner: either have umbrella invoke bats, or replace it. Keep 0=pass / non-zero=fail aggregation and a skip that does not fail the suite.
- Decide how bats obtains the Spack env activation and shared helpers (a common setup helper / bats 'load').
- Provide bats-core itself (Spack package, git submodule, or vendored) so it is available in the environment.

Depends conceptually on the path-cleanup chore (shared setup/helpers should be host-agnostic).

title: Q5 associations layer: Arrow tables linking observables to truth track ids
description: Third Q5 layer (ddm-4nd.5 decision). Emit OPTIONAL Arrow association table(s) mapping each observable (segment / photon-hit) to its contributing truth TrackId(s): segments -> Contributors[] + PrimaryId; photons -> PrimaryId. Keyed to the HepMC/trajectory truth (ddm truth layer) by track id. First-class optional Arrow product, NOT squeezed into HepMC; emitted only when truth is requested. Note single-track-id assumption is violated by default fold (see ddm-q3y).

title: Q5 MC-truth layer: emit HepMC GenEvent (primaries + trajectory genealogy)
description: Second Q5 layer (ddm-4nd.5 decision). Add an OPTIONAL MC-truth product from the TG4Event summary: primaries (TG4PrimaryVertex: 4-pos, generator/reaction/xsec/weight, particles[PDG,4-mom,trackid]) + trajectory genealogy (TG4Trajectory: trackid, parentid, PDG, initial 4-mom; points path). Represent as HepMC3::GenEvent (idiomatic, lossless, symmetric with the Q3 kinematics INPUT). Trajectory step-point path is the one truth item HepMC can't type -> optional Arrow side-table. Emitted only when truth is requested (config gate, Q4). Couples to Q3 primaries schema alignment (NuHepMC).

title: Integrate the plugin into the edep-sim Phlex node (run inside edep-sim-phlex, inside Phlex)
description:
Final integration: make the edep-sim Phlex node load the edep-simphony-plugin user actions, so the GPU optical sim runs as part of a Phlex dataflow job.

The plugin loads into standalone edep-sim via Geant4 macro commands (/edep/actions/load*, EXTRAPHYSICS EXTERN mechanism) before /run/initialize. The edep-sim Phlex node (epic ddm-4nd) drives edep-sim's core via macro strings / node config. This task wires the plugin's EXTRAPHYSICS physics constructor and user-action factories (RunAction/EventAction/StepAction/TrackAction) into that node's one-time G4 macro + node configuration, and ensures the OPTICKS_* runtime env is present for the Phlex process.

Open questions to resolve with ddm-4nd design:
- how per-execution the GPU photon output (GPUPhotonHits etc.) surfaces as node outputs vs. staying in edep-sim's ROOT file;
- GPU resource management within a Phlex graph (the CPU blocks during simulate(); one GPU launch per event; no concurrency across the GPU box).

Deliverable: a Phlex job config that runs edep-sim+plugin as a node and produces GPU photon output, plus a test analogous to ddm-p5j.6 but driven through Phlex (GPU-required).

comments.author: Brett Viren
comments.text:
INVESTIGATION (not a test tweak): the plugin cannot surface GPU output inside the phlex node as-is.

- Plugin SimphonyRunAction.cc:284-294 obtains its output TFile via dynamic_cast<EDepSim::RootPersistencyManager*>(GetPersistencyManager())->GetTFile(). On !pm || !pm->IsOpen() it warns and RETURNS without creating GPUPhotonHits (lines 286-291). So all plugin ROOT trees (GPUPhotonHits/Tracks/Steps/CPU*) live only in edep-sim's RootPersistencyManager TFile.
- The edep-sim phlex node (Tracking.cpp:155-158) installs SummaryPersistency, a NO-ROOT-output persistency manager (per ddm-4nd Q1/Q5), not RootPersistencyManager. So the plugin's cast -> nullptr -> GPU trees never created; no GPU photon output surfaces.
- Data-model gap: even if it ran, GPU hits go to the plugin's GPUPhotonHits tree, NOT into TG4Event.PhotonDetectors, so the existing esp_observables converter (which reads PhotonDetectors) would not see them.

So the two 'open questions' in the description are real blockers. Options:
A. Refactor the plugin to expose GPU hits as in-memory data (e.g. from SEvt) via a getter, and add an edep-sim-phlex path/converter that turns them into an Arrow product (proper phlex-native integration; plugin + node work). RECOMMENDED.
B. Give the phlex node a RootPersistencyManager so the plugin's TFile path works -- contradicts the node's no-ROOT design.
C. Have the plugin also fill TG4Event.PhotonDetectors (or the node's summary) so esp_observables picks up GPU hits; still needs plugin + node changes.

Note: CPU optical already works through the phlex node today (tests/edep-simphony: setStackPhotons -> G4 -> TG4Event.PhotonDetectors -> observables.photons). The blocked piece is specifically the GPU/plugin path.

title: Provide GPU test fixtures: LAr optical GDML geometry + run macros
description:
The plugin test needs a geometry and macros. The plugin is geometry-agnostic but requires a GDML whose LAr has proper optical material properties (RINDEX etc.) and whose photon sensors carry a G4 SensitiveDetector (LArTPCSensorIdentifier finds them automatically).

Sources:
- reference/edep-simphony-plugin/macro/ (run_3gev_electron.mac -- despite the name currently a 1 MeV e-; simphony_plugin.mac loads plugin actions via /edep/actions/load*).
- The upstream tests_benchmark repo (github.com/Ningclover/tests_benchmark) provides an example geometry GDML, .mac files, and CPU-vs-GPU driver run.sh modes.

Deliverable: a small, self-contained optical GDML + macro set checked into this repo (or installed by the package) suitable for a short GPU smoke test. Keep the particle gun small (few MeV, few events) so photon counts stay within OPTICKS_MAX_SLOT=M1 for a debug run. Aligns with ddm-qiv.4 (self-contained tests: capture required environment & config files).
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Brett Viren
comments.text:
Fulfilled: committed a LAr optical GDML fixture + run macros used by both edep-simphony tests.
- tests/edep-simphony/geometry/benchmark_small.gdml: a 20 cm LAr-cube variant of the edep-simphony-benchmark optical geometry (LAr scintillator + WLS shells + 100%-efficiency photon-detector shell with edep-sim SurfaceDetector aux). Small enough for fast CPU optical tracking; the DUNE-scale benchmark geometry is GPU-only.
- tests/edep-simphony/geometry/benchmark_ggd/ (gegede generator) + build_small_gdml.sh to regenerate at any scale.
- Run macros: tests/edep-simphony-cli/{cpu,gpu}.mac (1 MeV e- primary; CPU scintillation-stacking / GPU plugin-action variants). Verified: CPU path yields 32181 PhotonDetectors hits / 2 events.

title: edepsim package: carry the hitless-event user-action change needed by the plugin
description:
The plugin only runs on events with no ionisation hits if edep-sim's external-action loop is moved before the 'if (!HCofEvent) return' early exit in src/EDepSimUserEventAction.cc (a documented one-line change; see reference/edep-simphony-plugin/README.md 'edep-sim (one source change)').

Options to evaluate:
1. Get the change upstreamed into ClarkMcGrew/edep-sim so a released version carries it (cleanest).
2. Carry a Spack patch in the edepsim package (extern/repos/edep-simphony-spack/.../packages/edepsim/package.py) applied for the relevant version, ideally behind a variant so the default edepsim used elsewhere in the env is unaffected -- OR confirm the change is harmless in general and apply unconditionally.

Deliverable: an edepsim build the plugin can drive, with the choice recorded. Check whether the current pinned edepsim@4.3.0 in extern/envs/gcc15/spack.yaml already contains this behavior before patching.

title: Remove local phlex transitive-dep workarounds once upstream fixes find_dependency
description:
Upstream phlex bug reported: https://github.com/Framework-R-D/phlex/issues/656
phlexConfig.cmake has an empty find_dependency block while phlexTargets.cmake
references imported targets spdlog::spdlog, TBB::tbb, fmt::fmt. So every
out-of-tree phlex consumer must find_package(TBB/spdlog/fmt) BEFORE
find_package(phlex) or configuration fails ("target not found").

LOCAL WORKAROUND currently in place (remove once #656 ships a fix and the gcc15
view carries the fixed phlexConfig):
- devel/edep-sim-phlex/CMakeLists.txt          (find_package TBB/spdlog/fmt before phlex)
- devel/edep-sim-phlex/cmake/edep_sim_phlexConfig.cmake.in  (matching find_dependency)
- devel/hepmc-phlex/CMakeLists.txt             (same workaround)
- devel/hepmc-phlex/cmake/hepmc_phlexConfig.cmake.in

When upstream is fixed: delete the extra find_package/find_dependency lines (keep
only what THIS package genuinely uses directly), reconfigure, confirm clean build.
Related: ddm-c3s.19 (a separate phlex packaging gap).

title: Decide shared 'backend' factoring for the three edep-sim node variants
description:
The three node variants -- FUNCTION (ddm-4nd.11), GENERATOR (ddm-4nd.12), MIXING
(ddm-4nd.13) -- differ only in FRONTEND (how primary kinematics is obtained/fed).
Their BACKEND can/should be shared:
- run manager + physics-list + geometry (Q1/Q4) setup;
- the Phlex-bridging persistency manager (Q5; no ROOT/TGeo);
- output product marshaling (Q5 observables/associations/truth);
- lifecycle + node-owned /run/beamOn control (Q4) + teardown/ownership (Q7).

Decide IF and HOW to factor this shared backend, e.g. a common base class or
library composed by three thin frontends. Consider that FUNCTION is a Phlex
transform while GENERATOR/MIXING are source/provider nodes -- the backend must
support both driving models (fed-per-call vs self-driven source).

Informed by building the function node first (ddm-4nd.11); this decision BLOCKS
finalizing the generator and mixing variants so they build on the agreed backend.

Deliverable: a factoring design (shared backend + three frontends) + how it maps
onto Phlex node patterns; feeds ddm-4nd.10 (packaging).
notes:
=== RESOLVED / MOOT (2026-07-06, Brett) ===
Generator and mixing concerns are externalized to standalone non-edep-sim nodes
(ddm-4nd.12, .13). That collapses the edep-sim node to a SINGLE FUNCTION node
(ddm-4nd.11), so there is no set of three edep-sim variants whose backend must be
shared -- the function-node body IS the backend. The multi-variant backend
factoring question is moot. Closing. Cross-package factoring remains in ddm-4nd.10.
Reopen if a second genuine edep-sim node variant re-emerges.
close_reason: Closed

title: Standalone kinematics mixing node (non-edep-sim, multi-stream HepMC3, upstream)
description:
A SOURCE-pattern Phlex node that DIRECTLY manages reading primary kinematics from
file(s): multiple INDEPENDENT streams, mixed by sliding a TIME WINDOW across each
stream's time series, sometimes BATCHING kinematics from more than one stream into
a single G4 event (pile-up / overlay / spill).

FRONTEND difference: the node owns multi-stream reading + windowing + cross-stream
batching. BACKEND: shared with the other variants. Depends on the shared-backend
factoring decision.

## RESEARCH FIRST (required before design): G4 + edep-sim mixing support vs gaps

Catalog what already exists so we invent only the delta:
- edep-sim primitives already present:
  * EDepSimCombinationGenerator (kinem/) and `/generator/combine <src> <dest>
    [relative]` (UserPrimaryGeneratorMessenger) -- combine multiple generators.
  * Multiple generators per event via AddGenerator (each adds vertices).
  * fAllowPartialEvents (fill ONE event from multiple interactions until a stream
    is exhausted) + EDepSim::NoMoreEvents handling in GeneratePrimaries.
  * Spill/time generators: EDepSimSpillTimeGenerator/Factory,
    EDepSimFreeTimeGenerator -- assign interaction times within a spill.
  * Examples: inputs/rooTracker-overlay.mac, inputs/muon-burst-10000.mac.
- Geant4 itself: confirm what (if anything) G4 provides for pile-up/overlay
  (generally user-managed: multiple G4PrimaryVertex per G4Event via multiple
  generators); event biasing; whether any built-in multi-file mixer exists.
- GAPS to invent: sliding-window across N independent streams; cross-stream
  batching policy; time-ordering/coincidence; how mixed kinematics are delivered
  (as combined HepMC3 GenEvent(s) via 4-A, or via edep-sim's multi-generator/
  combine path); interplay with NuHepMC per-vertex metadata.

Deliverable: a research note (G4/edep-sim support + gaps), THEN the multi-stream
sliding-window mixing source node.
notes:
=== REFRAME (2026-07-06, Brett): standalone non-edep-sim mixing node, upstream ===
Factor file-based mixing into its OWN Phlex node placed UPSTREAM of the function
node (ddm-4nd.11), independent of edep-sim. It reads/merges multiple independent
kinematics streams (sliding time window, cross-stream batching) and emits combined
HepMC3 GenEvents.
- Removes the need for any "mixing" edep-sim node variant.
- ISOLATES the "cheating" (non-official-Phlex file input) inside this single node,
  which can later migrate to official Phlex input; stays independent of edep-sim.
- RESEARCH REFRAME: mixing now happens in HepMC3-land (merge GenEvents), so "what
  does Geant4 provide for mixing" is largely MOOT. edep-sim's combine/spill/
  partial-event primitives are no longer the mechanism -- keep them only as
  reference for time-assignment/merge semantics. Core research becomes: HepMC3
  GenEvent merging + sliding-window/time-ordering + batching policy + preserving
  NuHepMC per-vertex metadata across the merge.
Retitled to reflect the standalone nature.

title: Standalone particle-gun kinematics node (non-edep-sim, emits HepMC3 GenEvents)
description:
A SOURCE-pattern Phlex node (provider): primary kinematics come from a
Geant4-INTERNAL "gun" generator (G4ParticleGun / G4GeneralParticleSource=GPS),
configured via G4 macro (Q4), NOT from an upstream data product. The node drives
beamOn and emits tracking outputs each call.

FRONTEND difference: no GenEvent input; kinematics are edep-sim's GPS/gun path
(EDepSimGPSKinematicsGenerator / GPSKinematicsFactory) configured through the
`/generator/kinematics/set gps` + `/gps/...` macro stream. => this variant does
NOT use approach 4-A HepMC injection; it uses edep-sim's default CreateGenerator
factory path. BACKEND: shared with the function node (run-manager/geometry/
persistency/marshaling/lifecycle).

Depends on the shared-backend factoring decision.

Acceptance: source node emits Q5 products driven purely by GPS/gun macro config.
notes:
=== REFRAME (2026-07-06, Brett): externalize as a standalone non-edep-sim node ===
Instead of a special "generator" edep-sim node VARIANT, implement particle-gun /
GPS-like features as their OWN (non-edep-sim, no Geant4) Phlex node that emits
HepMC3 GenEvents UPSTREAM of the function node (ddm-4nd.11).
- Removes the need for any edep-sim "generator" variant; the edep-sim node stays a
  single FUNCTION node that consumes a GenEvent (Q3) regardless of source.
- This node depends only on the shared HepMC3 type (ddm-4nd.7), not on G4/edep-sim.
Retitled to reflect the standalone nature.
=== IMPLEMENTED + BUILDS GREEN (2026-07-06) ===
New package devel/hepmc-phlex/ (project hepmc_phlex, namespace hepmc_phlex),
deps: phlex + HepMC3 + Boost ONLY (NO Geant4/edep-sim; verified via ldd).

- hepmc_phlex/Data.hpp : GenEvent { shared_ptr<const HepMC3::GenEvent> event; }
  -- the canonical inter-node HepMC3 kinematics product.
- hepmc_phlex/GenEventGun.{hpp,cpp} : G4ParticleGun analogue emitting a GenEvent.
  Config parsed+VALIDATED in the ctor (fail-fast). Fires `number` identical
  primaries (status=1) from one vertex. Two kinematics forms (XOR): direct
  `momentum` [px,py,pz], or `energy`(kinetic)+`direction`; plus pdg, mass,
  position, time, number, momentum_unit(MeV/GeV), length_unit(mm/cm). Full schema
  + validation rules + Jsonnet example documented in README.md.
- modules/gen_event_gun.cpp : PHLEX_REGISTER_PROVIDERS -> m.provide(
  "hepmc_gen_event_gun", [](data_cell_index)->GenEvent).output_product(
  "input", suffix "genevent", layer). Module lib: hmp_gen_event_gun.
- Same phlex#656 workaround as edep-sim-phlex (find TBB/spdlog/fmt before phlex);
  tracked for removal in ddm-ovy.

Builds: libhepmc_phlex.so + libhmp_gen_event_gun.so, no missing ldd deps.

NEXT (smoke test): align the edep-sim Tracking node onto hepmc_phlex::GenEvent
(edep-sim-phlex would then depend on hepmc-phlex) so GenEventGun -> Tracking
connects. Then a minimal 2-node workflow.
=== REFACTOR: emit HepMC3::GenEvent by value (2026-07-06) ===
Verified phlex's product model (model/products.hpp): a node returns its output BY
VALUE; phlex MOVES it into framework-owned storage (product<T>{T obj;} held via
unique_ptr<product_base>) and hands consumers `T const&`. type_id for a
non-aggregate class -> opaque 0xFF (the static_assert is commented out), so
HepMC3::GenEvent is a valid product type (routed by product_selector name;
typeid is the safety check). The WCT wrapper structs exist for TYPE-DISTINCTION
among structurally-identical shared_ptr<abstract> types, NOT because phlex rejects
shared_ptr.

Change: GenEventGun::operator() now returns HepMC3::GenEvent BY VALUE; removed the
hepmc_phlex::GenEvent wrapper (deleted Data.hpp). The module emits HepMC3::GenEvent
(suffix "genevent"). Rebuilds green. Consumers use HepMC3::GenEvent directly with
NO dependency on this package for the type.
=== CONFIRMED COMPLETE + CLOSED (2026-07-09, Claude) ===
Standalone particle-gun node (devel/hepmc-phlex/) is implemented, builds green (libhepmc_phlex.so + libhmp_gen_event_gun.so, no Geant4/edep-sim deps), and is VERIFIED WORKING end-to-end.

Verification: hmp_gen_event_gun is the SOURCE node in both edep-sim-phlex smoke tests (run-smoke.sh and run-smoke-hdf.sh, both green today). It fires a 1 GeV mu- as a HepMC3::GenEvent that the edep-sim Tracking node consumes and Geant4 tracks into real energy deposition (segments=1580/1505), which then round-trips all the way to HDF5. This exercises the reframed acceptance (emit HepMC3 GenEvents upstream of the function node) fully.

Source matches the notes: PHLEX_REGISTER_PROVIDERS -> m.provide('hepmc_gen_event_gun', ...).output_product('input', suffix, layer) [current phlex experimental::identifier API]; GenEventGun ctor parses+validates config fail-fast; operator() returns HepMC3::GenEvent BY VALUE (no wrapper). Only dep ddm-4nd.14 is closed; no open blockers.

Note: no package-local test in hepmc-phlex itself; the node is covered by the downstream edep-sim-phlex smoke tests, which is the meaningful integration coverage. A standalone HepMC3-observer test would be nice-to-have, not a gap blocking this deliverable.
close_reason: Standalone particle-gun node complete: devel/hepmc-phlex implemented + builds green, emits HepMC3::GenEvent by value with fail-fast config validation, no Geant4 dep. Verified working end-to-end as the source node in both edep-sim-phlex smoke tests (GenEvent -> tracking -> observables -> HDF5, green). Only dependency ddm-4nd.14 closed; no blockers.

title: Audit consumers assuming one track id per edep-sim segment (default folds delta-rays)
description:
DISCOVERED during Q5 discussion. edep-sim's TG4HitSegment is an AGGREGATE: with the
DEFAULT SegmentSD maxSeparation (1mm), delta-ray secondaries are folded into the
parent segment, so segment.Contributors is a MULTI-track list and PrimaryId is a
heuristic ("interesting" ancestor). Any existing downstream code that assumes a
segment/depo has ONE uniquely associated track id is silently wrong under default
config (deposits from multiple tracks attributed to one).

## Do

1. Identify downstream consumers of edep-sim segments/depos that assume single
   track id (drift/response sims, truth-association / ML-truth builders).
2. Decide per-consumer: consume the full Contributors list, or run edep-sim with
   `/edep/hitSeparation <SD> 0 mm` (one track id per segment; delta-rays become
   their own segments; PreInit-only, per-SD) to restore the assumption.
3. Note the physics trade-off: folding delta-rays into the parent is arguably the
   more physical charge picture; splitting gives cleaner truth at more/shorter
   segments. Per-run choice -> structured node param (ddm-4nd.4 addendum).

Cross-ref: ddm-4nd.5 (segment semantics), ddm-4nd.4 (config knobs).

title: Package & dependency factoring for the edep-sim Phlex ecosystem
description:
Cross-cutting decision surfaced across Q3/Q5/Q6/Q7: how to split the new code into
packages so heavy/awkward dependencies stay isolated and the node<->plugin
contracts are clean.

## Components anticipated

- HepMC3 GenEvent type adoption + NuHepMC conventions (Q3).
- HepMC3 -> G4 external kinematics generator .so (deps: edep-sim, G4, HepMC3) (Q3/Q7).
- Node<->plugin channel/"feed" contract lib (minimal: HepMC3 GenEvent + a queue;
  possibly a borrow interface) shared via DT_NEEDED by node + generator (Q7).
- The edep-sim Phlex node (deps: phlex, edep-sim, G4, feed lib, HepMC3).
- Output converters: primaries/trajectories -> HepMC (truth); segments/photons ->
  Arrow (observables, N_e); associations -> Arrow (Q5). Deps: arrow, ROOT?, HepMC3.
- User-hook plugins + their data-out sink convention (Q6), mirroring the feed.

## Tensions / questions

- Keep Arrow (observables/associations converters) OUT of the G4 node package, or
  fold in? Affects whether the node pulls Arrow + G4 together.
- The extern-C plugin ABI + RTLD_LOCAL dlopen (Q7) dictates the shared-contract-lib
  boundary.
- One-G4RunManager/process + serial node (Q7) bounds process-level composition.
- Avoid a dependency diamond among phlex/geant4/hepmc3/edep-sim/arrow/ROOT; define
  the narrow-waist libraries.

## Deliverable

A package decomposition + dependency graph (planning-phase input). Likely revisited
as Q6/Q7 settle.
notes:
=== Q7 approach choice drives this factoring (2026-07-06) ===
If Q7 picks approach 4-A (node constructs+injects the generator): the HepMC->G4
kinematics generator is a NORMAL LINKED class, not a dlopen'd plugin. Then the
NODE package link-depends on edep-sim + G4 + HepMC3 directly (heavier node), and
there is NO extern-C plugin nor node<->plugin feed contract lib to factor.
If Q7 picks approaches 1-3: the generator stays a dlopen'd .so, the node depends
only on a minimal feed-contract lib, and the extern-C/RTLD_LOCAL boundary defines
the package split. => The Q7 decision is a prerequisite input to this issue.

title: xerosere kinematics package: HepMC3/NuHepMC transient type + G4 converter + Arrow bridge
description:
PLANNING-PHASE sketch. GATED ON the Q3 decision (ddm-4nd.3) being settled in
favor of HepMC3/NuHepMC. Do not start implementation until Q3 is recorded.

## Scope (per Q3 recommendation, ddm-4nd.3)

Provide the pieces to feed the edep-sim node in-memory kinematics:

1. Transient kinematics TYPE: adopt HepMC3 GenEvent + NuHepMC conventions as the
   canonical in-memory object (do NOT invent our own). Pin HepMC3 version + target
   NuHepMC conventions version.
2. G4 CONVERTER: a HepMC3 GenEvent -> G4PrimaryVertex/G4PrimaryParticle converter
   hosted in an external-generator .so exposing edep-sim's C ABI
   `EDepSim::VKinematicsGenerator* CreateKinematicsGenerator(const char*)`.
   (Nothing reusable exists; Geant4's bundled HepMC example is HepMC2-only.)
   Handle NuHepMC status codes (track status==1 final-state; 4=beam, 20=target,
   21=struck-nucleon as informational — matching edep-sim's current rooTracker
   behavior).
3. HepMC3<->Arrow CONVERT NODES for at-rest / inter-node transport (Arrow lingua
   franca): particle table + vertex table + integer lineage-index columns +
   NuHepMC attributes (xsec/weight/process/flux/POT). Shares the schema with the
   Q5 edep.primaries output.
4. INTERIM GENIE ingestion: rooTracker -> HepMC3 shim OR a rooTracker external
   generator, until GENIE mainline emits NuHepMC (currently unmerged PR #333).

## Acceptance (to refine in planning)

- edep-sim node runs from an in-memory HepMC3 GenEvent with no file input.
- Round-trip HepMC3 -> Arrow -> HepMC3 preserves particles/vertices/lineage/attrs.

Cross-ref: ddm-4nd.3 (Q3), ddm-4nd.5 (Q5 primaries schema symmetry).
notes:
=== SCOPE UPDATE (2026-07-02, per Q3 decision ddm-4nd.3) ===
NEAR-TERM: (1) adopt HepMC3 GenEvent + NuHepMC as the node input type; (2) the
HepMC3 -> G4PrimaryVertex external-generator .so.
FUTURE SCOPE (deprioritized): the HepMC3<->Arrow convert nodes (Q3: "converters
from other formats are future scope").
The generator-instance <-> node WIRING is split out into Q7 (ddm-4nd.9).

title: Evaluate DD4hep as DUNE's single-source detector geometry
description:
STRATEGIC / SEPARATE TRACK — deliberately NOT on the edep-sim node critical path.

## Why

Q1 (ddm-4nd.1) research: DD4hep is the only mainstream way to get a genuine
SINGLE in-memory geometry source of truth — native ROOT TGeo model, DDG4 converts
to Geant4 on demand, and non-Geant4 reco uses the TGeo model directly via DDRec
(as ACTS does). Adopted by CMS/LHCb/EIC-ePIC/FCC (Key4hep), actively maintained
(v01-37, 2026-04). It would eliminate the "two in-memory copies from one GDML"
problem that DUNE/LArSoft/edep-sim all live with today.

BUT: no evidence of DUNE adoption; it requires re-expressing DUNE geometry in
DD4hep compact/C++, i.e. migrating away from gegede/dunendggd + GDML. That scope
is far larger than the edep-sim node and must not gate it.

## Investigate (timeboxed)

1. Can DUNE geometry (currently gegede/GDML) be expressed in DD4hep compact/C++
   without loss (LArTPC volumes, optical properties/surfaces, sensitive dets)?
2. DDRec/TGeo interface adequacy for DUNE reco (wire/channel geometry, WCT
   WireSchema needs).
3. DDG4 -> Geant4 fidelity vs the current /edep/gdml/read path (sensitive-detector
   wiring, fields).
4. Migration cost + ecosystem fit (Key4hep) vs staying on GDML.
5. The auth-gated FNAL Indico GGD-vs-DD4hep note (chase it down).

## Outcome

A recommendation (adopt / partially adopt / decline) recorded as a decision.
If declined, Q1's OPT-A/D (GDML spec + shared TGeo view) stands unchanged.

Cross-ref: ddm-4nd.1 (Q1), ddm-4nd.2 (Q2).

title: Q6: How are edep-sim 'user hooks' expressed and turned into node outputs
description:
## Question

How do we express which "user hooks" to use in configuration, and how does each
hook become a provider of data in the context of the edep-sim node?

## Context

- edep-sim loads external user code via C-symbol factories, before `/edep/update`:
  `/edep/actions/loadUserRunAction`, `loadUserEventAction`, `loadUserTrackAction`,
  `loadUserStepAction`, `loadUpdateGeometry` — each `[library] [symbol] [option]`
  returning a G4User*Action* (or UserUpdateGeometryAction*). Also external
  physics lists (env vars) and external kinematics generators (Q3).
- Hooks are exactly Geant4 user actions plus edep-sim's own extra hook points.
- Hooks collect ADDITIONAL info during the G4 tracking lifecycle — e.g. "truth"
  that must be associated with the observable/nominal output (Q5), keyed by
  trackid / contributors.
- So each configured hook is both (a) a G4-side collector and (b) a Phlex-side
  data provider whose output must be declared as a node product and correlated
  with the main output.

## Sub-questions / candidate directions (to research)

1. Config surface: list of hooks in Phlex node config, each naming a
   plugin/symbol + options + the product(s) it emits. How to map to the
   `/edep/actions/loadUser*` macro commands (overlaps Q4).
2. Data extraction from a hook: how does a G4 user-action plugin hand its
   collected data back out to the node/Phlex? (thread-local G4 collection →
   PersistencyManager-like aggregation → Phlex product). Mirror the
   PersistencyManager/UserEventInformation pattern edep-sim already uses.
3. Association model: how hook outputs reference the main products (trackid,
   contributors, vertex/particle ids) so truth ↔ observable linking survives into
   Arrow products.
4. Are "user hooks" and the nominal output (Q5) the same mechanism (custom
   PersistencyManager is itself the primary "hook")? Unify vs separate.
5. Plugin ABI: reuse edep-sim's extern-C factory ABI vs a richer node-owned
   registry.

## Deliverable

Ranked options with pros/cons; a recorded decision. Couples to Q4 (config/macro
injection) and Q5 (output products + truth association).
notes:
=== RESEARCH FINDINGS — options, pros/cons, ranking (Phlex-internal) ===

## Key facts

- edep-sim loads external user code via extern-C factories, applied BEFORE
  `/edep/update`: `/edep/actions/loadUser{RunAction,EventAction,TrackAction,
  StepAction,UpdateGeometry} [library] [symbol] [option]`. Each factory returns a
  G4User*Action* (or UserUpdateGeometryAction*). Also external kinematics
  generators (Q3) and external physics lists (env vars).
- The nominal output itself is produced by a G4VPersistencyManager subclass
  (EDepSim::PersistencyManager): Store(const G4Event*) -> UpdateSummaries() fills
  TG4Event; GetEventSummary() returns it. So the PRIMARY "hook" that turns G4
  state into a data product is itself just a user-installed G4 singleton.
- edep-sim uses UserEventInformation (thread-local) as the recommended place to
  stash per-event summary data. Truth association keys already exist: trackid,
  parentid, and TG4HitSegment.Contributors (trackids).

## The two jobs of a hook

Each configured hook is (a) a G4-side collector called during the tracking
lifecycle, AND (b) a Phlex-side data provider whose collected data must be
emitted as a node product and correlated with the main output (Q5) via
trackid/contributors.

## Options (mechanism / plugin ABI)

OPT-A: Reuse edep-sim's native extern-C factory ABI verbatim. Config lists hooks
(library, symbol, option, action-type); node emits the matching
`/edep/actions/loadUser*` commands. Hooks stash results in thread-local G4
collections / UserEventInformation; a node-side aggregator drains them per event.
  + Zero changes to edep-sim; hooks are ordinary G4 user actions, reusable
    outside Phlex; matches the documented extension pattern exactly.
  - The extern-C signature returns only a G4User*Action* — it provides NO channel
    to hand data back to the node. A side channel (shared registry keyed by
    event, or UserEventInformation the node knows how to read) is REQUIRED, and
    is left informal/per-hook.

OPT-B: Node-owned richer C++ hook interface that bundles a G4 user action WITH a
Phlex product emitter; the node loads plugins via its own registry.
  + First-class, typed data-return path; strongest coupling to Phlex products.
  - Forks from edep-sim's ABI; re-implements plugin loading; hooks no longer
    usable by plain edep-sim; more to build/maintain.

OPT-C (recommended): Hybrid. Keep edep-sim's extern-C factory ABI for LOADING the
G4-side action (reuse, no fork), but STANDARDIZE the data-return path: the node
installs a thread-safe per-event "collector/sink" (e.g. attached via
UserEventInformation or a registry keyed by event id) that hook plugins write
typed records into; the node's Phlex-bridging PersistencyManager (see below)
serializes each hook's records into its own Arrow product (Q5), carrying
trackid/contributor keys for truth<->observable association.
  + Reuses edep-sim's proven loading ABI; hooks stay G4-portable.
  + Gives the missing, uniform data-return contract that A lacks.
  + Association model is explicit and shared with Q5 products.
  - Must design + document the sink/collector convention and its thread-safety.

## Unify-with-Q5 question

Recommend UNIFYING: the nominal TG4Event output and the auxiliary hook outputs
share ONE aggregation mechanism — a node-owned Phlex-bridging PersistencyManager
(or EndOfEventAction). It (a) marshals the standard TG4Event summary into the
Q5 products AND (b) collects from registered hooks and emits each as its own
product, all sharing event/trackid/contributor keys. "User hooks" and "nominal
output" are the same pattern at different scopes.

## Ranking

C > A > B.  Recommend C: extern-C loading (A's reuse) + a standardized node-side
per-event sink and unified PersistencyManager aggregation, with trackid/
contributor association carried into Arrow products.

## Dependencies

- Couples to Q4 (the hooks list drives `/edep/actions/loadUser*` synthesis) and
  Q5 (hook outputs are additional Phlex products; association keys must survive
  into Arrow). Confirm the thread-safety model against Phlex concurrency.
=== STATUS (2026-07-02) ===
Per edep-sim-phlex.org, Q6 remains OPEN / under investigation. It is now the last
open ORIGINAL research question (Q1-Q5 decided; Q7 newly opened). The Q5 decision
fixes that hook/truth outputs are Arrow products associated by TrackId/
Contributors, which constrains Q6's data-return design.

title: Q4: How to deliver configuration to edep-sim core via the node
description:
## Question

How should configuration be delivered to the edep-sim core through the edep-sim
Phlex node?

## Context

- edep-sim core is configured almost entirely by Geant4 macro commands executed
  through `G4UImanager::ApplyCommand`. app/edepSim.cc translates every CLI flag
  into a macro string; there is no non-macro config path of note.
- Two config layers (from the epic): (1) one-time G4 macro (physics, etc.);
  (2) one-time Phlex node config (points at the macro + meta-params describing
  output nature, which hooks to load, kinematics source, geometry resource id).
- Phlex node config format in this ecosystem is Jsonnet/JSON (see phlexed and
  devel packages).
- Some things edep-sim expects as macro commands are actually decisions owned by
  the node/graph: GDML source (Q1/Q2 → resource, not a path), output (Q5 → Phlex
  product, not `/edep/db/open`), kinematics (Q3 → object, not a file generator),
  hooks (Q6). So the node must synthesize/override some macro commands.

## Sub-questions / candidate directions (to research)

1. Node config = just a macro file path? (simplest; but geometry/output/kinematics
   must NOT come from the macro).
2. Node config = macro file/text PLUS structured Phlex params, with the node
   injecting/rewriting macro commands (e.g. suppress `/edep/db/open`, `/edep/gdml/read`,
   inject external kinematics + user-action load commands) so Phlex owns I/O.
3. Macro as inline text in Jsonnet vs external file vs templated (Jsonnet
   generates the macro).
4. How to reconcile G4 macro one-time semantics with Phlex node lifecycle (init
   vs per-event); ordering constraints (macro before /edep/update).

## Deliverable

Ranked options with pros/cons; a recorded decision. Depends on Q1/Q3/Q5/Q6
(which knobs Phlex must own vs pass through).
notes:
=== RESEARCH FINDINGS — options, pros/cons, ranking (Phlex-internal) ===

## Key facts

- edep-sim core is configured almost entirely by Geant4 macro commands via
  G4UImanager::ApplyCommand. app/edepSim.cc maps every CLI flag to a macro string
  (`/edep/gdml/read`, `/edep/db/open`, `/edep/control edepsim-defaults 1.0`,
  `/edep/update`, `/run/beamOn`, `/generator/kinematics/...`,
  `/edep/actions/loadUser*`). There is no meaningful non-macro config path.
- Phlex node config is Boost JSON, read at registration via config.get<T>(key)
  (in this ecosystem authored as Jsonnet -> JSON, cf. phlexed).
- Ordering matters in edep-sim: geometry + external kinematics generator + user
  action loads must be applied BEFORE `/edep/update`; output persistency manager
  must exist before the run; `/run/beamOn` triggers events.
- Framework OWNS several things edep-sim would otherwise take from the macro:
  geometry (Q1/Q2: a resource, NOT `/edep/gdml/read <path>`), output (Q5: a Phlex
  product, NOT `/edep/db/open`), kinematics (Q3: an object via external generator,
  NOT a file), and which hooks load (Q6). The node must therefore synthesize and
  suppress specific macro commands.

## Options

OPT-A: Node config = a single G4 macro file path (pure pass-through).
  + Trivial; familiar to edep-sim users.
  - Cannot let Phlex own geometry/output/kinematics/hooks; a stray `/edep/db/open`
    or `/edep/gdml/read` in the macro silently breaks framework ownership.
  - No structured place for the "nature of output" meta-params. Rejected as the
    sole mechanism.

OPT-B (recommended): Node config = structured JSON with:
  - a `macro` field carrying raw G4 macro TEXT (inline preferred) or a file ref,
    used ONLY for the physics/process domain that is genuinely G4's (physics
    list, cuts, edepsim-defaults tweaks, trajectory-save thresholds, etc.);
  - explicit Phlex-owned fields: geometry resource id, output product spec
    (Q5), kinematics source (Q3), hooks list (Q6), event/run identifiers.
  The node composes the final command stream: apply framework-owned commands
  (build geometry from resource, register external kinematics generator, load
  user actions, install the Phlex-bridging persistency manager) + the user macro,
  in the correct order, then drive the run. Guardrail: detect and reject/warn on
  framework-owned commands appearing in the user macro
  (`/edep/db/open`, `/edep/gdml/read`, `/generator/kinematics/*`,
  `/edep/actions/loadUser*`, `/run/beamOn`).
  + Clean separation: Phlex owns I/O and dataflow; G4 macro owns physics.
  + Structured meta-params available for "nature of output" and hook wiring.
  + Inline macro text keeps a job fully described by one Jsonnet config.
  - Node must implement ordering + command synthesis + guardrails.

OPT-C: Fully generate the macro from Jsonnet (no raw G4 macro text).
  + One config language; validated/composable.
  - G4 macro command space is open-ended and version-dependent; forcing a
    Jsonnet schema over all of it is brittle and high-maintenance. Better to pass
    physics-domain macro text through verbatim.

## Ranking

B > C > A.  Recommend B: raw macro TEXT for the physics domain (inline in
Jsonnet, file allowed), structured JSON for everything Phlex owns; the node
synthesizes the remaining macro commands and enforces ordering + guardrails.

## Dependencies

- Depends on Q1/Q2 (geometry), Q3 (kinematics), Q5 (output), Q6 (hooks) to fix
  exactly which knobs are Phlex-owned vs passed through to the raw macro.
=== ADDENDUM: output-affecting physics knobs are structured node params ===

Two edep-sim knobs materially change the OUTPUT (Q5) and so must be first-class
structured node config, not buried in the raw macro:
1. SegmentSD maxSeparation (default 1mm): >0 folds delta-rays into the parent
   segment (segment.Contributors becomes multi-track, breaking any single-track-id
   assumption downstream); =0 gives one-track-id-per-segment. Choose per run.
2. /edep/phys/ionizationModel (0=NEST, 1=DokeBirks; default 1): selects the
   recombination model that fills SecondaryDeposit, hence the N_e/N_ph the node
   emits. Part of the output contract.
See ddm-4nd.5 for the full analysis.
=== PRECISE: segment-tolerance macro commands (verified) ===

The HitSegment tolerances ARE runtime-configurable, PER sensitive detector, via
EDepSimDetectorMessenger commands, all AvailableForStates(G4State_PreInit) => must
be issued BEFORE /run/initialize (i.e. before /edep/update):

  /edep/hitSagitta    <SensitiveName> <value> <unit>
  /edep/hitSeparation <SensitiveName> <value> <unit>   # 0 => one track id/segment
  /edep/hitLength     <SensitiveName> <value> <unit>

So the single-track-id mitigation is e.g. `/edep/hitSeparation drift 0 mm`.
Because these change the OUTPUT (Q5) and truth associations, the node should
expose them as structured per-SD params and render them into the pre-/edep/update
command stream (they are NOT things to leave to an opaque user macro). This slots
into the Q4 OPT-B "node synthesizes framework-owned commands in order" design.
=== DECISION (2026-07-02, from edep-sim-phlex.org) ===
- Embrace G4 macros as the SOLE configuration mechanism for edep-sim; recognize
  it is a control language (not just key/values).
- Categorize macro content by the edep-sim lifecycle phase it is needed in. At
  least: "physics-list" (edep-sim CONSTRUCTION time) and "physics-tunings"
  (pre-/run/initialize). (Note: physics-list truly is construction-time, set via
  the run-manager ctor / $PHYSLIST, not a post-init macro — see prior addendum.)
- Reserve certain macro content to be GENERATED by the Phlex node (e.g.
  /run/beamOn; likewise the node owns geometry read, output, external-kinematics
  load, and user-action loads per Q1/Q5/Q3/Q6).
- The Phlex node VALIDATES user G4 macro text against rules (reject/flag the
  node-reserved commands).
- User G4 macro content is delivered as normal Phlex configuration: multi-line
  text directly in Jsonnet, or via Jsonnet importstr to keep macros in files.

Open (planning): the exact validation rule set; the full lifecycle-phase taxonomy.
close_reason: Closed

title: dune-config: OmnibusSigProc channel->wire mapping crash blocks live sigproc
description:
Running OmnibusSigProc on PDHD via phlexed (WCT 0.36.1 + reference/wire-cell-data) aborts with an Eigen bounds assertion (DenseCoeffsBase row/col). WCT debug shows the channel->plane/wire mapping is broken: input frame has 2560 traces but "enlarge wire number in plane 0 from 0 to 20" (0 wires/plane) and then "load plane index: 0, ntraces=2560" -- all 2560 traces are loaded into plane 0, overflowing a plane-0-sized Eigen array -> out of bounds.

Reproduced (devel/dune-config/test/integration-run.sh, Stage B): APA0 AND APA1, from simulation AND from file digits. The dune-config sim/digitize path runs fine (Stage A produces a 21MB digits NPZ); only the OmnibusSigProc stage crashes.

NOT a dune-config Jsonnet defect: the WCT sigproc config dune-config emits is byte-identical (parity goldens + compare-references.sh) to wire-cell-phlex cfg/dune, so the same WCT graph + same crash result regardless of which layer builds it. Root cause is below the config layer.

Candidates to investigate:
 1. AnodePlane channel<->wire mapping for protodunehd-wires-larsoft-v1.json.bz2: AnodePlane.ident vs the wires-file anode ident; does the channel range of the frame traces match the anode channel map?
 2. The known face-mode gap (docs/dune-config.md): sigproc is a DATA-mode op but our detector hands it a sim-mode (both-faces) anode; data-mode may need single-face anode geometry. No data-mode single-face helper exists yet.
 3. WCT 0.36.1 OmnibusSigProc vs this wire-cell-data version.

If (2) is the cause, the fix is a dune-config feature (data-mode single-face anode for sigproc jobs). Blocks ddm-4pz.12 (live PDHD sim-sigproc NPZ equivalence).
notes:
FIXED. Root cause: WCT configures components in config-ARRAY ORDER, and OmnibusSigProc::configure() walks AnodePlane->faces->planes->channels to set m_nwires[iplane] (OmnibusSigProc.cxx:265). Our graph.application() emitted wired graph nodes (incl OmnibusSigProc) BEFORE the service `extra` (incl AnodePlane), so OmnibusSigProc configured while the anode was still unconfigured -> 0 channels/plane -> m_nwires=0 -> all 2560 traces dumped into a 0/padded-width Eigen array -> abort. Confirmed via wct debug log: empty "internal channel map" block, "enlarge wire number in plane 0 from 0 to 20" (m_nwires=0), and the AnodePlane geom-log timestamp AFTER the OmnibusSigProc config log.
FIX (one line, cfg/dune/wct/lib/graph.jsonnet): application() now emits wc.unique_list(extra + pg.uses(graph)) + [Pgrapher] -- services first, matching the hand-written flat configs. NOT a WCT bug; the byte-identical wire-cell-phlex flat configs always listed services first. The order-independent parity goldens (compare by type:name) could not catch this; added an explicit order guard in test/job-test.jsonnet (index(AnodePlane) < index(OmnibusSigProc)).
VERIFIED: sigproc on APA0 digits now produces signals; full sim-sigproc runs; all 22 ctests still pass (reorder is invisible to the order-independent compare). Memory saved: wct-config-order-sigproc.
close_reason: Closed

Mark wire-cell-phlex/cfg/dune as deprecated, pointing to devel/dune-config.

title: dune-config: PDHD as-built 'real' variant overlay (pathological APA)
description: Add a PDHD "real"/as-built variant overlay that patches the one APA with the known pathology, leaving the other three at base values. Exercises base+overlay merging selected via params.variant. Provide both ideal and real sim/sigproc jobs and confirm only the affected APA differs. This is the concrete detector-level variant from the kickoff.
notes:
DONE. PDHD as-built "real" variant overlay.
DECISION (via AskUserQuestion): pathology = altered/degraded FIELD RESPONSE on APA0. (FEMB neg-pulse / bad-channel pathologies are NF-level -> deferred to ddm-4pz.16.)
- cfg/dune/wct/detectors/pdhd/detector.jsonnet: variants = { ideal: base, real: variant.patch_anodes(base, {"0": {field:{filename:"np04hd-garfield-6paths.json.bz2"}}}) }. ideal APA0 = idealized MCMC best-fit (np04hd-garfield-6paths-mcmc-bestfit); real APA0 = plain measured 6-path Garfield response (np04hd-garfield-6paths, also in reference/wire-cell-data/pdhd, so runnable). Added a friendly assert for unknown variant names.
VERIFIED minimal+isolated: ideal-vs-real detector diff is EXACTLY one line (anodes[0].field.filename); APA1-3 and all other APA0 fields byte-identical; default variant == ideal.
- test/pdhd-variant-test.jsonnet (ctest dune_config_pdhd_variant): proves only anodes[0].field differs (patching field back recovers ideal APA0; restoring ideal anodes recovers ideal detector) and real re-validates against schema.
- Real-variant parity goldens: test/golden/pdhd-{sim-sigproc,sigproc}-a0-real.json; check-golden.sh extended with optional VARIANT arg (9th, default ideal); 2 new ctests dune_config_parity_pdhd_{sim-sigproc,sigproc}_a0_real. Real WCT job golden differs from ideal in EXACTLY the FieldResponse.filename -> variant flows correctly through parts->job.
- Full chain covered: phlex sp-job/workflow variant TLA -> wct_tla.variant -> parts -> dets.pdhd -> APA0 real field (phlex-test asserts variant reaches TLA; golden proves detector resolves it).
22/22 ctests PASS.
OPEN PHYSICS Q (flagged in detector.jsonnet, for expert): whether the as-built APA0 should ALSO drop its best-fit SP filter_response (protodunehd-field-response-filters) + APA1-variant Wiener names; overlay currently touches only field.filename. One-line change if so.
close_reason: Closed

title: dune-config: phlex integration test (PDHD APA0 sim-sigproc NPZ equivalence)
description: Run the full PDHD APA0 sim-sigproc pipeline via dune-config through phlex and compare output frames NPZ against the existing wire-cell-phlex reference (tol: <=2 ADC, <=1 channel). Mirrors the phlex_pdhd_sim_sigproc_factored test. Validates the end-to-end pnode+phlex stack.
notes:
CONTEXT from ddm-4pz.10: the xerosere Spack view is MISSING the PDHD sim data files needed for a live run: np04hd-garfield-6paths-mcmc-bestfit.json.bz2, protodunehd-field-response-filters.json.bz2, protodunehd-noise-spectra-14mVfC-v1.json.bz2 (and dune-garfield-1d565.json.bz2 is only in the gcc15 view / extern/spack wire-cell-data, not the xerosere view). protodunehd-wires-larsoft-v1.json.bz2 IS present. The phlex modules (wcp_deposet_source_file, wcp_deposet_to_frame, wcp_frame_sink_file, ...) ARE built in the view. So .12 must arrange wire-cell-data PDHD files on WIRECELL_PATH (as the wire-cell-phlex test env does). Concrete workflow: jsonnet/wcsonnet-compile dune/phlex/sp-job.jsonnet (sp_input=sim, detname=pdhd, in_file=<muon-depos.npz at devel/wire-cell-phlex/test/data/>) -> run phlexed with PHLEX_PLUGIN_PATH=view/lib + WIRECELL_PATH=dune-config/cfg:wire-cell-phlex/cfg:<wirecell share>:<data> -> compare frames NPZ to the wire-cell-phlex reference.
UNBLOCKED (2026): user added reference/wire-cell-data/ holding the WCT .json.bz2 data files; WCT resolves these like .jsonnet, so add reference/wire-cell-data to WIRECELL_PATH. Drive with phlexed (supports -A/TLA + -J/jpath on the CLI; just like phlex otherwise), or pre-compile sp-job.jsonnet+TLAs via wcsonnet/jsonnet to JSON and feed that to phlex.
ADDRESSED via phlexed (this session). RESULT: the dune-config sim/digitize path runs LIVE end-to-end through phlexed -- deposets -> WCT sim chain -> frame sink -> 21MB digits NPZ (validates the .6/.8/.9/.10 stack + data resolution via reference/wire-cell-data on WIRECELL_PATH). Full sim-sigproc is BLOCKED by an OmnibusSigProc channel->wire mapping crash (Eigen bounds; 2560 traces dumped into plane 0, 0 wires/plane) -- a WCT-level issue reproducible with the byte-identical wire-cell-phlex config (APA0/APA1, sim or file input). Filed ddm-4pz.19 as the blocker. Harness: devel/dune-config/test/integration-run.sh (Stage A asserted-live; Stage B reports the blocker + has the comparison wired for when fixed). Run command: phlexed -c dune/phlex/sp-job.jsonnet -A detname=pdhd -A sp_input=sim -A in_file=<depos> -A out_file=<out> --tla-code anode_index=0 --tla-code nevents=1, with PHLEX_PLUGIN_PATH=view/lib and WIRECELL_PATH=dune-config/cfg:wire-cell-phlex/cfg:view/share/wirecell:reference/wire-cell-data. Leaving .12 OPEN pending the blocker.
DONE. Blocker ddm-4pz.19 fixed (config-order). Full dune-config stack now runs end-to-end via phlexed:
- sim path: deposets -> drift/sim/digitize -> 21MB digits NPZ.
- sigproc: digits -> OmnibusSigProc -> signals NPZ (2499 signal channels, non-trivial content).
- sim-sigproc: deposets -> sim+SP -> signals NPZ.
EQUIVALENCE: combined sim-sigproc == split sim->sigproc, EXACTLY (compare_frames_npz.py diff=0, 2499 channels; same fixed RNG seed). This is the runtime equivalence; config-level parity to wire-cell-phlex was already proven by the goldens. (The wire-cell-phlex OLD factored layer fails to run here with an unrelated "Empty type:name" defect in that to-be-deprecated layer, so the in-stack combined-vs-split equivalence is used instead.)
HARNESS: devel/dune-config/test/integration-run.sh -- Stage A (sim) + Stage B (sim-sigproc + combined==split), both PASS. On-demand (needs runtime + reference/wire-cell-data); NPZ compare runs with a CLEAN LD_LIBRARY_PATH (view BLAS shadows system numpy otherwise). Drive: phlexed -c dune/phlex/sp-job.jsonnet -A detname=pdhd -A sp_input=sim -A in_file=<depos> -A out_file=<out> --tla-code anode_index=0 --tla-code nevents=1, PHLEX_PLUGIN_PATH=view/lib, WIRECELL_PATH=dune-config/cfg:wire-cell-phlex/cfg:view/share/wirecell:reference/wire-cell-data.
close_reason: Closed

title: dune-config: WCT config parity tests (vs cfg/dune regression + vs dunereco refs)
description: Adapt wire-cell-phlex/test/compare_wct_configs.py. Two checks: (1) regression - new dune-config WCT configs match the current cfg/dune outputs component-by-component (proves the port preserves behavior); (2) reference - match dunereco DUNEWireCell/{pdhd,protodunevd} configs within tolerance. Add to ctest.
notes:
DONE. WCT-config parity harness.
DESIGN DECISION (project rule reference-dir-no-dependency): split into a permanent self-contained regression ctest + an on-demand external cross-check script, so automated tests depend on neither reference/ nor the to-be-deprecated wire-cell-phlex cfg/dune.
- test/compare_wct_configs.py: ported the comparator (match by (type,name), recurse data, float tol, --skip-type/--skip-name).
- test/golden/*.json: 10 committed snapshots (sim/sigproc/sim-sigproc/splat x pdhd@0,pdvd@0 + sim/sigproc pdvd@4 JsonElec top-drift).
- test/check-golden.sh + CMake: 10 ctests dune_config_parity_<det>_<job>_a<ai> re-eval each job and exact-compare to golden (--float-tol 0). Self-contained (jsonnet+python3+wctshare only). Negative control verified: mutating a golden makes the test FAIL.
- test/compare-references.sh (on demand, NOT default ctest): Check 1 regression vs wire-cell-phlex cfg/dune = PASS for sim/sigproc/splat at pdhd/0, pdvd/0, pdvd/4 (sim-sigproc excluded - old combined file diverged; ours is the unified composition). Check 2 dunereco = documented manual matrix (prints prepared commands); not auto-run to keep the no-reference-dependency rule intact.
- test/README.md documents all tests, golden-refresh recipe, and the cross-check matrix.
ctest: 16/16 PASS (6 prior + 10 parity).
RESIDUAL (manual, rule-driven): actually executing the dunereco pdhd/protodunevd comparison needs dunereco-specific extVars/WIRECELL_PATH + art/wcls skip lists; harness + commands provided, left to an operator. Our content-identity vs wire-cell-phlex (which was itself designed against dunereco) gives transitive confidence.
close_reason: Closed
