title: Await Phlex resources API (absent in 0.2.0)
description:
The Phlex 'resource' concept (design docs sec 3.10 Resources; 3.5.2 Observers histogramming example) is NOT implemented in the installed phlex 0.2.0. Verified 2026-06-23: phlex::experimental::module_graph_proxy exposes only make/fold/observe/predicate/transform/unfold -- there is NO 'resource' verb; 'resource' appears in the headers only as resource_usage (CPU/mem telemetry). So m.resource<T>() and handle->make<T>(...) from the design docs cannot be compiled today, and the registration API is still phlex::experimental.

This gate tracks getting a phlex that provides the resources API (expected 0.3.0+). It blocks the channel-map 'resource' delivery work (ddm-3j8.1.13 Phase 1, ddm-3j8.1.14 Phase 2). Sibling of ddm-66h (the 0.3.0 source/driver gate); both are 0.3.0-enablement.

RESOURCE MODEL (from the design docs, for when we implement): a 'resource' is a shared entity with NO provenance (unlike data products). m.resource<Tag>() returns a handle; the handle delivers an object to a node (e.g. h->make<TH1F>(...)), passed as a trailing algorithm arg. The framework SERIALIZES access only to 'limited' (thread-unsafe/GPU/mutable) resources. User resource TYPES need not depend on phlex (sec 3.10.4) -- only the registration code does.
acceptance_criteria: A phlex version with the resources registration API (m.resource<>/handle->make<>) is available in the view; ddm-3j8.1.13/14 can be unblocked.

title: Channel-map file search-path resolution
description:
Resolve a relative channel-map file name (e.g. 'pd2vd/PD2VDTPCChannelMap_v2.txt') to an absolute path via an ordered SEARCH PATH, rather than requiring callers to pass a full path. OnlineOfflineChannels currently takes an absolute filename; this adds the lookup layer above it.

SEARCH ROOTS (in order): (1) any path given explicitly in job config; (2) entries from an env var (DDM_CHANNELMAP_PATH / a DETCHANNELMAPS_SHARE-style var); (3) the vendored install dir as the built-in default = <prefix>/share/dune_daq_codec/channelmaps (installed by ddm-3j8.1.12). An absolute name bypasses the search.

WHERE: a small reusable resolver (likely in dune-daq-codec next to OnlineOfflineChannels, header-only) so both unit tests and the Phlex layer share it; the Phlex resource node (ddm-3j8.1.14) supplies the configured search entries. The built-in default lets a job run with just a relative map name and no extra config.
acceptance_criteria: A relative channel-map name resolves to the vendored install dir by default; job-config and env entries take precedence; absolute paths bypass; missing file gives a clear error listing the roots searched.
notes:
Built-in default root = the install location from ddm-3j8.1.12: ${CMAKE_INSTALL_DATADIR}/dune_daq_codec/channelmaps. Relates ddm-3j8.1.12 (vendored files), ddm-3j8.1.14 (Phlex config supplies extra roots).
PHASE 3 (2026-06-23): feeds the Phase-1 factory's search roots (ddm-3j8.1.13). Phlex-independent and buildable now on top of OnlineOfflineChannels; not gated by the phlex resources API (ddm-999).

title: Channel map as a parse-once, queryable, shared object
description:
Elevate channel-map handling from 'each consumer parses a file' to a single PARSE-ONCE, queryable OBJECT that is PASSED IN to the decode/bridge step (it already takes a ChannelMap by reference; this is about the concrete object + where it lives + lifetime).

WHY (memory): a future Phlex job may decode all ~150 FDHD APAs; if each decoding node parsed its own copy of a map file that itself enumerates all 150 APAs, that is O(GB) duplicated. The map must be parsed ONCE and SHARED (one object queried by all consumers). This also decouples map CONFIGURATION (which file/version) from CONSUMPTION (the decode).

DESIGN:
- A queryable map object: holds the (det,crate,slot,stream,channel)->offline table (see ddm-3j8.1.12 for the file formats/columns) and answers offline(det,crate,slot,stream,channel). Built once from a file by a loader; thereafter read-only and shareable (e.g. via shared_ptr / a Phlex resource, ddm-?).
- HOME (user suggestion): dune-daq-types is a candidate so the lowest layer owns the shared query interface (every layer already depends on it). Caveat: dune-daq-types is currently header-only zero-dep; a file PARSER pulls <fstream> + format-specific logic. RECOMMENDATION: put the abstract ChannelMap query interface (+ a header-only in-memory TableChannelMap holder) in dune-daq-types, and the format-specific FILE LOADERS in the dune-daq-channelmap package (ddm-3j8.1.12, which vendors the .txt). dune-daq-wct's current ChannelMap/IdentityChannelMap then becomes the dune-daq-types interface + a placeholder impl.
- Where the map is APPLIED: today the dune-daq-wct bridge applies it (decode -> dense local channels -> bridge maps to offline). Keep that, OR push into the decode 'node'; either way the object is an injected input, not parsed per-consumer.

Relates ddm-3j8.1.12 (the real map + loaders), ddm-3j8.1.5 (bridge consumes it).
notes:
Bridge wiring done (dune-daq-wct commit cc8050b): OnlineOfflineChannelMap implements the ChannelMap interface by wrapping OnlineOfflineChannels; traces_from() now emits offline ids from a real ChannelMap*.txt. IdentityChannelMap kept as placeholder/test double. ctest 2/2. REMAINING for 3j8.1.12: select correct table per run/detector (operational); channel-arg convention (streamchan 0-63 vs wibframechan 0-255) must be resolved per fragment frame-format at the decode/bridge boundary before using a 13-col cold map with WIBEth data.
PHASE 1 of the channel-map resource plan (2026-06-23). The parse-once queryable OBJECT itself is DONE: dune_daq_codec::OnlineOfflineChannelMap wraps OnlineOfflineChannels (codec commit e556880); it parses once and is queryable. What remains for THIS issue is the SHARED, config-constructed 'resource object' that Phlex will hand to multiple nodes:

PLAN (Phase 1, phlex-INDEPENDENT per design sec 3.10.4):
- New package dune-daq-phlex (depends on dune-daq-codec; phlex linked only by the Phase-2 registration TU, ddm-3j8.1.14).
- A ChannelMapResource holding std::shared_ptr<const OnlineOfflineChannelMap> (parse-once, share-many; the ~150-APA FDHD O(GB) memory rationale), built by a factory from job config (a map path now; search-path via ddm-3j8.1.15).
- Concurrency: immutable after construction -> delivered as const& -> a NON-limited resource (no framework serialization; concurrent reads are safe). Contrast the histogramming example which makes a NEW TH1F per node (serial).
- LAYERING (open): ChannelMapResource has no phlex dep, so it could live in dune-daq-codec; only the Phase-2 registration needs phlex. Decide at implementation.

DECISION (2026-06-23): do NOT implement now. The layer is thin and the Phlex resource API is absent in 0.2.0 (see ddm-999), so design details will likely shift once phlex actually supports resources. Deferred until then.

title: dune-daq-codec: PDS decode (DAPHNE / DAPHNEStream)
description: Decode the PDS (photon detection) readout formats present in BOTH PDHD and PDVD: FragmentType kDAPHNE=3 (self-triggered) and kDAPHNEStream=13 (streaming). Small data volume but must be supported. Structurally DIFFERENT from the TPC bit-array (WIBEth/TDEEth): DAPHNE self-trigger is likely variable-length per-channel waveforms — the candidate 'irregular format' the descriptor ADR (docs/format-descriptor-adr.md) flagged as a possible reason to reconsider DIY vs a grammar for THIS format specifically. First step: read reference/fddetdataformats DAPHNEFrame/DAPHNEStreamFrame to characterize regular-vs-irregular, then decide descriptor vs bespoke decode. Output: dense or per-channel waveform blocks + metadata, consistent with the TPC codec's output style. Pure, zero-dependency, validated against PDS fixtures.

title: Pin codec test fixtures (WIBEth/TDEEth) with independent golden ADCs
description: Extract small, committable fragment fixtures from the PDVD reference file (data/np02vd_raw_run040380_0019_*.hdf5, ~4GB, NOT committable) for the codec tests: e.g. one WIBEth fragment (or a single 7200-byte WIBEth frame) from /TriggerRecord01663.0000/RawData/Detector_Readout_0x00000190_WIBEth, and one TDEEth fragment. Provide EXPECTED (golden) decoded ADC values from an INDEPENDENT source of truth (e.g. a one-off run of DUNE's own fddetdataformats/python tools OUTSIDE our build, or DUNE python) — validating our reimplemented decoder against its own output proves nothing. Commit the small binary fixtures + golden arrays; codec tests (ddm-3j8.1.1) consume them. Blocks the codec.
notes: DONE (WIBEth) (source/dune-daq-codec commit 6c79554). SHORTCUT (answers the DUNE-stack-build concern): no DUNE Spack stack build needed — DUNE's WIBEthFrame.hpp is header-only and compiles standalone against reference/ + local/, so DUNE's own WIBEthFrame::get_adc is the golden oracle directly. Cross-checked our decode vs get_adc over 786k real ADCs (PDHD+PDVD, 0 mismatches). Committed fixtures (test/fixtures/wibeth_fragment.bin = real PDHD frag header+2 frames; wibeth_golden.bin = DUNE golden ADCs) via the dev-only tools/gen_golden_wibeth.cpp (not a CMake target, uses reference); reference-FREE test_golden loads them + asserts decode==golden (ctest 3/3). Memory: dune-golden-via-reference-headers. REMAINING: TDEEth + PDS (DAPHNE/DAPHNEStream) golden fixtures via the same generator pattern -> tracked by 3j8.1.11 (TDEEth/CRT) and 3j8.1.9 (PDS).
close_reason: WIBEth golden validation done: decoder matches DUNE's own get_adc bit-for-bit over 786k real ADCs (no DUNE stack build — its header is header-only). Committed reference-free golden fixtures + test_golden (ctest 3/3). TDEEth/PDS golden tracked by 3j8.1.11/3j8.1.9.

title: Decide: own data-driven format descriptor vs Kaitai (record as ADR)
description:
Record the decision (and rationale) for how dune-daq-codec describes formats. The two poles:
(A) OWN MINIMAL DATA-DRIVEN DESCRIPTOR: a small per-version table (WordType, bits_per_adc, n_channels, n_samples, header field map, invariants) parameterizing ONE generic decode loop (our get_adc_2d analog). Captures single-source-of-truth, version parameterization, invariant validation, and rebuild-free new versions, with ZERO external dependency and full control of the hot path. Leaning: this fits the regular packing + the project's minimalism.
(B) KAITAI STRUCT: declarative .ksy as source of truth + generated parser + 'valid:' checks. More machinery (JVM compiler or committed generated code + runtime lib) and a bulk-ADC perf risk; value is low given how regular our packing is. Keep as a documented fallback if formats become complex/variable.
Decision should also state: build-time codegen vs runtime-interpreted (only a data-driven/interpreted descriptor gives 'new format without rebuild'). Output: an ADR in dune-daq-codec/docs; feeds the codec implementation (ddm-3j8.1.1).
notes: DECISION (2026-06-18, user): DIY — a project-owned data-driven format descriptor, NOT Kaitai. Rationale: the packing is flat/regular (parameterized LE N-bit array); a per-version descriptor (word,bits,n_channels,n_samples,header map,invariants) over one generic decode loop gives single-source-of-truth + version parameterization + declarative invariants + rebuild-free new versions with ZERO external dependency and full control of the hot path. Kaitai's expressiveness isn't needed and its bit-by-bit/per-element parsing is a perf risk in the bulk-ADC path; kept as a documented fallback IF a future detector (DAPHNE/PDS/trigger) has a materially irregular packing (user has no experience there yet but accepts the DIY risk now). ADR to be written into the new source/dune-daq-codec/docs.
close_reason: ADR-001 written to source/dune-daq-codec/docs/format-descriptor-adr.md: DIY data-driven descriptor accepted over Kaitai.

title: Design version-robustness: per-version invariants (fail) + auto-detect (stretch)
description:
Make the decoder robust to silent format drift:
- WHERE version comes from: DAQEthHeader.version (6 bits), WIBEthHeader.version (4 bits), FragmentType (kWIBEth=12), and any HDF5 file/record attributes. Note the user's concern: these may NOT change when the layout silently does -> versions are necessary but not sufficient.
- MIN BAR (must): for a declared version, assert INVARIANTS and FAIL loudly on violation. Candidate invariants: fragment/dataset size == expected for declared geometry (n_channels*n_samples*bits / word-size + headers); static_assert-style size relations; reserved/pad bits zero; channel/crate/slot/stream in range; timestamp monotonic/coarse-sane; ADC values within 2^bits. 
- STRETCH (best): AUTO-DETECT by trial-parse: parse under each candidate version's descriptor and pick the one whose invariants hold (ambiguity -> error). Fundamentally heuristic; document limits.
- Note synergy with a DATA-DRIVEN descriptor (next task): a per-version table of (bits, n_channels, n_samples, header map, invariants) makes both invariant-checking and trial-detection clean, and lets NEW versions be added WITHOUT rebuilding the decode loop.
notes: DONE + tested (source/dune-daq-codec, commit b0e63e0). Version-robustness realized as the data-driven FormatDescriptor + invariant validator (ADR-001), grounded in real-file findings. dune_daq_codec/FormatDescriptor.hpp (header-only, depends on dune-daq-types): FormatDescriptor{fragment_type,name,word_bits,bits_per_adc,n_channels,n_samples,frame_header_bytes,frame_bytes} + self_consistent(); registry + descriptor_for(FragmentType) (WIBEth=7200 confirmed; TDEEth same frame_bytes, geometry provisional). validate(descriptor, total_bytes, header)/require_valid(): INVARIANTS = marker 0x11112222 is the validity gate; frame-count = (total_bytes - sizeof(FragmentHeader)) % frame_bytes == 0 with total_bytes = AUTHORITATIVE HDF5 dataset length (NOT header.size, which is unreliable); declared version+type ADVISORY (recorded not failed). auto_detect() = unique invariant-passing descriptor (ambiguous when formats share frame_bytes -> name disambiguates). docs/version-robustness.md captures the design. Sets up dune-daq-codec build (header-only INTERFACE; find_package(dune_daq_codec)). test_invariants 1/1. Future per-frame checks (reserved/pad zero, ranges, timestamps) deferred to decode (3j8.1.1).
close_reason: Version-robustness designed + implemented as data-driven FormatDescriptor + invariant validator (marker gate; frame-alignment via authoritative dataset length; advisory versions; auto-detect). Doc + ctest 1/1.

title: Evaluate Kaitai Struct for our packing (expressiveness, deps, performance)
description:
Assess Kaitai concretely against OUR packing:
- EXPRESSIVENESS: can a .ksy cleanly express (a) DAQEthHeader/WIBEthHeader bitfields, (b) the flat 14-bit LSB-first LE ADC bit-array, (c) the Fragment/TriggerRecord container? Bit-arrays: yes in principle (repeat of bN); verify cross-64-bit-word LE semantics match get_adc_2d_as_1d.
- DEPENDENCY COST: build-time kaitai-struct-compiler (Scala/JVM) to generate C++ (or commit generated code to avoid the JVM dep); runtime kaitai_struct_cpp_stl_runtime (small C++11 lib, permissive license). Weigh against the project's deliberate dependency minimalism.
- PERFORMANCE: Kaitai reads bit-by-bit and tends to materialize per-element objects/vectors; the ADC bulk path is hot (64x64 ADC/frame, millions/event). Likely much slower than the tight get_adc loop / a SIMD-friendly unpack. This is the main concern.
- VERDICT to record: Kaitai's value is highest for COMPLEX/variable formats + declarative validation; ours is flat+regular, so the bulk-ADC benefit is low and the perf/dep cost is real. Possible niche: headers/container + 'valid:' checks (not perf-critical). Feed the decision task.
close_reason: DIY data-driven descriptor chosen over Kaitai (zero-dep, fits the regular packing, no hot-path perf risk). Findings + ADR recorded; Kaitai declined (kept as fallback for future irregular detectors).

title: Survey formal binary-grammar languages (incl. Kaitai); check reference repos
description:
FINDINGS (initial, refine as needed):
- Candidate that targets C++ decoding from a declarative spec: KAITAI STRUCT (.ksy YAML -> generated C++; supports bit-sized ints b1..b64, bit-endian, enums, instances, switch-on-type, and 'valid:' invariant constraints).
- DFDL (Daffodil): very expressive XML standard but Java-centric, no light C++ runtime -> too heavy.
- Construct (Python), Hachoir, ImHex/010-templates: not for a production C++ decoder.
- Protobuf/FlatBuffers/Cap'n Proto: define THEIR OWN serialization; cannot describe an externally-fixed 14-bit packing -> NOT applicable.
- Reference repos ship NO machine-readable grammar (find over reference/ for .ksy/.fbs/.proto/.capnp = none; only ROOT classes_def.xml + geant4 XML). Canonical DUNE spec = EDMS 2088713 (prose) + the C++ struct/templates.
Deliverable: confirm/extend this survey and conclude which (if any) tool is worth deeper evaluation (-> Kaitai eval task).
close_reason: DIY data-driven descriptor chosen over Kaitai (zero-dep, fits the regular packing, no hot-path perf risk). Findings + ADR recorded; Kaitai declined (kept as fallback for future irregular detectors).

title: Binary-format specification & version-robustness strategy
description:
Decide HOW we describe the DUNE DAQ binary packing and how the decoder defends against silent format drift. Two intertwined questions (raised 2026-06-18):
(1) Is there a formal binary-grammar language (a 'BNF for bytes') we should use, e.g. Kaitai Struct?
(2) DAQ formats change silently (sometimes without bumping embedded version strings). The decoder must at least detect per-declared-version INVARIANTS and FAIL on violation; at best AUTO-DETECT the actual format version. A formalized/data-driven spec could give stronger validation tools and let new formats be accommodated WITHOUT rebuilding.

KEY CONTEXT (from reading reference/): the WIBEth packing is REGULAR and SIMPLE — get_adc_2d_as_1d (fddetdataformats/Utils.hpp) is a ~25-line template over (WordType,NWords,BitsPerADC,ADCSPerChannel,NChannels): LSB-first N-bit fields in little-endian 64-bit words, cross-word spill handled. Headers are plain C++ bitfields. No variable-length/nesting/recursion. Reference repos ship NO machine-readable grammar; canonical spec = EDMS 2088713 (prose) + the C++ struct. This regularity strongly affects the cost/benefit of any external grammar tool. Holds the decision; children break out the investigation.
close_reason: All children complete: survey + Kaitai eval (DIY chosen), ADR-001, and the version-robustness design (FormatDescriptor + invariants). Strategy settled and implemented.

title: Bridge decoded DUNE DAQ frames into the DUNE data model (WCT/Arrow)
description: Turn the dense 2D ADC blocks + metadata from dune-daq-codec into the project's DUNE data model: a WCT IFrame (dense) and/or a wire-cell-arrow wc.frame.dense Arrow table (to_arrow_dense already exists). This is where the decoded DAQ data joins the existing narrow-waist (and can then be persisted via arrow-hdf or fed to a Phlex graph). Keep technology/domain layering: codec stays pure; this bridge depends on wire-cell-arrow (+ WCT). Decide channel-id mapping (DAQ crate/slot/stream -> WCT channel) here or flag it as a separate concern.
notes: DONE (source/dune-daq-wct, commit 7104b48). Bridge: DenseAdc + StreamMeta -> WCT ITraces (offline chid via ChannelMap, tbin 0, charge=ticks) -> dense IFrame (make_frame). = the traces of wc.frame; frame_tags/trace_tags/cmm empty for raw readout; time/tick from CONFIG (DAQ lacks sample period). Joins the narrow waist at IFrame level (existing wire-cell-arrow to_arrow_dense / wire-cell-phlex-arrow handle Arrow). Depends dune-daq-codec + WCT only. ctest 1/1. CHANNEL-MAP CAVEAT: ships IdentityChannelMap PLACEHOLDER (online id, NOT offline); correct offline IDs require ddm-3j8.1.12 (P1, related). All ids route through the ChannelMap interface -> swapping in the real map is localized. Memory: dune-daq-wct-bridge.
close_reason: Bridge implemented + tested: DenseAdc -> WCT IFrame traces (offline chid via ChannelMap), tick/time as config, tags/cmm empty. Joins narrow waist at IFrame level. Offline-ID correctness via the placeholder-pluggable ChannelMap is tracked by ddm-3j8.1.12 (P1).

title: dune-daq-hdf: read DUNE DAQ HDF5 layout -> Fragments (raw HDF5)
description: PURE package: read the DUNE DAQ HDF5 file structure with the raw HDF5 C API (we read the layout ourselves; we do NOT depend on reference/hdf5libs — memory reference-dir-no-dependency) and yield the contained fragments (raw dataset bytes + identity: TriggerRecord/TimeSlice + SourceID) using our own minimal types (ddm-3j8.1.3). Layout reimplemented by reading reference/hdf5libs/...HDF5RawDataFile.* + reference doc ddm-3j8.1.2. Mirrors arrow-hdf's raw-HDF5 approach. No ADC decoding here (that is dune-daq-codec). Unit-test against a small DAQ HDF5 fixture (no DUNE libs).
notes:
DONE + tested (source/dune-daq-hdf, commit 660113b; types follow-up 2f997af). DaqHdf5File: raw HDF5 C API reader (no hdf5libs, no Arrow), uses header-only dune-daq-types. API: records() -> top-level <prefix><n>.<seq> groups; fragments(rid) -> datasets under RawData classified by composite name <Subsystem>_0x<id>_<FragType> into {SourceID, FragmentType, dataset_path, byte_size}; record_header(rid); read_bytes(path) + FragmentView. Traverse+classify by name. ctest 2/2: synthetic file (write+readback: records/fragments/SourceID/type/bytes/marker) + GATED real-file smoke test (read_real) against data/PDVD/np02vd_run039349_0035 (28 records, parses real WIBEth/TDEEth/DAPHNE names, marker valid, frame-aligned). 

KEY FINDING (assumption violation, reliably confirmed across files): FragmentHeader.size FIELD is UNRELIABLE — sometimes a round buffer size (1 MiB, 640 KiB), sometimes SMALLER than the actual stored content (would truncate frames). The HDF5 DATASET byte length is authoritative and consistently frame-aligned (72 + N x frame_bytes). => reader/codec must size payloads from the dataset length, NOT header.size; the frame-count invariant is (dataset_bytes-72)%frame_bytes==0, NOT header.size==dataset_bytes; validity via marker 0x11112222. Documented in dune-daq-codec/docs/daq-hdf5-layout.md; feeds version-robustness invariants (3j8.1.7.3). Installed to /devel/ddm/install (find_package(dune_daq_hdf)).
close_reason: dune-daq-hdf reader implemented (records/fragments/SourceID/FragmentType/bytes), traverse+classify, zero-dep; synthetic + real-PDVD-file tests pass (ctest 2/2). Found+documented that FragmentHeader.size is unreliable -> dataset byte length is authoritative.

title: Define project-owned minimal DAQ types (reimplemented, zero dependency)
description: Both dune-daq-codec and dune-daq-hdf need to interpret DUNE DAQ bytes, but we must NOT depend on the reference clones (memory reference-dir-no-dependency) — not even for tests. So define OUR OWN minimal types/views, reimplemented by reading the reference headers: a FragmentHeader view + payload accessor (over raw dataset bytes), SourceID, the FragmentType enum values we handle, and the DAQEthHeader bitfield struct. Keep them minimal (only fields we use) and shared by the codec + reader (a small project-owned header set). This replaces the earlier (now-wrong) plan to consume reference/daqdataformats + reference/detdataformats headers directly. Verify field offsets/bit layout against the reference and the reference doc (ddm-3j8.1.2).
notes: DONE + tested. Created source/dune-daq-types (commit ce49c0b): header-only, zero-dependency, INTERFACE CMake target dune_daq_types::dune_daq_types, dev-installed to /devel/ddm/install. Types (namespace dune_daq, reimplemented from read-only reference/, link nothing): FragmentHeader (72B; marker 0x11112222, version, size, fragment_type, element_id SourceID; valid_marker()/type()/payload_size()), SourceID (8B) + Subsystem + subsystem_name(), FragmentType enum (0..19 incl kWIBEth=12/kTDEEth=15/kDAPHNE=3/kDAPHNEStream=13/kCRT=14) + to_string/fragment_type_from_string (dataset-name suffixes), DAQEthHeader (16B eth bitfield + timestamp). Byte-exact via compile-time static_asserts on all sizeof/offsets; test_layout decodes crafted buffers with the REAL PDVD values (size 1195272 = 72+166x7200, fragment_type kWIBEth, SourceID) + DAQEthHeader bitfields. ctest 1/1. Consumers (codec 3j8.1.1, PDS 3j8.1.9, reader 3j8.1.4) find_package(dune_daq_types). STRUCTURAL NOTE: chose a separate header-only package (not folding types into dune-daq-codec) so dune-daq-hdf needs only the container types without depending on codec logic.
close_reason: dune-daq-types header-only package created (FragmentHeader/SourceID/FragmentType/DAQEthHeader), byte-exact (static_asserts + real-PDVD-value test), zero-dependency, installed. ctest 1/1.

title: Reference: document DUNE DAQ HDF5 layout + WIBEth/WIB ADC packing
description:
Capture, in-repo, the exact specs we are reimplementing against (we do NOT depend on the reference clones — memory reference-dir-no-dependency). Authoritative READ-ONLY sources now available:
- DUNE DAQ HDF5 layout: reference/hdf5libs/include/hdf5libs/HDF5RawDataFile.hpp + src/HDF5RawDataFile.cpp (TriggerRecord/TimeSlice group org, dataset naming, SourceID placement, attributes).
- WIBEth ADC packing (14-bit): reference/fddetdataformats/include/fddetdataformats/WIBEthFrame.{hpp,hxx} (channel x tick layout, word/bit order, get_adc/set_adc); WIB (12-bit) similarly.
- DAQEthHeader fields: reference/detdataformats/include/detdataformats/DAQEthHeader.hpp.
- Container: reference/daqdataformats (Fragment header layout, FragmentType, SourceID).
Deliver an in-repo doc (e.g. dune-daq-codec/docs or a design note) recording these layouts precisely, plus a named reference fragment + expected ADC values to use as the codec's verification fixture.
notes: DONE. Documentation products written in the new source/dune-daq-codec repo (commit 1908847): docs/wibeth-format.md (WIBEth frame layout + 14-bit LSB-first LE ADC packing + extraction algorithm + invariants), docs/daq-hdf5-layout.md (self-describing versioned DUNE DAQ HDF5 layout for dune-daq-hdf), docs/format-descriptor-adr.md (ADR-001 + descriptor design + version-robustness). Reimplemented from read-only reference/ (fddetdataformats WIBEthFrame/Utils, detdataformats DAQEthHeader, hdf5libs HDF5RawDataFile/HDF5FileLayout, daqdataformats) + EDMS 2088713. PENDING for full closure of the codec work: pin a concrete reference WIBEth fragment + expected ADC values as the decoder test fixture (needs a sample DUNE DAQ HDF5 file).
close_reason: Reference/spec docs delivered in source/dune-daq-codec/docs (WIBEth packing, DAQ HDF5 layout, descriptor ADR). Test fixture (real fragment + expected ADCs) to be pinned during codec implementation.

title: dune-daq-codec: ADC bit-codec (packed <-> dense 2D)
description:
PURE package (no Phlex/WCT/Arrow): decode a DUNE DAQ Fragment payload of packed, unaligned ADC samples into a dense 2D block (int16, channels x ticks) + metadata (channel/crate/slot/stream ids, timestamps); (later) encode the reverse. Uses OUR OWN reimplemented DAQ types (ddm-3j8.1.3), NOT the reference clones (memory reference-dir-no-dependency). The bit-packing is reimplemented by reading reference/fddetdataformats/.../WIBEthFrame.{hpp,hxx} (and reference doc ddm-3j8.1.2) — we do not link fddetdataformats.

Scope: START with WIBEth (kWIBEth=12, 14-bit), DECODE only; add WIB (12-bit) and encode later. Output types are common/Arrow-friendly (e.g. std::vector<int16_t> + a small shape/metadata struct); Arrow/WCT framing is downstream (bridge ddm-3j8.1.5), so this stays pure and unit-testable with a synthetic/reference fragment (no HDF5, no DUNE libs). Validate decoded ADCs against the reference fixture from ddm-3j8.1.2.
notes: DONE + tested (source/dune-daq-codec, commit 37046af). Generic LSB-first bit-array decode loop driven by FormatDescriptor: fragment(N frames) -> DenseAdc int16[n_channels][N*n_samples] (row-major [channel][tick]) + StreamMeta from first DAQEthHeader. decode(descriptor,bytes) + decode(bytes) (auto-select by fragment_type). Validates first (require_valid; frame count from authoritative byte length). dune-daq-codec now a compiled SHARED lib (Decode.cpp); FormatDescriptor header-only. test_decode 2/2 (+invariants): synthetic WIBEth fragment with known ADC pattern (spec-mirroring encoder) round-trips EVERY ADC, plus shape/metadata/auto-select/misalignment-reject. Installed (find_package(dune_daq_codec) -> dune_daq_codec::dune_daq_codec). PENDING/FOLLOW-UPS: golden validation vs real DUNE ADCs = ddm-3j8.1.8; TDEEth/CRT geometry confirmation + encode = new follow-up issue ddm-3j8.1.11. WIBEth DECODE (the core) is complete.
close_reason: Generic WIBEth decode loop implemented + round-trip tested (every ADC, metadata, auto-select, misalignment reject). Golden validation tracked by ddm-3j8.1.8; TDEEth/CRT geometry + encode by ddm-3j8.1.11.

title: Support DUNE DAQ HDF5 format for reading into the DUNE data model.
description:
Read DUNE DAQ HDF5 files into the DUNE data model (Arrow-based), i.e. 'decode' the persistent DAQ format. The DAQ HDF5 layout and payloads differ fundamentally from arrow-hdf's organization:
- HDF5 group organization is DUNE-DAQ-specific (TriggerRecord / TimeSlice -> per-SourceID Fragment datasets), NOT the arrow-hdf (cell/creator/product) layout.
- Fragment payloads are packed, unaligned 12- or 14-bit ADC samples (per-detector frame formats) intermixed with headers — not Arrow.

REFERENCE-ONLY spec sources (read, do NOT depend on — project rule, memory reference-dir-no-dependency): reference/daqdataformats (Fragment/TriggerRecord/TimeSlice/SourceID, FragmentType incl. kWIB=2, kWIBEth=12), reference/detdataformats (DAQEthHeader/DAQHeader/HSIFrame/DetID), reference/fddetdataformats (WIBEthFrame.hpp/.hxx = the WIBEth 14-bit ADC packing), reference/hdf5libs (HDF5RawDataFile = the DAQ HDF5 file layout). We REIMPLEMENT the minimal types + decode/read logic in our own source/ packages with ZERO dependency on these clones (not even for unit tests). Decisions (2026-06-18): reimplement the ADC codec ourselves; read the HDF5 layout ourselves with raw HDF5; split into dune-daq-codec + dune-daq-hdf; start WIBEth, decode/read only.

title: Support for input of files not produced by DUNE Data Model
description: One very important type of file is in the DUNE DAQ HDF5 format.  This does not adhere to the DUNE data model and specifically not the Arrow based one.  Two key differences: it has its own HDF5 group organization and the datasets provide packed, unaligned 12 or 14 bit samples intermixed with other information.  We call 'decoding' the logic required to convert this persistent format into one that matches the DUNE data model.  This epic is to bring support for such external formats and explicitly for the DUNE DAQ HDF5 format.

title: Single-provider read path: end-to-end in-graph read-back
description: Wire the single-provider read path end-to-end in a Phlex graph and test it by reading back a file the write path produced. Found during ddm-c3s.6: a provider only fires when a CONSUMING node demands its product via input_family; an output sink does not. So the read graph must be: generate_layers (known cells) -> phlex_arrow_hdf_source (emits TableGroup, c3s.6 done) -> a CONSUMING read transform TableGroup->wcphlex::Frame (wire-cell-phlex-arrow; register from_table_group/frame_from_table_group as a transform — only write-side to_table_group is registered today) -> a sink/observer (e.g. wcp_frame_observer, or two_frame_observer to compare against a freshly-built frame). NOT blocked by 66h: this uses generate_layers for known cells; the file-driven cell auto-discovery is the separate c3s.16. Memory: phlex-provider-needs-consumer.
acceptance_criteria:
- A Phlex graph reads a write-path HDF5 file via phlex_arrow_hdf_source, reconstructs WCT products through a registered read transform, and a sink/observer confirms the reconstructed product (ident/traces/tags) matches what was written.
- Driven by generate_layers sized to the file (file-driven driver = c3s.16).
notes: DONE + tested (wire-cell-phlex-arrow ce2197c, phlex-arrow-hdf 145c8a7). Read direction wired end-to-end in a Phlex graph: FromArrowModule.cpp registers wire_cell_phlex_arrow_from_arrow (TableGroup->wcphlex::<T> via *_from_table_group). integration_read.{jsonnet,sh}: write integration_out.h5 (full write chain), then read graph generate_layers -> phlex_arrow_hdf_source(reads HDF5->TableGroup) -> wire_cell_phlex_arrow_from_arrow(TableGroup->Frame) -> wcp_frame_observer(asserts non-null). frame_observer uses NDEBUG-undef'd assert, so clean exit = every cell's frame read+reconstructed. ctest 5/5 (phlex-arrow-hdf). KEY WIRING (memory phlex-product-creator-naming): provider needs a CONSUMING transform to fire (memory phlex-provider-needs-consumer); product_query.creator/observer input_from = module INSTANCE name ('from_arrow'), while the file-address creator = writer's algorithm_name.full() = instance:node ('to_arrow:wc_frame_to_arrow'). Content fidelity already covered by unit tests test_input_read (read) + test_convert (reconstruct). Driven by generate_layers sized to the file; file-driven cell discovery = c3s.16 (66h-blocked).
close_reason: End-to-end single-provider read-back works in a Phlex graph (source -> from_arrow transform -> frame_observer); integration_read passes (ctest 5/5). Read-direction convert plugin added.

title: phlex-arrow-hdf: file-driven read driver (source/driver collusion)
description: The read DRIVER that auto-discovers which data-cell indices exist in an input HDF5 file (from arrow-hdf scan() / the c3s.15 hierarchy) and yields exactly those cells so the single-provider reader (c3s.6) supplies a product for each. This is the driver<->source 'collusion' that needs Phlex's new source/driver model. Split out of the original c3s.6 when the single-provider provider was unblocked on 0.2.0. Builds on the single-provider provider (c3s.6) + the scan-to-hierarchy interpretation (c3s.15) + (eventually) the shared open-file/scan service (c3s.10).
acceptance_criteria: - A read job opens an HDF5 file produced by the write path and, with no preconfigured cell count, drives exactly the cells present, with the provider supplying each product; round-trips a multi-cell file end-to-end.
notes:
DESIGN FINDINGS (2026-06-18 investigation of Phlex 0.2.0 driver/source model):

1. NO provider->driver runtime channel exists. The driver is an async_driver<data_cell_index_ptr> (utilities/async_driver.hpp): a generator that calls d.yield(index); framework pulls them into an input_node. Cells are produced via the NEUTRAL cursor abstraction data_cell_cursor::yield_child(layer,number)/yield_job() and fixed_hierarchy (model/fixed_hierarchy.hpp) — none of which touch any file technology. Sources/providers (m.provide(lambda(data_cell_index)->product)) are pure REACTORS; they cannot tell the driver what to iterate, and the driver does not query them. The collusion is one-directional: the driver is the sole authority on the index spine.

2. RECOMMENDED tech-independent architecture (matches the design preference to NOT bind the driver to arrow-hdf scan): keep the technology scan OUTSIDE the driver and reduce it to a NEUTRAL cell hierarchy = our CellHierarchy (ddm-c3s.15, raw components -> (layer,number) tree, no arrow-hdf dep). Feed that neutral hierarchy to a generic hierarchy-driven driver (the fixed_hierarchy shape: the set of layer paths/cells to yield). The driver consumes only (layer,number); arrow-hdf scan() stays inside the technology layer. Driver<->provider agreement is then CONSISTENT BY CONSTRUCTION because both derive from the same file scan — collusion via a SHARED NEUTRAL ARTIFACT, not a runtime channel.

3. BLOCKER: phlex/driver.hpp is NOT present in the installed 0.2.0 (it is #included by framework_graph.hpp/load_module.hpp but the file is absent; driver_proxy/driver_bundle are only forward-referenced; fixed_hierarchy comments say a driver function must be 'registered via driver_proxy::drive()'). So a CUSTOM driver plugin cannot be written against the shipped 0.2.0 headers; the built-in generate_layers only takes static counts. => c3s.16 cannot be implemented until Phlex ships the driver registration API (ddm-66h). A real provider->driver channel, if Phlex chooses to add one, would also land there.

4. ALIGNS with #535 ('discover based on configuration needs', 'generate metadata at build time') and the translator/preserver rework (ddm-4or). When the driver API lands, implement: (a) phlex-arrow-hdf scan -> neutral CellHierarchy; (b) a generic driver fed that hierarchy; (c) provider reads per yielded cell (c3s.6, done). Relates ddm-c3s.15, ddm-c3s.10.
0.3.0 MODEL VERIFIED (2026-07-01, reference/phlex @ v0.3) -- supersedes the 0.2.0 findings above; the driver API DOES ship in 0.3.0 (phlex/driver.hpp).

DRIVER DESIGN:
- A driver plugin is registered with PHLEX_REGISTER_DRIVER(d, config). Its entry gets a phlex::detail::driver_proxy (built by the framework from the sources named in the driver's config -- framework_graph::sources_for) plus configuration, and returns a driver_bundle.
- A driver FUNCTION has signature (data_cell_yielder const&  OR data_cell_cursor const&, SourceT const&...): the first arg emits cells; the remaining args are the CONFIGURED SOURCES, dynamic_cast to their concrete type (driver.hpp as_driver_source). driver_proxy.driver(fixed_hierarchy, fn) builds the bundle (also a driver-builder overload taking a shared_ptr with .hierarchy()/.driver_function(), as the built-in layer_generator does).
- => The driver RECEIVES THE SAME ArrowHdfSource instance registered via add_source. COLLUSION is via that shared live object: driver reads src.hierarchy()/src.cells() (const accessors we add) to yield exactly the file's cells; the source's create_providers() supplies a product per cell from the SAME open file+scan. Consistent by construction, no runtime provider->driver channel needed (matches the earlier recommendation).
- NOTE: source::indices() is non-const while the driver gets 'SourceT const&', so the driver drives from a CONST accessor (hierarchy()/cells()) on the concrete source, not from indices(); indices() remains the framework-facing enumerator.

IMPLEMENTATION (this issue): a PHLEX_REGISTER_DRIVER plugin in phlex-arrow-hdf that takes ArrowHdfSource const& (from ddm-c3s.18) and yields its discovered cells over the scan-derived fixed_hierarchy. Config: job 'driver' section names this driver + the source(s) it consumes; 'sources' section registers the ArrowHdfSource. 

DEPENDS ON ddm-c3s.18 (the ArrowHdfSource class) which must land first.
UNBLOCKED (2026-07-01): ddm-c3s.18 landed. ArrowHdfSource (phlex-arrow-hdf) now exposes const cells() -> std::vector<data_cell_index_ptr> (the file's cells holding the configured product, outermost-first) and hierarchy() -> phlex_arrow::CellHierarchy. The file-driven driver (this issue) can now be written as a PHLEX_REGISTER_DRIVER plugin taking ArrowHdfSource const& and yielding src.cells() over a fixed_hierarchy built from src.hierarchy(). Ready to implement.
IMPLEMENTED but BLOCKED (2026-07-01). DriverModule.cpp (phlex-arrow-hdf) is complete: PHLEX_REGISTER_DRIVER(d,config) builds a fixed_hierarchy from config 'layers' (layer shape only — NO cell counts) and returns d.driver(hierarchy, [](data_cell_yielder, ArrowHdfSource const& src){ yield(job); for (cell : src.cells()) yield(cell); }). uses_sources names the ArrowHdfSource, which the framework passes as the typed source arg -> the driver drives exactly the cells the source discovered (the c3s.16 collusion), with no preconfigured count.

BLOCKED by ddm-c3s.19: phlex/driver.hpp is not installed by phlex v0.3.0, so the plugin cannot compile. The CMake target is guarded (find_file phlex/driver.hpp) and auto-enables once the header ships; integration_read.jsonnet stays on generate_layers meanwhile so the suite is green (6/6). When unblocked: enable the target + add a driven integration test (uses_sources+layers, no counts) as the acceptance check.
DONE (2026-07-01). File-driven read driver implemented and validated end-to-end. phlex_arrow_hdf_driver (DriverModule.cpp): PHLEX_REGISTER_DRIVER builds a fixed_hierarchy from config 'layers' (shape only) and returns d.driver(hierarchy, [](data_cell_yielder, ArrowHdfSource const& src){ yield(job); for (cell : src.cells()) yield(cell); }). Config 'uses_sources' names the ArrowHdfSource; the framework passes that SAME source instance as the typed arg, so the driver drives exactly the cells the source discovered by scanning the file -- the collusion, with NO preconfigured cell count.

Fix for the source-name lookup: SourceModule now registers via add_source under the config label (module_label = the 'sources' section key), so the driver's uses_sources can reference it (was hardcoded, caused 'Unknown source with name: reader').

Worked around the driver.hpp packaging gap (ddm-c3s.19) by vendoring a verbatim v0.3.0 phlex/driver.hpp under phlex-arrow-hdf/vendor/, used ONLY when the installed header is absent (find_file guard -> auto-transitions when phlex ships it).

VALIDATED: integration_read.jsonnet now drives via phlex_arrow_hdf_driver (no counts); integration_read.sh asserts 'driving 2 data cells' so a silent zero-cell pass is caught. Full phlex-arrow-hdf suite 6/6 green; downstream convert test green. Meets acceptance: read job opens the HDF5 file and, with no preconfigured cell count, drives exactly the cells present.
close_reason: Closed

title: Naming for source directory inside package repo.
description: Currently, perhaps from mimicking phlex, each new package places source under a sub-directory named after the package with hyphens converted to underscore.  Are there reasons not to use a generic src/?  If not, make the switch.
notes: RESOLVED (no directory renames; co-located <pkg>/<pkg>/ layout kept per discussion — the dir name is the public #include prefix/API and namespaces the shared install/include, so renaming to src/ is not viable; only sources, which we don't separate, could move). The actionable part = stop installing headers for PURE-PLUGIN packages so nothing builds against these 'final' packages. Categorization (verified via find_package + cross-package #include scan): PUBLIC libs (keep headers+Config) = arrow-hdf, phlex-arrow-common, wire-cell-arrow. PURE PLUGIN (headers now NOT installed) = phlex-arrow-hdf (commit d7f2b7c), wire-cell-phlex-arrow (commit a0da4af; also dropped its Config/EXPORT + unused Config.cmake.in). CORRECTION to the initial categorization: wire-cell-phlex is NOT pure-plugin — it is MIXED: its Data.h (wcphlex::Frame/DepoSet/TensorSet) + lib + Config are PUBLIC and consumed by wire-cell-phlex-arrow (Convert.h includes wire_cell_phlex/Data.h; links wire_cell_phlex::wire_cell_phlex), so its header install was LEFT INTACT. Optional future trim: wire-cell-phlex could exclude its truly-private headers (Executor.h/Boundary*.h/FacadeWireSchema.h/TrivialDepoFramer.h) while keeping Data.h. Impl .so still installed for both plugins (runtime dep); plugins load + integration tests pass.
close_reason: Kept co-located layout (dir name = public include prefix, not renamable to src/). Turned off header (and, for wire-cell-phlex-arrow, Config) installation for the two genuine pure-plugin packages. wire-cell-phlex left intact (mixed: Data.h is public). Plugins load; tests pass.

title: Use .hpp for header files.
description: The new packages, probably mimicking wire-cell-toolkit, all use .h for header files.  It is more proper to use .hpp.  A script to change file names, includes and cmake files should be developed and run
notes: DONE. Renamed all 28 of our package headers .h -> .hpp across all 6 packages; rewrote #include directives (anchored on our 6 unique include prefixes + bare test_helpers.hpp / modules/executor_config.hpp — external WireCell .h includes intentionally left as .h); switched the 4 install(DIRECTORY FILES_MATCHING) patterns to *.hpp; updated consumer-facing docs. Done directly with git mv + targeted verified rebuilds (NOT a throwaway script — a blanket .h rewrite would wrongly hit WCT's quoted .h includes). Commits per repo: arrow-hdf 786561d, phlex-arrow-common bf9ad3b, phlex-arrow-hdf 154ab50, wire-cell-arrow c7e1de6, wire-cell-phlex 050965c, wire-cell-phlex-arrow f9e5456 (+ doc commits). All C++/integration tests pass (arrow-hdf 4/4, phlex-arrow-common 4/4, wire-cell-arrow 18/18, phlex-arrow-hdf 3/3, wire-cell-phlex-arrow 1/1). wire-cell-phlex 24/26: the 2 failures are PRE-EXISTING/environmental (phlex_pdhd_sim_sigproc_factored: missing DUNE jsonnet import 'dune/phlex/job/sim-sigproc.jsonnet', unrelated to headers). Convention recorded: memory ddm-header-extension-hpp.
close_reason: All package headers renamed .h->.hpp with includes + CMake + docs updated across 6 packages; builds + all C++/integration tests pass (the 2 wire-cell-phlex failures are pre-existing missing-DUNE-jsonnet, unrelated).

title: Find how to properly use spdlog from a phlex plugin
description: Apparently a plugin can't find the spdlog logger used by phlex and this potentially leaves log levels set by Phlex to not take effect and log messages potentially missed.
notes: RESOLVED (phlex-arrow-hdf commit 1f72bbf). FINDING: spdlog's registry IS shared correctly binary<->plugins (one shared compiled libspdlog.so.1.16; spdlog::spdlog target propagates SPDLOG_COMPILED_LIB/SPDLOG_SHARED_LIB -> single registry, verified). The problem is NOT spdlog sharing — it is that WireCell Toolkit HIJACKS the global default logger: a WCT-pulling plugin's load makes WCT set_default_logger() to its 'wct' logger with ZERO sinks, silencing all default-logger output process-wide (phlex's startup line prints only because it runs before WCT loads; phlex registers no named logger -> get('phlex') nil). RECIPE: a plugin must NOT use the spdlog default logger; OWN a named logger with an explicit sink, fetched-or-created once (spdlog::get(name) ?: spdlog::stderr_color_mt(name)) and link spdlog::spdlog. (Alternative for WCT-level integration: WireCell::Log::logger(name).) Implemented as the logger() helper in ArrowHdfOutput.cpp; coverage drop notice now a proper spdlog line; integration_drop asserts visibility (ctest 3/3). Full writeup: memory spdlog-from-phlex-plugin.
close_reason: Root cause: WCT hijacks spdlog's global default logger (sink-less 'wct'); spdlog sharing itself is fine. Recipe = own a named logger with explicit sink; applied to phlex-arrow-hdf. Documented in memory spdlog-from-phlex-plugin.

title: phlex-arrow-common: interpret arrow-hdf scan trie as Phlex cell hierarchy
description:
Capture the Phlex cell-hierarchy semantics being REMOVED from arrow-hdf (ddm-c3s.1). arrow-hdf's scan() now returns a GENERIC path trie (arrow_hdf::Hierarchy: HierNode{name, product, children}, children sorted lexicographically) — no cell/creator/product meaning. The Phlex read side must interpret that generic structure into the cell hierarchy. This is read-side work -> aligns with ddm-c3s.3 / ddm-c3s.6 and is DEFERRED until Phlex >= 0.3.0 (per epic constraint).

## Concept removed from arrow-hdf (to re-implement on the Phlex read side)
The old types/logic were:
  struct ProductLoc { std::string creator, product; };
  struct HierCell { std::string layer; int64_t number; std::vector<ProductLoc> products; std::vector<HierCell> children; };
  struct Hierarchy { std::vector<std::string> layer_names; std::vector<ProductLoc> root_products; std::vector<HierCell> cells; static from_addresses(...); };
from_addresses: split each product Address into (layer,number) pairs + trailing creator+product; verify a consistent layer name per depth; NUMERICALLY sort cells by number; attach ProductLoc at the leaf cell (root_products if no cells).

## To do (in the Phlex glue, read-side)
Consume arrow_hdf::Hierarchy (or the flat product paths from scan) and rebuild the Phlex cell tree: parse each path's components as alternating (layer_name, number) pairs followed by creator, product; unescape via arrow_hdf::path_unescape; validate layer-name consistency per depth; numeric-sort by cell number; group products per cell. This restores numeric ordering and the (layer,number)/creator/product semantics that arrow-hdf no longer bakes in.

## References
phlex-file-io-design.md sections 6,7,8. arrow-hdf Hierarchy.h/Hdf5File::scan (post-refactor). Relates-to ddm-c3s.3, ddm-c3s.6. Blocked-by ddm-c3s.1 (done).
notes: DONE + tested (phlex-arrow-common, commit b6bed85). CellHierarchy.{hpp,cpp}: re-implements the cell-hierarchy semantics removed from arrow-hdf, Phlex-side. CellHierarchy::from_addresses(vector<vector<string>> raw components) -> {layer_names[per depth], root_products, cells(tree)} via parse_address; products grouped at leaf cell (root_products if no cells); children NUMERIC-sorted by number; one layer name validated per depth (throws on mismatch); products sorted (creator,product) for determinism. LAYERING: input is neutral raw components; the tech package (phlex-arrow-hdf) supplies them from arrow_hdf scan()+path_unescape, so phlex-arrow-common gains NO arrow-hdf dependency (std+CellAddress only). Types ProductLoc/CellNode/CellHierarchy mirror the old arrow-hdf ProductLoc/HierCell/Hierarchy. test_cell_hierarchy 5/5 (numeric sort, grouping, root product, layer-consistency throw, empty).
close_reason: CellHierarchy::from_addresses re-implements the removed cell-hierarchy interpretation on neutral raw components (no arrow-hdf dep): numeric sort, per-depth layer validation, product grouping, root products. Tested 5/5.

title: Driver/provider shared open-file/scan service [DEFERRED]
description:
DEFERRED until Phlex 0.3.0. Design and implement the shared open-file/scan
service so a technology's driver and its provider(s) operate on the same file and
the same scan.

## Context

On read, the driver (enumerates cells) and the provider(s) (return products per
cell) are separate Phlex plugins but MUST agree on the same input file and the
same scan result: the driver yields cell `(event,12)`; the provider must locate
`event 12` in that same file. If each opens/scans independently they can drift
and waste work. See `phlex-file-io-design.md` (ddm umbrella top dir), section 8.3.

## Why deferred

Blocked by "Await Phlex 0.3.0". The new `source` class (a factory of one or more
providers, accepted by the driver) is the natural owner of this shared state, but
its API and the driver/source collusion mechanism are not yet defined. The design
of this service should follow the 0.3.0 source model.

## Deliverables (against the 0.3.0 API)

1. A technology-side "opened file + scan" object (from `arrow-<tech>`) that is
   created once and shared by the driver and the provider(s) of that technology.
2. A mechanism (likely the new Phlex `source`) to own/share it, established from
   configuration that names the same file.
3. Clear ownership/lifetime and thread-safety contract for the shared object.

## Dependencies

- "Await Phlex 0.3.0" (blocker).
- `arrow-hdf` (the scan + open-file object).
- `phlex-arrow-common` read-side helpers (deferred).

## References

- `phlex-file-io-design.md` sections 8.3, 9.

## Acceptance

- After 0.3.0: driver and provider provably share one scan/open-file object (one
  open, one scan) verified by a test or instrumentation.
notes:
RESOLVED-BY-DESIGN (2026-07-01): the Phlex 0.3.0 phlex::source object IS the natural owner of the shared open-file+scan. ArrowHdfSource (ddm-c3s.18) opens+scans once in its ctor; its create_providers() readers AND the file-driven driver (ddm-c3s.16, which receives the SAME source instance via add_source/sources_for) both operate on that one open file+scan. Ownership/lifetime = the source object held by the graph's node catalog; thread-safety contract = per current single-open-file reader (libhdf5 not thread-safe -> provider max_concurrency=serial in the bundle). Implement as part of ddm-c3s.18; no separate service needed.
REALIZED (2026-07-01, via ddm-c3s.18): ArrowHdfSource owns one ArrowHdfInput whose single open HDF5 handle serves BOTH the scan (ArrowHdfInput::scan -> CellHierarchy, used for indices()/cells()) AND the per-cell reads (create_providers' provider_function). So the source object is the shared open-file/scan owner; the driver (c3s.16) receives the same source instance. Provider bundles are max_concurrency=serial (libhdf5 single-handle). Fully closes the design question; remaining scope here is only multi-provider/tech generalization.
CLOSING (2026-07-01): the shared open-file/scan service is realized. ArrowHdfSource (ddm-c3s.18) owns one ArrowHdfInput whose single HDF5 handle serves both scan and per-cell reads; the driver (ddm-c3s.16) receives the same source instance. Demonstrated end-to-end (integration_read drives the source's discovered cells). Multi-technology generalization remains under ddm-c3s.11.
close_reason: Closed

title: Output coverage policy: product->tech mapping, no silent drops
description:
Define and implement the product -> technology coverage policy so persistence
neither silently drops nor silently double-writes products.

## Context

Each output module receives EVERY store and is expected to ignore products it
does not handle. Without a policy, a product that no module claims vanishes with
no error, and a product claimed by two modules is written twice. The N x M
matrix does not disappear: it becomes a CONFIGURATION of which product goes to
which technology. See `phlex-file-io-design.md` (ddm umbrella top dir),
section 6.

## Deliverables

1. A configuration form (per output module) declaring which products (and
   optionally which layers) that module persists.
2. A job-level coverage check that asserts every product the user requested to
   persist is claimed by at least one module; fail fast (or loudly warn) on
   unclaimed products.
3. Logging of intentional drops (a module ignoring an unsupported type) so
   "ignore" is never silent.
4. Detection/handling of double-claim (same product claimed by multiple modules)
   — error, or explicit opt-in for intentional redundancy.

## Dependencies

- `phlex-arrow-common` (product selection) provides the per-module claim set.

## References

- `phlex-file-io-design.md` section 6 (output module duties) and section 5
  (matrix-as-configuration).

## Acceptance

- A job configured with an unclaimed requested product fails fast with a clear
  message.
- A job with a double-claimed product is reported (and requires explicit opt-in
  to proceed).
notes: DONE + tested. (#1 per-module claim) phlex_arrow::ModuleClaim + ArrowHdfOutput config 'products' = claim list; ArrowHdfOutput::claim() exposes it. (#2 job-level check) analyze_coverage/enforce_coverage (CoveragePolicy.h/.cpp): unclaimed -> fail fast with clear message; unit-tested (test_coverage_policy 4/4 incl enforce throws). (#3 log drops) ArrowHdfOutput logs each unclaimed-but-present Arrow product once via std::clog; regression test integration_drop. (#4 double-claim) analyze_coverage conflicts + allow_redundant opt-in; unit-tested. Commits: phlex-arrow-common e74e871, phlex-arrow-hdf 1f49c52. CAVEAT: the job-level CALL SITE (config validator composing all modules' claims) belongs to the deferred driver path (Phlex>=0.3.0); policy engine+logging+claim() complete now. Memory: output-coverage-policy.

title: Converter round-trip fidelity validation harness
description:
Build a round-trip fidelity validation harness for the transient <-> Arrow
converters (the N axis). This is the main correctness burden of the narrow-waist
design.

## Context

The narrow-waist collapses the matrix only if each native object survives the
round trip native -> Arrow -> native. Subtle data loss hides in the converters,
not in the technology serializers. Known examples already recorded: `ArrowTrace`
must lazily copy `charge()` (cannot zero-copy a `std::vector` accessor);
`IDepo::energy()` is persisted in Arrow because WCT's numpy path drops it;
`SimpleTensor::metadata()` const-accessor footgun. See `phlex-file-io-design.md`
(ddm umbrella top dir), section 4, and the beads memories
`arrow-facade-charge-copy`, `wct-simpletensor-metadata-null-deref`.

## Deliverables

1. A reusable test harness that, for a given native type, asserts deep equality
   after `from_arrow(to_arrow(x)) == x` across the type's accessors.
2. Coverage for each WCT type currently converted in `wire-cell-arrow`
   (ITrace, IFrame sparse + dense, IDepo/IDepoSet, ITensor/ITensorSet).
3. Documented per-type fidelity notes: what is preserved, what is intentionally
   normalized/dropped, and why.
4. Validate `arrow::RecordBatch::ValidateFull()` in every converter test
   (RecordBatch::Make does not validate type equality; mismatches only bite at
   IPC — see memory `arrow-nested-list-struct-builder`).

## References

- `sources/wire-cell-arrow/test/` (existing round-trip tests to extend).
- `phlex-file-io-design.md` section 4.

## Acceptance

- The harness runs in CTest and exercises every existing converter.
- Fidelity notes are written down for each type.
notes: DONE + tested (wire-cell-arrow, branch main, commit 6eb24c2). Harness pre-existed (test_helpers.h generators + deep-equal comparators; test_roundtrip.cpp). This issue STRENGTHENED it to satisfy all 4 deliverables: (#1 harness) validate_full() helpers added; (#2 coverage) test_roundtrip now exercises EVERY converter incl. standalone IDepo+ITensor (previously only via sets): ITrace, IDepo, ITensor, IFrame sparse/empty/single+dense, IDepoSet priors/noprior/empty, ITensorSet+empty; (#4 ValidateFull) every produced RecordBatch/Table (and all 4 FrameTables members) is ValidateFull()'d before the fidelity compare; (#3 notes) docs/fidelity.md documents per-type preserved/normalized/dropped + why, linked from README. ctest 17/17.
close_reason: Round-trip fidelity harness strengthened (ValidateFull + full per-type coverage incl standalone IDepo/ITensor) and per-type fidelity notes written (docs/fidelity.md). ctest 17/17.

title: phlex-arrow-hdf: single-provider read provider
description:
DEFERRED until Phlex 0.3.0. Create the READ side of `phlex-arrow-hdf`: the thin
Phlex provider(s) and driver wiring that read Arrow products from HDF5 by
delegating to `arrow-hdf`'s scan + read, using the generic read-side helpers in
`phlex-arrow-common`.

## Context

Complements the write-side output-module issue. Reading uses a driver
(enumerates cells from the file's structure) plus providers (return products per
cell). See `phlex-file-io-design.md` (ddm umbrella top dir), sections 7, 8.

## Why deferred

Blocked by "Await Phlex 0.3.0". The new `source`/driver model (source = factory
of providers; driver accepts one or more sources) changes exactly this wiring,
and the driver/source collusion mechanism is unsettled.

## Deliverables (against the 0.3.0 API)

1. Wire `arrow-hdf`'s file scan to the generic driver function in
   `phlex-arrow-common` so the driver yields the cells present in the HDF5 file.
2. Register provider(s) that, per cell, map the index to an Address and call
   `arrow-hdf::read`, returning the Arrow product. (A `from_arrow` transform from
   `wire-cell-phlex-arrow` then reconstructs the native type.)
3. Ensure the driver and provider(s) SHARE a single open-file/scan object from
   `arrow-hdf` (see the separate shared-scan-service issue) rather than each
   opening/scanning independently.

## Dependencies

- "Await Phlex 0.3.0" (blocker).
- `arrow-hdf` (read + scan).
- `phlex-arrow-common` read-side helpers (deferred).
- Driver/provider shared-scan service (deferred).

## References

- `phlex-file-io-design.md` sections 7, 8.
- Current Phlex source/driver API for orientation only:
  `sources/phlex/form/form_source.cpp`, `phlex/driver.hpp`,
  `phlex/model/fixed_hierarchy.hpp`. Re-read after 0.3.0.

## Acceptance

- After 0.3.0: an integration test writes an HDF5 file (write side) then reads it
  back through this read side, reconstructing the original products and matching
  the data-cell hierarchy.
notes: DONE + tested (arrow-hdf f972685, phlex-arrow-hdf 5dc5b61). Single-provider read PROVIDER implemented: (1) arrow-hdf read_tables(base)->NamedTables (inverse of write_tables; recovers type label + member tables, TableGroup-agnostic), tested via test_write_tables round-trip. (2) phlex-arrow-hdf ArrowHdfInput: data_cell_index -> index_address(writer-creator,product) -> arrow_hdf::Address -> read_tables -> phlex_arrow::TableGroup; opens file lazily read-only. (3) phlex_arrow_hdf_source plugin (PHLEX_REGISTER_PROVIDERS + m.provide). test_input_read: writes 2 cells at the reader's computed addresses, reads each back, checks type/members/per-cell content/distinctness. ctest 4/4. FINDING (memory phlex-provider-needs-consumer): a bare source->output graph does NOT fire the provider in 0.2.0 (output sink creates no demand); the in-graph read-back needs a CONSUMING read transform -> moved to ddm-c3s.17 (not 66h-blocked).
close_reason: Single-provider read provider (read_tables + ArrowHdfInput + source plugin) implemented and unit round-trip tested (ctest 4/4). In-graph end-to-end read-back needs a consuming read transform -> tracked as c3s.17.

title: wire-cell-phlex-arrow: to_arrow/from_arrow transform nodes
description:
Create `wire-cell-phlex-arrow`: a thin Phlex package providing transform nodes
that convert between WCT product wrappers and Arrow, in both directions, by
calling the existing `wire-cell-arrow` converters.

## Context

This is the N-axis (domain) Phlex glue for Wire-Cell Toolkit. The pure
converters already exist in `wire-cell-arrow`
(`to_arrow(IFrame/IDepo/ITrace/ITensor/...)` and reconstructing facades
`ArrowFrame`/`ArrowDepo`/`ArrowTrace`). This package only wraps them as Phlex
graph nodes. See `phlex-file-io-design.md` (ddm umbrella top dir), section 5.

## Deliverables

1. WRITE-side transform nodes: for each WCT wrapper type (the `wcphlex::Frame`,
   `Depo`, `DepoSet`, `Tensor`, `TensorSet`, ... structs in
   `sources/wire-cell-phlex/wire_cell_phlex/Data.h`), a transform
   `wcphlex::<T> -> arrow::Table` that calls `WireCell::Arrow::to_arrow(...)`.
2. READ-side transform nodes: for each type, a transform
   `arrow::Table -> wcphlex::<T>` that reconstructs the WCT object via the
   Arrow facade (e.g. `ArrowFrame`). NOTE: the read-side transforms do not
   themselves depend on the deferred driver/provider work — they consume an
   Arrow product produced by whatever source supplies it — but they are only
   exercisable end-to-end once the read path exists. They can be written and
   unit-tested now against in-memory Arrow tables.
3. Use the generic convert-node helper from `phlex-arrow-common` to avoid
   boilerplate; this package should be mostly type instantiations.

## Dependencies

- `wire-cell-arrow` (exists).
- `phlex-arrow-common` (write-side: the convert-node helper).

## References

- `phlex-file-io-design.md` section 5.
- `sources/wire-cell-arrow/wire_cell_arrow/Converters.h` (converter signatures).
- `sources/wire-cell-phlex/wire_cell_phlex/Data.h` (wrapper structs).
- Memory: arrow facade behavior (charge copy, companion-table naming) is recorded
  in beads memories `arrow-facade-charge-copy`, `arrow-frame-companion-table-naming`.

## Acceptance

- Builds against Phlex + wire-cell-arrow + phlex-arrow-common.
- Unit tests: a `wcphlex::Frame` -> Arrow transform node produces the expected
  `wc.frame` table; an Arrow `wc.frame` table -> `wcphlex::Frame` transform node
  round-trips (scalars, tags, traces) using the in-memory facade.
notes: DONE + tested (wire-cell-phlex-arrow, branch main, commit c855011). Convert.{h,cpp}: to_table_group(wcphlex::Frame/DepoSet/TensorSet) write-side via WireCell::Arrow::to_arrow_sparse/to_arrow; T_from_table_group read-side via ArrowFrame/ArrowDepoSet/ArrowTensorSet facades. ConvertModule.cpp registers write transforms through phlex_arrow::register_convert (the c3s.2 #3 helper). test_convert: Frame->wc.frame TableGroup (4 members) ->Frame round-trip (ident/time/tick/charges/tags) ctest 1/1. Deps wire-cell-arrow + wire-cell-phlex dev-installed to /devel/ddm/install. Read-side end-to-end exercise still pending the deferred read path (Phlex >=0.3.0).
close_reason: wire-cell-phlex-arrow convert nodes implemented, built, round-trip tested; deps dev-installed.

title: phlex-arrow-common: single-provider read-side helpers
description:
DEFERRED until Phlex 0.3.0. Add the READ-SIDE helpers to `phlex-arrow-common`:
a generic driver function and a generic per-cell provider that are
technology-agnostic.

## Context

Complements the write-side `phlex-arrow-common` issue. On read, Phlex uses a
driver (enumerates the data cells) plus providers (supply products per cell).
The Phlex-aware-but-tech-agnostic parts of those belong here so each thin
`phlex-arrow-<tech>` package stays small. See `phlex-file-io-design.md`
(ddm umbrella top dir), sections 7, 8.

## Why deferred

The Phlex team is replacing the provider/driver model in release 0.3.0 (new
`source` class = factory of providers; driver accepts one or more sources). The
driver/source "collusion" mechanism is not yet settled. Building generic
driver/provider helpers now would target a soon-obsolete API. Blocked by the
"Await Phlex 0.3.0" issue.

## Deliverables (to design against the 0.3.0 API)

1. **Generic driver function**: given a technology-neutral hierarchy descriptor
   (layer names + cell tree, produced by `arrow-<tech>`'s file scan), build the
   `fixed_hierarchy` from the layer names and yield every cell.
2. **Generic per-cell provider**: given a requested cell index, map it to the
   neutral `Address` and call the technology read (`arrow-<tech>`), returning the
   Arrow product to the graph (a `from_arrow` transform then reconstructs the
   native type).
3. Keep the SAME design caution as the write-side issue: no abstractions that
   duplicate Phlex; use first-class providers.

## References

- `phlex-file-io-design.md` sections 7, 8.
- Phlex driver/hierarchy API (CURRENT, pre-0.3.0, for orientation only):
  `sources/phlex/phlex/driver.hpp`, `phlex/model/fixed_hierarchy.hpp`.
- Re-read these once 0.3.0 lands; the API will change.

## Acceptance

- Determined after the 0.3.0 source/driver model is known. At minimum: a generic
  driver replays a synthetic descriptor's cells, and a generic provider resolves
  a cell to an Address and returns the right Arrow table.
notes: DONE + tested (phlex-arrow-common, commit 28d6890). Added index_structured(data_cell_index, creator, product)->StructuredAddress and index_address(...)->vector<string> components: the read-side address builder a single-provider source uses (it receives a data_cell_index and the WRITER's creator from config, not its own source()). Shares the cell-walk with store_structured (refactored to delegate; now the store.index()/store.source() special case). Inverse = existing parse_address. Verified index_address(writer-creator) == store_address for the same cell, and round-trips. Tech read (TableGroup<-HDF5) stays in phlex-arrow-hdf (c3s.6); domain reconstruction = from_table_group (c3s.4, done). No arrow-hdf dependency added (layering preserved).
close_reason: Read-side neutral address helper (index_structured/index_address) added, sharing the cell-walk with the write side; tested (matches write address, round-trips). Tech/domain read pieces belong to c3s.6/c3s.4.
